Genesis1:1 In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
Genesis1:2 And the earth was waste and empty
Genesis1:3 And God said
Genesis1:4 And God saw the light that it was good; and God divided between the light and the darkness.
Genesis1:5 And God called the light Day
Genesis1:6 And God said
Genesis1:7 And God made the expanse
Genesis1:8 And God called the expanse Heavens. And there was evening
Genesis1:9 And God said
Genesis1:10 And God called the dry [land] Earth
Genesis1:11 And God said
Genesis1:12 And the earth brought forth grass
Genesis1:13 And there was evening
Genesis1:14 And God said
Genesis1:15 and let them be for lights in the expanse of the heavens
Genesis1:16 And God made the two great lights
Genesis1:17 And God set them in the expanse of the heavens
Genesis1:18 and to rule during the day and during the night
Genesis1:19 And there was evening
Genesis1:20 And God said
Genesis1:21 And God created the great sea monsters
Genesis1:22 And God blessed them
Genesis1:23 And there was evening
Genesis1:24 And God said
Genesis1:25 And God made the beast of the earth after its kind
Genesis1:26 And God said
Genesis1:27 And God created Man in his image
Genesis1:28 And God blessed them; and God said to them
Genesis1:29 And God said
Genesis1:30 and to every animal of the earth
Genesis1:31 And God saw everything that he had made
Genesis2:1 And the heavens and the earth and all their host were finished.
Genesis2:2 And God had finished on the seventh day his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made.
Genesis2:3 And God blessed the seventh day
Genesis2:4 These are the histories of the heavens and the earth
Genesis2:5 and every shrub of the field before it was in the earth
Genesis2:6 But a mist went up from the earth
Genesis2:7 And Jehovah Elohim formed Man
Genesis2:8 And Jehovah Elohim planted a garden in Eden eastward
Genesis2:9 And out of the ground Jehovah Elohim made every tree grow that is pleasant to the sight
Genesis2:10 And a river went out of Eden
Genesis2:11 The name of the one is Pison: that is it which surrounds the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 And the gold of that land is good; bdellium and the onyx stone are there.
Genesis2:13 And the name of the second river is Gihon: that is it which surrounds the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 And the name of the third river is Hiddekel: that is it which flows forward toward Asshur. And the fourth river
Genesis2:15 And Jehovah Elohim took Man
Genesis2:16 And Jehovah Elohim commanded Man
Genesis2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil
Genesis2:18 And Jehovah Elohim said
Genesis2:19 And out of the ground Jehovah Elohim had formed every animal of the field and all fowl of the heavens
Genesis2:20 And Man gave names to all cattle
Genesis2:21 And Jehovah Elohim caused a deep sleep to fall upon Man; and he slept. And he took one of his ribs and closed up flesh in its stead.
Genesis2:22 And Jehovah Elohim built the rib that he had taken from Man into a woman; and brought her to Man.
Genesis2:23 And Man said
Genesis2:24 Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother
Genesis2:25 And they were both naked

Genesis3:4 And the serpent said to the woman
Genesis3:5 but God knows that in the day ye eat of it
Genesis3:6 And the woman saw that the tree was good for food
Genesis3:7 And the eyes of them both were opened

Genesis4:2 And she further bore his brother Abel. And Abel was a shepherd

Genesis4:4 And Abel
Genesis4:5 and upon Cain

Genesis4:19 And Lemech took two wives: the name of the one was Adah
Genesis4:20 And Adah bore Jabal: he was the father of those who dwell in tents

Genesis4:22 And Zillah

Genesis5:1 This is the book of Adam's generations. In the day that God created man
Genesis5:2 Male and female created he them; and blessed them
Genesis5:3 And Adam lived a hundred and thirty years
Genesis5:4 And the days of Adam after he had begotten Seth were eight hundred years; and he begot sons and daughters.
Genesis5:5 And all the days of Adam that he lived were nine hundred and thirty years; and he died.
Genesis5:6 And Seth lived a hundred and five years
Genesis5:7 And Seth lived after he had begotten Enosh eight hundred and seven years
Genesis5:8 And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years; and he died.
Genesis5:9 And Enosh lived ninety years
Genesis5:10 And Enosh lived after he had begotten Cainan eight hundred and fifteen years
Genesis5:11 And all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years; and he died.
Genesis5:12 And Cainan lived seventy years
Genesis5:13 And Cainan lived after he had begotten Mahalaleel eight hundred and forty years
Genesis5:14 And all the days of Cainan were nine hundred and ten years; and he died.
Genesis5:15 And Mahalaleel lived sixty-five years
Genesis5:16 And Mahalaleel lived after he had begotten Jared eight hundred and thirty years
Genesis5:17 And all the days of Mahalaleel were eight hundred and ninety-five years; and he died.
Genesis5:18 And Jared lived a hundred and sixty-two years
Genesis5:19 And Jared lived after he had begotten Enoch eight hundred years
Genesis5:20 And all the days of Jared were nine hundred and sixty-two years; and he died.
Genesis5:21 And Enoch lived sixty-five years
Genesis5:22 And Enoch walked with God after he had begotten Methushelah three hundred years
Genesis5:23 And all the days of Enoch were three hundred and sixty-five years.
Genesis5:24 And Enoch walked with God; and he was not
Genesis5:25 And Methushelah lived a hundred and eighty-seven years
Genesis5:26 And Methushelah lived after he had begotten Lemech seven hundred and eighty-two years
Genesis5:27 And all the days of Methushelah were nine hundred and sixty-nine years; and he died.
Genesis5:28 And Lemech lived a hundred and eighty-two years
Genesis5:29 And he called his name Noah
Genesis5:30 And Lemech lived after he had begotten Noah five hundred and ninety-five years
Genesis5:31 And all the days of Lemech were seven hundred and seventy-seven years; and he died.
Genesis5:32 And Noah was five hundred years old

Genesis6:2 that the sons of God saw the daughters of men that they were fair
Genesis6:3 And Jehovah said
Genesis6:4 In those days were the giants on the earth
Genesis6:5 And Jehovah saw that the wickedness of Man was great on the earth
Genesis6:6 And Jehovah repented that he had made Man on the earth
Genesis6:7 And Jehovah said
Genesis6:8 But Noah found favour in the eyes of Jehovah.
Genesis6:9 This is the history of Noah. Noah was a just man
Genesis6:10 And Noah begot three sons
Genesis6:11 And the earth was corrupt before God
Genesis6:12 And God looked upon the earth
Genesis6:13 And God said to Noah
Genesis6:14 Make thyself an ark of gopher wood: [with] cells shalt thou make the ark; and pitch it inside and outside with pitch.
Genesis6:15 And thus shalt thou make it: let the length of the ark be three hundred cubits
Genesis6:16 A light shalt thou make to the ark; and to a cubit high shalt thou finish it above. And the door of the ark shalt thou set in its side: [with] a lower
Genesis6:17 For I
Genesis6:18 But with thee will I establish my covenant; and thou shalt go into the ark

Genesis6:20 Of fowl after their kind
Genesis6:21 And take thou of all food that is eaten
Genesis6:22 And Noah did it; according to all that God had commanded him

Genesis7:2 Of all clean beasts thou shalt take to thee by sevens
Genesis7:3 Also of the fowl of the heavens by sevens
Genesis7:4 For in yet seven days I will cause it to rain on the earth forty days and forty nights; and every living being which I have made will I destroy from the ground.
Genesis7:5 And Noah did according to all that Jehovah had commanded him.
Genesis7:6 And Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters was on the earth.
Genesis7:7 And Noah went in
Genesis7:8 Of clean beasts

Genesis7:14 they
Genesis7:15 And they went to Noah

Genesis7:19 And the waters prevailed exceedingly on the earth; and all the high mountains that are under all the heavens were covered.
Genesis7:20 Fifteen cubits upward the waters prevailed; and the mountains were covered.
Genesis7:21 And all flesh that moved on the earth expired
Genesis7:22 everything which had in its nostrils the breath of life
Genesis7:23 And every living being was destroyed that was on the ground
Genesis7:24 And the waters prevailed on the earth a hundred and fifty days.

Genesis8:2 And the fountains of the deep and the windows of heaven were closed

Genesis8:4 And the ark rested in the seventh month

Genesis8:8 And he sent out the dove from him
Genesis8:9 But the dove found no resting-place for the sole of her foot

Exodus12:1 And Jehovah spoke to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt
Exodus12:2 This month shall be unto you the beginning of months: it shall be the first month of the year to you.
Exodus12:3 Speak unto all the assembly of Israel
Exodus12:4 And if the household be too small for a lamb
Exodus12:5 Your lamb shall be without blemish
Exodus12:6 And ye shall keep it until the fourteenth day of this month; and the whole congregation of the assembly of Israel shall kill it between the two evenings.
Exodus12:7 And they shall take of the blood
Exodus12:8 And they shall eat the flesh in that night
Exodus12:9 Ye shall eat none of it raw
Exodus12:10 And ye shall let none of it remain until the morning; and what remaineth of it until the morning ye shall burn with fire.
Exodus12:11 And thus shall ye eat it: your loins shall be girded
Exodus12:12 And I will go through the land of Egypt in that night
Exodus12:13 And the blood shall be for you as a sign on the houses in which ye are; and when I see the blood
Exodus12:14 And this day shall be unto you for a memorial; and ye shall celebrate it [as] a feast to Jehovah; throughout your generations [as] an ordinance for ever shall ye celebrate it.
Exodus12:15 Seven days shall ye eat unleavened bread: on the very first day ye shall put away leaven out of your houses; for whoever eateth leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day that soul shall be cut off from Israel.
Exodus12:16 And on the first day ye shall have a holy convocation
Exodus12:17 And ye shall keep the [feast of] unleavened [bread]; for in this same day have I brought your hosts out of the land of Egypt; and ye shall keep this day in your generations [as] an ordinance for ever.
Exodus12:18 In the first [month]
Exodus12:19 Seven days shall there be no leaven found in your houses; for whoever eateth what is leavened that soul shall be cut off from the assembly of Israel
Exodus12:20 Ye shall eat nothing leavened: in all your dwellings shall ye eat unleavened bread.
Exodus12:21 And Moses called all the elders of Israel
Exodus12:22 And take a bunch of hyssop

Exodus40:4 And thou shalt bring in the table

Exodus40:6 And thou shalt set the altar of burnt-offering before the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:7 And thou shalt set the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:8 And thou shalt fix the court round about
Exodus40:9 And thou shalt take the anointing oil

Exodus40:11 And thou shalt anoint the laver and its stand

Exodus40:13 And thou shalt clothe Aaron with the holy garments
Exodus40:14 And thou shalt bring his sons near
Exodus40:15 And thou shalt anoint them

Exodus40:21 And he brought the ark into the tabernacle
Exodus40:22 And he put the table in the tent of meeting

Exodus40:24 And he put the candlestick in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:25 And he lighted the lamps before Jehovah; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.
Exodus40:26 And he put the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the veil.
Exodus40:27 And he burnt on it fragrant incense; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.
Exodus40:28 And he hung up the curtain of the entrance to the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 And he put the altar of burnt-offering at the entrance to the tabernacle of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:30 And he set the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar

Leviticus1:17 and he shall split it open at its wings

Leviticus2:5 And if thine offering be an oblation [baken] on the pan

Leviticus2:7 And if thine offering be an oblation [prepared] in the cauldron

Leviticus2:12 As to the offering of the first-fruits

Leviticus2:14 And if thou present an oblation of thy first-fruits to Jehovah
Leviticus2:15 And thou shalt put oil on it

Leviticus11:1 And Jehovah spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Leviticus11:2 Speak unto the children of Israel
Leviticus11:3 Whatever hath cloven hoofs
Leviticus11:4 Only these shall ye not eat of those that chew the cud
Leviticus11:5 and the rock-badger
Leviticus11:6 and the hare
Leviticus11:7 and the swine
Leviticus11:8 Of their flesh shall ye not eat
Leviticus11:9 These shall ye eat of all that are in the waters: whatever hath fins and scales in waters
Leviticus11:10 but all that have not fins and scales in seas and in rivers
Leviticus11:11 They shall be even an abomination unto you: of their flesh shall ye not eat
Leviticus11:12 Whatever in the waters hath no fins and scales
Leviticus11:13 And these shall ye have in abomination of the fowls; they shall not be eaten; an abomination shall they be: the eagle
Leviticus11:14 and the falcon
Leviticus11:15 every raven after its kind;
Leviticus11:16 and the female ostrich and the male ostrich
Leviticus11:17 and the owl
Leviticus11:18 and the swan
Leviticus11:19 and the stork; the heron after its kind
Leviticus11:20 Every winged crawling thing that goeth upon all four shall be an abomination unto you.
Leviticus11:21 Yet these shall ye eat of every winged crawling thing that goeth upon all four: those which have legs above their feet with which to leap upon the earth.
Leviticus11:22 These shall ye eat of them: the arbeh after its kind
Leviticus11:23 But every winged crawling thing that hath four feet shall be an abomination unto you.
Leviticus11:24 And by these ye shall make yourselves unclean; whoever toucheth their carcase shall be unclean until the even.
Leviticus11:25 And whoever carrieth [ought] of their carcase shall wash his garments
Leviticus11:26 Every beast that hath cloven hoofs
Leviticus11:27 And whatever goeth on its paws
Leviticus11:28 And he that carrieth their carcase shall wash his garments
Leviticus11:29 And these shall be unclean unto you among the crawling things which crawl on the earth: the mole
Leviticus11:30 and the groaning lizard
Leviticus11:31 These shall be unclean unto you among all that crawl: whoever toucheth them when they are dead
Leviticus11:32 And on whatever any of them when they are dead doth fall
Leviticus11:33 And every earthen vessel into which [any] of them falleth whatever is in it shall be unclean; and ye shall break it.
Leviticus11:34 All food that is eaten on which [such] water hath come shall be unclean; and all drink that is drunk shall be unclean
Leviticus11:35 And everything where upon [any part] of their carcase falleth shall be unclean; oven and hearth shall be broken down: they are unclean
Leviticus11:36 Nevertheless
Leviticus11:37 And if any part of their carcase fall upon any sowing-seed which is to be sown
Leviticus11:38 but if water have been put on the seed
Leviticus11:39 And if any beast which is to you for food die
Leviticus11:40 And he that eateth of its carcase shall wash his garments
Leviticus11:41 And every crawling thing which crawleth on the earth shall be an abomination; it shall not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Whatever goeth on the belly
Leviticus11:43 Ye shall not make yourselves abominable through any crawling thing which crawleth
Leviticus11:44 For I am Jehovah your God; and ye shall hallow yourselves
Leviticus11:45 For I am Jehovah who brought you up out of the land of Egypt
Leviticus11:46 This is the law of cattle
Leviticus11:47 to make a difference between the unclean and the clean

Leviticus12:2 Speak unto the children of Israel
Leviticus12:3 And on the eighth day shall the flesh of his foreskin be circumcised.

Leviticus12:5 And if she bear a female
Leviticus12:6 And when the days of her cleansing are fulfilled
Leviticus12:7 And he shall present it before Jehovah
Leviticus12:8 And if her hand cannot find what is sufficient for a sheep

Leviticus13:7 But if the scab have spread much in the skin
Leviticus13:8 and the priest shall look on him

Leviticus13:10 and the priest shall look on him
Leviticus13:11 it is an old leprosy in the skin of his flesh; and the priest shall pronounce him unclean
Leviticus13:12 But if the leprosy break out much in the skin
Leviticus13:13 and the priest looketh

Leviticus13:16 But if the raw flesh change again
Leviticus13:17 and the priest shall look on him

Leviticus13:19 and there is in the place of the boil a white rising

Leviticus13:21 But if the priest look on it

Leviticus13:28 But if the bright spot have remained in its place

Leviticus13:30 and the priest look on the sore
Leviticus13:31 And if the priest look on the sore of the scall
Leviticus13:32 And when the priest looketh on the sore on the seventh day
Leviticus13:33 he [that hath the sore] shall shave himself; but the scall shall he not shave; and the priest shall shut up [him that hath] the scall seven days a second time.
Leviticus13:34 And the priest shall look on the scall on the seventh day
Leviticus13:35 But if the scall have spread much in the skin after his cleansing
Leviticus13:36 and the priest shall look on him
Leviticus13:37 But if the scall have in his sight remained as it was

Leviticus13:42 And if there be in the bald head

Leviticus13:44 he is a leprous man
Leviticus13:45 And as to the leper in whom the sore is

Leviticus13:47 And if a sore of leprosy is in a garment

Leviticus13:53 But if the priest look

Leviticus13:55 And the priest shall look on the sore after the washing
Leviticus13:56 But if the priest look
Leviticus13:57 And if it appear still in the garment
Leviticus13:58 But the garment
Leviticus13:59 This is the law of the sore of leprosy in a garment of wool or linen

Leviticus15:1 And Jehovah spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Leviticus15:2 Speak unto the children of Israel
Leviticus15:3 And this shall be his uncleanness in his flux: whether his flesh run with his flux
Leviticus15:4 Every bed whereon he lieth that hath the flux shall be unclean; and every object on which he sitteth shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:5 And whoever toucheth his bed shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:6 And he that sitteth on any object whereon he sat that hath the flux shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:7 And he that toucheth the flesh of him that hath the flux shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:8 And if he that hath the flux spit upon him that is clean
Leviticus15:9 And what carriage soever he rideth upon that hath the flux shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:10 And whoever toucheth anything that was under him shall be unclean until the even; and he that carrieth them shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:11 And whomsoever he toucheth who hath the flux and hath not rinsed his hands in water he shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:12 And the vessel of earth that he toucheth who hath the flux shall be broken; and every vessel of wood shall be rinsed in water.
Leviticus15:13 And when he that hath a flux is clean of his flux
Leviticus15:14 And on the eighth day he shall take two turtle-doves
Leviticus15:15 And the priest shall offer them
Leviticus15:16 And if any man's seed of copulation pass from him
Leviticus15:17 And every garment
Leviticus15:18 And a woman with whom a man lieth with seed of copulation they shall bathe in water
Leviticus15:19 And if a woman have a flux
Leviticus15:20 And everything that she lieth upon in her separation shall be unclean; and everything that she sitteth upon shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:21 And whoever toucheth her bed shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:22 And whoever toucheth any object that she sat upon shall wash his garments
Leviticus15:23 And if it be on the bed
Leviticus15:24 And if a man lie with her at all
Leviticus15:25 And if a woman have her flux of blood many days out of the time of her separation
Leviticus15:26 Every bed whereon she lieth all the days of her flux shall be unto her as the bed of her separation; and every object on which she sitteth shall be unclean
Leviticus15:27 And whoever toucheth them shall be unclean
Leviticus15:28 And if she be cleansed of her flux then she shall count seven days
Leviticus15:29 And on the eighth day she shall take two turtle-doves
Leviticus15:30 And the priest shall offer the one as a sin-offering
Leviticus15:31 And ye shall separate the children of Israel from their uncleanness
Leviticus15:32 This is the law for him that hath a flux
Leviticus15:33 and for a woman who is sick in her separation

Leviticus18:2 Speak unto the children of Israel

Leviticus18:4 Mine ordinances shall ye do and my statutes shall ye observe to walk therein: I am Jehovah your God.
Leviticus18:5 And ye shall observe my statutes and my judgments
Leviticus18:6 No one shall approach to any that is his near relation
Leviticus18:7 The nakedness of thy father
Leviticus18:8 The nakedness of thy father's wife shalt thou not uncover: it is thy father's nakedness.
Leviticus18:9 The nakedness of thy sister
Leviticus18:10 The nakedness of thy son's daughter
Leviticus18:11 The nakedness of thy father's wife's daughter
Leviticus18:12 The nakedness of thy father's sister shalt thou not uncover: she is thy father's near relation.
Leviticus18:13 The nakedness of thy mother's sister shalt thou not uncover; for she is thy mother's near relation.
Leviticus18:14 The nakedness of thy father's brother shalt thou not uncover; thou shalt not approach his wife: she is thine aunt.
Leviticus18:15 The nakedness of thy daughter-in-law shalt thou not uncover: she is thy son's wife; thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.
Leviticus18:16 The nakedness of thy brother's wife shalt thou not uncover: it is thy brother's nakedness.
Leviticus18:17 The nakedness of a woman and her daughter shalt thou not uncover; thou shalt not take her son's daughter
Leviticus18:18 And thou shalt not take a wife to her sister
Leviticus18:19 And thou shalt not approach a woman in the separation of her uncleanness to uncover her nakedness.
Leviticus18:20 And thou shalt not lie carnally with thy neighbour's wife
Leviticus18:21 And thou shalt not give of thy seed to let them pass through [the fire] to Molech
Leviticus18:22 And thou shalt not lie with mankind as one lieth with a woman: it is an abomination.
Leviticus18:23 And thou shalt lie with no beast to become unclean therewith; and a woman shall not stand before a beast to lie down with it: it is confusion.
Leviticus18:24 Make not yourselves unclean in any of these things; for in all these have the nations which I am casting out before you made themselves unclean.
Leviticus18:25 And the land hath become unclean; and I visit the iniquity thereof upon it
Leviticus18:26 But *ye* shall observe my statutes and my judgments
Leviticus18:27 (for all these abominations have the men of the land done
Leviticus18:28 that the land vomit you not out
Leviticus18:29 For whoever committeth any of these abominations
Leviticus18:30 And ye shall observe my charge
Leviticus19:1 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Leviticus19:2 Speak unto all the assembly of the children of Israel
Leviticus19:3 Ye shall reverence every man his mother
Leviticus19:4 Ye shall not turn unto idols
Leviticus19:5 And if ye sacrifice a sacrifice of peace-offering to Jehovah
Leviticus19:6 On the day when ye sacrifice it shall it be eaten
Leviticus19:7 And if it be eaten at all on the third day
Leviticus19:8 And he that eateth it shall bear his iniquity; for he hath profaned the hallowed thing of Jehovah; and that soul shall be cut off from among his peoples.
Leviticus19:9 And when ye reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 And thy vineyard shalt thou not glean
Leviticus19:11 Ye shall not steal
Leviticus19:12 And ye shall not swear by my name falsely
Leviticus19:13 Thou shalt not oppress thy neighbour
Leviticus19:14 Thou shalt not revile a deaf person
Leviticus19:15 Ye shall do no unrighteousness in judgment; thou shalt not respect the person of the lowly
Leviticus19:16 Thou shalt not go about as a talebearer among thy people; thou shalt not stand up against the life of thy neighbour: I am Jehovah.
Leviticus19:17 Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thy heart; thou shalt earnestly rebuke thy neighbour
Leviticus19:18 Thou shalt not avenge thyself
Leviticus19:19 My statutes shall ye observe. Thou shalt not let thy cattle gender with another sort; thou shalt not sow thy field with seed of two sorts; and a garment woven of two materials shall not come upon thee.
Leviticus19:20 And if a man lie with a woman for copulation
Leviticus19:21 And he shall bring his trespass-offering to Jehovah
Leviticus19:22 And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the trespass-offering before Jehovah for his sin which he hath done; and the sin which he hath done shall be forgiven him.
Leviticus19:23 And when ye come into the land and plant all manner of trees for food
Leviticus19:24 and in the fourth year all the fruit thereof shall be holy for praise to Jehovah;
Leviticus19:25 and in the fifth year shall ye eat the fruit thereof
Leviticus19:26 Ye shall eat nothing with the blood. Ye shall not practise enchantment
Leviticus19:27 Ye shall not shave the corners of your head round
Leviticus19:28 And cuttings for a dead person shall ye not make in your flesh
Leviticus19:29 Do not profane thy daughter
Leviticus19:30 My sabbaths shall ye keep
Leviticus19:31 Turn not unto necromancers and unto soothsayers; seek not after them to make yourselves unclean: I am Jehovah your God.
Leviticus19:32 Before the hoary head thou shalt rise up
Leviticus19:33 And if a stranger sojourn with thee in your land
Leviticus19:34 As one born among you shall the stranger who sojourneth with you be unto you; and thou shalt love him as thyself; for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt: I am Jehovah your God.
Leviticus19:35 Ye shall do no unrighteousness in judgment
Leviticus19:36 just balances
Leviticus19:37 And ye shall observe all my statutes

Leviticus23:19 And ye shall sacrifice one buck of the goats for a sin-offering
Leviticus23:20 And the priest shall wave them with the bread of the first-fruits as a wave-offering before Jehovah
Leviticus23:21 And ye shall make proclamation on that same day a holy convocation shall it be unto you: no manner of servile work shall ye do: [it is] an everlasting statute in all your dwellings throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 And when ye reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:24 Speak unto the children of Israel
Leviticus23:25 No manner of servile work shall ye do; and ye shall present an offering by fire to Jehovah.
Leviticus23:26 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:27 Also on the tenth of this seventh month is the day of the atonement: a holy convocation shall it be unto you; and ye shall afflict your souls
Leviticus23:28 And ye shall do no manner of work on that same day; for it is a day of atonement
Leviticus23:29 For every soul that is not afflicted on that same day
Leviticus23:30 And every soul that doeth any manner of work on that same day
Leviticus23:31 No manner of work shall ye do: [it is] an everlasting statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Leviticus23:32 A sabbath of rest shall it be unto you; and ye shall afflict your souls. On the ninth of the month at even
Leviticus23:33 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:34 Speak unto the children of Israel
Leviticus23:35 On the first day there shall be a holy convocation: no manner of servile work shall ye do.
Leviticus23:36 Seven days ye shall present an offering by fire to Jehovah; on the eighth day shall be a holy convocation unto you; and ye shall present an offering by fire to Jehovah: it is a solemn assembly; no manner of servile work shall ye do.
Leviticus23:37 These are the set feasts of Jehovah
Leviticus23:38 besides the sabbaths of Jehovah
Leviticus23:39 But on the fifteenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 And ye shall take on the first day the fruit of beautiful trees
Leviticus23:41 And ye shall celebrate it as a feast to Jehovah seven days in the year: [it is] an everlasting statute throughout your generations; in the seventh month shall ye celebrate it.
Leviticus23:42 In booths shall ye dwell seven days; all born in Israel shall dwell in booths;
Leviticus23:43 that your generations may know that I caused the children of Israel to dwell in booths
Leviticus23:44 And Moses declared the set feasts of Jehovah to the children of Israel.
Leviticus24:1 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:2 Command the children of Israel that they bring unto thee pure beaten olive oil for the light
Leviticus24:3 Outside the veil of the testimony
Leviticus24:4 Upon the pure candlestick shall he arrange the lamps before Jehovah continually.
Leviticus24:5 And thou shalt take fine wheaten flour
Leviticus24:6 And thou shalt set them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 And thou shalt put pure frankincense upon each row; and it shall be a bread of remembrance
Leviticus24:8 Every sabbath day he shall arrange it before Jehovah continually
Leviticus24:9 And it shall be Aaron's and his sons'; and they shall eat it in a holy place; for it is most holy unto him of Jehovah's offerings by fire: [it is] an everlasting statute.
Leviticus24:10 And the son of an Israelitish woman but withal the son of an Egyptian
Leviticus24:11 and the Israelitish woman's son blasphemed the Name
Leviticus24:12 And they confined him
Leviticus24:13 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:14 Lead the reviler outside the camp; and all that heard [him] shall lay their hands upon his head
Leviticus24:15 And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel
Leviticus24:16 And he that blasphemeth the name of Jehovah shall certainly be put to death; all the assembly shall certainly stone him; as well the stranger as he that is home-born
Leviticus24:17 And if any one smiteth any man mortally
Leviticus24:18 And he that smiteth a beast mortally shall make it good
Leviticus24:19 And if a man cause a blemish in his neighbour
Leviticus24:20 breach for breach
Leviticus24:21 He that smiteth a beast [mortally] shall make it good; and he that smiteth a man [mortally] shall be put to death.
Leviticus24:22 Ye shall have one law: as the stranger
Leviticus24:23 And Moses spoke to the children of Israel; and they led the reviler outside the camp and stoned him with stones. And the children of Israel did as Jehovah had commanded Moses.
Leviticus25:1 And Jehovah spoke to Moses in mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 Speak unto the children of Israel and say unto them
Leviticus25:3 Six years shalt thou sow thy field
Leviticus25:4 but in the seventh year shall be a sabbath of rest for the land
Leviticus25:5 That which springeth up from the scattered seed of thy harvest thou shalt not reap
Leviticus25:6 And the sabbath of the land shall be for food for you
Leviticus25:7 and for the beasts that are in thy land: all the produce thereof shall be for food.
Leviticus25:8 And thou shalt count seven sabbaths of years
Leviticus25:9 Then shalt thou cause the loud sound of the trumpet to go forth in the seventh month
Leviticus25:10 And ye shall hallow the year of the fiftieth year
Leviticus25:11 a year of jubilee shall that fiftieth year be unto you: ye shall not sow
Leviticus25:12 For it is the jubilee; it shall be holy unto you; out of the field shall ye eat its produce.
Leviticus25:13 In this year of the jubilee ye shall return every man unto his possession.
Leviticus25:14 And if ye sell ought unto your neighbour
Leviticus25:15 According to the number of years since the jubilee
Leviticus25:16 According to the greater number of the years
Leviticus25:17 And ye shall not oppress one another; but thou shalt fear thy God; for I am Jehovah your God.
Leviticus25:18 And ye shall do my statutes
Leviticus25:19 And the land shall yield its fruit
Leviticus25:20 And if ye say
Leviticus25:21 then I will command my blessing upon you in the sixth year
Leviticus25:22 and ye shall sow in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 And the land shall not be sold for ever; for the land is mine; for ye are strangers and sojourners with me.
Leviticus25:24 And in all the land of your possession ye shall grant a redemption for the land.
Leviticus25:25 If thy brother grow poor
Leviticus25:26 And if the man have no one having right of redemption
Leviticus25:27 then shall he reckon the years since the sale thereof
Leviticus25:28 And if his hand have not found what sufficeth for him to restore it to him
Leviticus25:29 And if any one sell a dwelling-house in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 But if it be not redeemed until a whole year is complete
Leviticus25:31 But the houses in villages that have no wall round about them shall be reckoned as the fields of the country: they may be redeemed
Leviticus25:32 But as to the cities of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 And if any one redeem from one of the Levites
Leviticus25:34 And the field of the suburbs of their cities shall not be sold; for it is their perpetual possession.
Leviticus25:35 And if thy brother grow poor
Leviticus25:36 Thou shalt take no usury nor increase of him; and thou shalt fear thy God; that thy brother may live beside thee.
Leviticus25:37 Thy money shalt thou not give him upon usury
Leviticus25:38 I am Jehovah your God
Leviticus25:39 And if thy brother grow poor beside thee
Leviticus25:40 as a hired servant
Leviticus25:41 Then shall he depart from thee
Leviticus25:42 For they are my bondmen
Leviticus25:43 Thou shalt not rule over him with rigour; and thou shalt fear thy God.
Leviticus25:44 And as for thy bondman and thy handmaid whom thou shalt have of the nations that are round about you
Leviticus25:45 Moreover of the children of them that dwell as sojourners with you
Leviticus25:46 And ye shall leave them as an inheritance to your children after you
Leviticus25:47 And if a stranger or sojourner become wealthy beside thee
Leviticus25:48 after that he is sold there shall be right of redemption for him; one of his brethren may redeem him.
Leviticus25:49 Either his uncle or his uncle's son may redeem him
Leviticus25:50 And he shall reckon with him that bought him from the year that he was sold to him unto the year of jubilee; and the price of his sale shall be according to the number of the years
Leviticus25:51 If there are yet many years
Leviticus25:52 and if there remain but few years unto the year of jubilee
Leviticus25:53 As a hired servant shall he be with him year by year; [his master] shall not rule with rigour over him before thine eyes.
Leviticus25:54 And if he be not redeemed in this manner
Leviticus25:55 For the children of Israel are servants unto me; they are my servants whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt: I am Jehovah your God.
Leviticus26:1 Ye shall make yourselves no idols
Leviticus26:2 Ye shall observe my sabbaths
Leviticus26:3 If ye walk in my statutes
Leviticus26:4 then I will give your rain in the season thereof
Leviticus26:5 and your threshing shall reach unto the vintage
Leviticus26:6 And I will give peace in the land
Leviticus26:7 And ye shall chase your enemies
Leviticus26:8 and five of you shall chase a hundred
Leviticus26:9 And I will turn my face towards you and make you fruitful
Leviticus26:10 And ye shall eat old store
Leviticus26:11 And I will set my habitation among you; and my soul shall not abhor you;
Leviticus26:12 and I will walk among you
Leviticus26:13 I am Jehovah your God
Leviticus26:14 But if ye hearken not unto me
Leviticus26:15 and if ye shall despise my statutes
Leviticus26:16 I also will do this unto you I will even appoint over you terror
Leviticus26:17 And I will set my face against you
Leviticus26:18 And if for this ye hearken not unto me
Leviticus26:19 and I will break the arrogance of your power; and I will make your heaven as iron
Leviticus26:20 and your strength shall be spent in vain
Leviticus26:21 And if ye walk contrary unto me
Leviticus26:22 And I will send the beasts of the field among you
Leviticus26:23 And if ye will not be disciplined by me through these
Leviticus26:24 then will I also walk contrary unto you
Leviticus26:25 And I will bring a sword upon you that avengeth with the vengeance of the covenant
Leviticus26:26 When I break the staff of your bread
Leviticus26:27 And if for this ye hearken not to me
Leviticus26:28 then I will walk contrary unto you also in fury; and I
Leviticus26:29 And ye shall eat the flesh of your sons
Leviticus26:30 And I will lay waste your high places
Leviticus26:31 And I will lay waste your cities and desolate your sanctuaries; and I will not smell your sweet odours.
Leviticus26:32 And I will bring the land into desolation; that your enemies who dwell there in may be astonished at it.
Leviticus26:33 And I will scatter you among the nations
Leviticus26:34 Then shall the land enjoy its sabbaths all the days of the desolation
Leviticus26:35 All the days of the desolation it shall rest
Leviticus26:36 And as to those that remain of you I will send faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies
Leviticus26:37 and they shall stumble one over another
Leviticus26:38 And ye shall perish among the nations
Leviticus26:39 And they that remain of you shall waste away through their iniquity in your enemies' lands; and also through the iniquities of their fathers shall they waste away with them.
Leviticus26:40 And they shall confess their iniquity
Leviticus26:41 so that I also walked contrary unto them
Leviticus26:42 I will remember my covenant with Jacob
Leviticus26:43 For the land shall be left by them
Leviticus26:44 And yet for all that
Leviticus26:45 But I will remember toward them the covenant with their ancestors whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt before the eyes of the nations
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes and ordinances and laws which Jehovah made between him and the children of Israel in mount Sinai
Leviticus27:1 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Leviticus27:2 Speak unto the children of Israel and say unto them
Leviticus27:3 And thy valuation shall be of the male from twenty years old even unto sixty years old: thy valuation shall be fifty shekels of silver
Leviticus27:4 and if it be of a female
Leviticus27:5 And if it be from five years old even unto twenty years old
Leviticus27:6 And if it be from a month old even unto five years old
Leviticus27:7 And if it be from sixty years old and above
Leviticus27:8 And if he be poorer than thy valuation
Leviticus27:9 And if it be a beast whereof men bring an offering unto Jehovah
Leviticus27:10 They shall not alter it nor change it
Leviticus27:11 And if it be any unclean beast
Leviticus27:12 and the priest shall value it
Leviticus27:13 And if they will in any wise redeem it
Leviticus27:14 And when any one halloweth his house
Leviticus27:15 And if he that halloweth it will redeem his house
Leviticus27:16 And if a man hallow to Jehovah [part] of a field of his possession
Leviticus27:17 If he hallow his field from the year of jubilee
Leviticus27:18 but if he hallow his field after the jubilee
Leviticus27:19 And if he that hallowed the field will in any wise redeem it
Leviticus27:20 but if he do not redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 and the field
Leviticus27:22 And if he hallow to Jehovah a field that he hath bought
Leviticus27:23 the priest shall reckon unto him the amount of thy valuation
Leviticus27:24 In the year of the jubilee the field shall return unto him of whom it was bought to him to whom the land belonged.
Leviticus27:25 And all thy valuation shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs shall be the shekel.
Leviticus27:26 Only the firstling which is offered as firstling to Jehovah among the cattle
Leviticus27:27 But if it be of an unclean beast
Leviticus27:28 Notwithstanding
Leviticus27:29 Nothing devoted
Leviticus27:30 And as to every tithe of the land
Leviticus27:31 And if any one will at all redeem of his tithes
Leviticus27:32 And as to every tithe of the herd
Leviticus27:33 He shall not search whether it be good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments which Jehovah commanded Moses for the children of Israel upon mount Sinai.

Numbers1:38 Of the sons of Dan: their generations

Numbers2:1 And Jehovah spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers2:2 The children of Israel shall encamp every one by his standard
Numbers2:3 And [for] those encamping eastward toward the sun-rising [there shall be] the standard of the camp of Judah according to their hosts; and the prince of the sons of Judah shall be Nahshon the son of Amminadab;
Numbers2:4 and his host
Numbers2:5 And those that encamp next unto him shall be the tribe of Issachar; and the prince of the sons of Issachar shall be Nethaneel the son of Zuar;
Numbers2:6 and his host
Numbers2:7 [With them shall be] the tribe of Zebulun; and the prince of the sons of Zebulun shall be Eliab the son of Helon;
Numbers2:8 and his host
Numbers2:9 All that were numbered of the camp of Judah were a hundred and eighty-six thousand four hundred
Numbers2:10 The standard of the camp of Reuben shall be southward according to their hosts; and the prince of the sons of Reuben shall be Elizur the son of Shedeur;
Numbers2:11 and his host
Numbers2:12 And those that encamp by him shall be the tribe of Simeon; and the prince of the sons of Simeon shall be Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai;
Numbers2:13 and his host
Numbers2:14 And [with them shall be] the tribe of Gad; and the prince of the sons of Gad shall be Eliasaph the son of Reuel;
Numbers2:15 and his host
Numbers2:16 All that were numbered of the camp of Reuben were a hundred and fifty-one thousand four hundred and fifty
Numbers2:17 And the tent of meeting shall set forth
Numbers2:18 The standard of the camp of Ephraim according to their hosts shall be westward; and the prince of the sons of Ephraim shall be Elishama the son of Ammihud;
Numbers2:19 and his host
Numbers2:20 And by him shall be the tribe of Manasseh; and the prince of the sons of Manasseh shall be Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur;
Numbers2:21 and his host
Numbers2:22 And [with them shall be] the tribe of Benjamin; and the prince of the sons of Benjamin shall be Abidan the son of Gideoni;
Numbers2:23 and his host
Numbers2:24 All that were numbered of the camp of Ephraim were a hundred and eight thousand one hundred
Numbers2:25 The standard of the camp of Dan shall be northward according to their hosts; and the prince of the sons of Dan shall be Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai;
Numbers2:26 and his host
Numbers2:27 And those that encamp by him shall be the tribe of Asher; and the prince of the sons of Asher shall be Pagiel the son of Ocran;
Numbers2:28 and his host
Numbers2:29 And [with them shall be] the tribe of Naphtali; and the prince of the sons of Naphtali shall be Ahira the son of Enan;
Numbers2:30 and his host
Numbers2:31 All that were numbered of the camp of Dan were a hundred and fifty-seven thousand six hundred. They shall set forth last according to their standards.
Numbers2:32 These are those that were numbered of the children of Israel
Numbers2:33 But the Levites were not numbered among the children of Israel; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.
Numbers2:34 And the children of Israel did according to all that Jehovah had commanded Moses: so they encamped according to their standards

Numbers3:4 And Nadab and Abihu died before Jehovah when they offered strange fire before Jehovah in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:5 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Numbers3:6 Bring the tribe of Levi near
Numbers3:7 and they shall keep his charge
Numbers3:8 And they shall keep all the utensils of the tent of meeting
Numbers3:9 And thou shalt give the Levites to Aaron and to his sons: they are wholly given to him out of the children of Israel.
Numbers3:10 And Aaron and his sons shalt thou appoint that they may attend to their priest's office; and the stranger that cometh near shall be put to death.
Numbers3:11 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Numbers3:12 And I
Numbers3:13 for every firstborn is mine. On the day that I smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 And Jehovah spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:15 Number the sons of Levi according to their fathers' houses
Numbers3:16 And Moses numbered them
Numbers3:17 And these are the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon

Numbers3:20 And the sons of Merari according to their families: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their fathers' houses.

Numbers3:22 Those that were numbered of them
Numbers3:23 The families of the Gershonites encamped behind the tabernacle westward.
Numbers3:24 And the prince of the father's house of the Gershonites was Eliasaph the son of Lael.

Numbers3:32 And the prince of princes of the Levites was Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest: he had the oversight of them that kept the charge of the sanctuary.

Numbers3:34 And those that were numbered of them

Numbers3:36 And the charge of the sons of Merari consisted in the oversight of the boards of the tabernacle

Numbers3:45 Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel

Numbers3:47 thou shalt take five shekels apiece by the poll
Numbers3:48 and thou shalt give the money unto Aaron and unto his sons for those in excess among them who are to be ransomed.

Numbers3:50 of the firstborn of the children of Israel he took the money
Numbers3:51 And Moses gave the money of them that were ransomed to Aaron and to his sons

Numbers4:2 Take the sum of the sons of Kohath from among the sons of Levi
Numbers4:3 from thirty years old and upward even unto fifty years old
Numbers4:4 This shall be the service of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting: it is most holy.
Numbers4:5 And when the camp setteth forward
Numbers4:6 and shall put thereon a covering of badgers' skin
Numbers4:7 And upon the table of shewbread they shall spread a cloth of blue; and put thereon the dishes
Numbers4:8 And they shall spread upon them a cloth of scarlet
Numbers4:9 And they shall take a cloth of blue
Numbers4:10 and they shall put it and all the utensils thereof within a covering of badgers' skin
Numbers4:11 And upon the golden altar they shall spread a cloth of blue
Numbers4:12 And they shall take all the instruments of service
Numbers4:13 And they shall cleanse the altar of the ashes
Numbers4:14 and they shall put upon it all the utensils thereof
Numbers4:15 And when Aaron and his sons have ended covering the sanctuary
Numbers4:16 And Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest shall have the oversight of the oil for the light
Numbers4:17 And Jehovah spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers4:18 Ye shall not cut off the families of the Kohathites from among the Levites
Numbers4:19 but this shall ye do unto them
Numbers4:20 but they shall not go in and see for a moment the holy things
Numbers4:21 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Numbers4:22 Take also the sum of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:23 From thirty years old and upward to fifty years old shalt thou number them; every one that cometh to labour in the work
Numbers4:24 This shall be the service of the families of the Gershonites
Numbers4:25 they shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle
Numbers4:26 and the hangings of the court
Numbers4:27 At the commandment of Aaron and his sons shall be all the service of the sons of the Gershonites
Numbers4:28 This is the service of the families of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting
Numbers4:29 The sons of Merari: after their families
Numbers4:30 from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old shalt thou number them
Numbers4:31 And this shall be the charge of their burden
Numbers4:32 and the pillars of the court round about
Numbers4:33 This is the service of the families of the sons of Merari

Numbers4:35 from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old

Numbers4:40 even those that were numbered of them
Numbers4:41 These are they that were numbered of the families of the sons of Gershon

Numbers4:43 from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old
Numbers4:44 even those that were numbered of them according to their families
Numbers4:45 These are they that were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:46 All those that were numbered of the Levites
Numbers4:47 from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old
Numbers4:48 even those that were numbered of them
Numbers4:49 According to the commandment of Jehovah they were numbered by Moses
Numbers5:1 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Numbers5:2 Command the children of Israel
Numbers5:3 both male and female shall ye put out; outside the camp shall ye put them
Numbers5:4 And the children of Israel did so
Numbers5:5 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Numbers5:6 Speak unto the children of Israel
Numbers5:7 then they shall confess their sin which they have done; and he shall recompense his trespass according to the principal thereof
Numbers5:8 And if the man have no kinsman to recompense the trespass unto
Numbers5:9 And every heave-offering of all the holy things of the children of Israel
Numbers5:10 And every man's hallowed things shall be his: whatever any man giveth the priest shall be his.
Numbers5:11 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Numbers5:12 Speak unto the children of Israel
Numbers5:13 and a man lie with her carnally
Numbers5:14 and the spirit of jealousy come upon him
Numbers5:15 then shall the man bring his wife unto the priest
Numbers5:16 And the priest shall bring her near
Numbers5:17 And the priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel; and the priest shall take of the dust that is on the floor of the tabernacle
Numbers5:18 And the priest shall set the woman before Jehovah
Numbers5:19 And the priest shall adjure her
Numbers5:20 But if thou hast gone astray to another instead of thy husband
Numbers5:21 then the priest shall adjure the woman with the oath of cursing
Numbers5:22 and this water that bringeth the curse shall enter into thy bowels
Numbers5:23 And the priest shall write these curses in a book
Numbers5:24 and he shall cause the woman to drink the bitter water that bringeth the curse
Numbers5:25 And the priest shall take out of the woman's hand the oblation of jealousy
Numbers5:26 And the priest shall take a handful of the oblation as a memorial thereof
Numbers5:27 And when he hath made her to drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman have not been defiled
Numbers5:29 This is the law of jealousies
Numbers5:30 or when the spirit of jealousy cometh upon a man
Numbers5:31 Then shall the man be free from iniquity
Numbers6:1 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Numbers6:2 Speak unto the children of Israel
Numbers6:3 he shall separate himself from wine and strong drink: he shall drink no vinegar of wine
Numbers6:4 All the days of his separation shall he eat nothing that is made of the vine
Numbers6:5 All the days of the vow of his separation there shall no razor come upon his head; until the days be fulfilled
Numbers6:6 All the days that he hath consecrated himself to Jehovah
Numbers6:7 He shall not make himself unclean for his father
Numbers6:8 All the days of his separation he is holy to Jehovah.
Numbers6:9 And if any one die unexpectedly by him suddenly
Numbers6:10 And on the eighth day he shall bring two turtle-doves
Numbers6:11 And the priest shall offer one for a sin-offering
Numbers6:12 And he shall [again] consecrate to Jehovah the days of his separation
Numbers6:13 And this is the law of the Nazarite on the day when the days of his consecration are fulfilled: he shall be brought to the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Numbers6:14 And he shall present his offering to Jehovah
Numbers6:15 and a basket with unleavened bread
Numbers6:16 And the priest shall present them before Jehovah
Numbers6:17 and he shall offer the ram
Numbers6:18 And the Nazarite shall shave the head of his consecration at the entrance to the tent of meeting
Numbers6:19 And the priest shall take the boiled shoulder of the ram
Numbers6:20 And the priest shall wave them as wave-offering before Jehovah; it is holy for the priest
Numbers6:21 This is the law of the Nazarite who hath vowed: his offering to Jehovah for his consecration
Numbers6:22 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Numbers6:23 Speak unto Aaron and unto his sons
Numbers6:24 Jehovah bless thee
Numbers6:25 Jehovah make his face shine upon thee
Numbers6:26 Jehovah lift up his countenance upon thee
Numbers6:27 And they shall put my name upon the children of Israel; and I will bless them.

Numbers15:6 And for a ram thou shalt offer as oblation two tenth parts of fine flour mingled with oil
Numbers15:7 and of wine for a drink-offering shalt thou offer the third part of a hin; for a sweet odour to Jehovah.

Numbers15:11 Thus shall it be done for one ox
Numbers15:12 according to the number that ye offer

Numbers15:29 For him that is born in the land among the children of Israel

Numbers15:31 For he hath despised the word of Jehovah

Numbers35:7 all the cities that ye shall give to the Levites shall be forty-eight cities
Numbers35:8 And the cities which ye shall give shall be of the possession of the children of Israel: from them that have much ye shall take much
Numbers35:9 And Jehovah spoke to Moses
Numbers35:10 Speak unto the children of Israel
Numbers35:11 then ye shall appoint for yourselves cities: cities of refuge shall they be for you; that a manslayer may flee thither
Numbers35:12 And ye shall have these cities for refuge from the avenger; that the manslayer die not
Numbers35:13 And the cities that ye shall give shall be six cities of refuge for you.
Numbers35:14 Three cities shall ye give on this side of the Jordan
Numbers35:15 For the children of Israel
Numbers35:16 And if he have smitten him with an instrument of iron
Numbers35:17 And if he have smitten him with a stone from the hand
Numbers35:18 Or if he have smitten him with an instrument of wood
Numbers35:19 the avenger of blood
Numbers35:20 And if he thrust at him out of hatred
Numbers35:21 or from enmity smite him with his hand
Numbers35:22 But if he have thrust at him suddenly without enmity
Numbers35:23 or [have smitten him] with any stone wherewith one may die
Numbers35:24 then the assembly shall judge between the smiter and the avenger of blood according to these judgments;
Numbers35:25 and the assembly shall rescue the manslayer out of the hand of the avenger of blood
Numbers35:26 But if the manslayer shall in any way come outside the limits of the city of his refuge whither he hath fled
Numbers35:27 and the avenger of blood find him outside the limits of his city of refuge
Numbers35:28 for the manslayer should have remained in the city of his refuge until the death of the high-priest; but after the death of the high-priest he may return into the land of his possession.
Numbers35:29 And this shall be unto you a statute of right throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Numbers35:30 Whoever shall smite a person mortally
Numbers35:31 And ye shall take no satisfaction for the life of a murderer
Numbers35:32 And ye shall take no satisfaction for him that hath fled to the city of his refuge
Numbers35:33 And ye shall not pollute the land wherein ye are; for blood
Numbers35:34 And ye shall not defile the land that ye inhabit

Joshua7:5 And the men of Ai smote of them about thirty-six men; and they pursued them from before the gate to Shebarim
Joshua7:6 And Joshua rent his clothes
Joshua7:7 And Joshua said
Joshua7:8 Ah Lord! what shall I say after Israel have turned their backs before their enemies?
Joshua7:9 When the Canaanites and all the inhabitants of the land shall hear [of it]
Joshua7:10 And Jehovah said to Joshua
Joshua7:11 Israel hath sinned
Joshua7:12 And the children of Israel shall not be able to stand before their enemies: they shall turn their backs before their enemies
Joshua7:13 Rise up
Joshua7:14 And ye shall be brought near in the morning according to your tribes; and it shall be
Joshua7:15 And it shall be
Joshua7:16 And Joshua rose early in the morning
Joshua7:17 And he caused the families of Judah to come forward
Joshua7:18 And he caused his household to come forward man by man
Joshua7:19 And Joshua said to Achan
Joshua7:20 And Achan answered Joshua and said
Joshua7:21 I saw among the spoils a beautiful mantle of Shinar
Joshua7:22 And Joshua sent messengers
Joshua7:23 And they took them out of the midst of the tent
Joshua7:24 Then Joshua
Joshua7:25 And Joshua said
Joshua7:26 And they raised over him a great heap of stones
Joshua8:1 And Jehovah said to Joshua
Joshua8:2 And thou shalt do to Ai and to its king as thou didst to Jericho and to its king; only
Joshua8:3 And Joshua arose
Joshua8:4 And he commanded them
Joshua8:5 And I and all the people that are with me will approach to the city; and it shall come to pass when they come out against us
Joshua8:6 And they will come out after us till we have drawn them from the city; for they will say
Joshua8:7 And ye shall rise up from the ambush and take possession of the city; and Jehovah your God will deliver it into your hand.
Joshua8:8 And it shall be when ye have taken the city
Joshua8:9 And Joshua sent them forth; and they went to lie in ambush
Joshua8:10 And Joshua rose early in the morning
Joshua8:11 And all the people of war that were with him went up
Joshua8:12 Now he had taken about five thousand men
Joshua8:13 And when they had set the people
Joshua8:14 And it came to pass when the king of Ai saw it
Joshua8:15 And Joshua and all Israel let themselves be beaten before them; and they fled by the way of the wilderness.
Joshua8:16 And all the people that were in the city were called together to pursue after them; and they pursued after Joshua
Joshua8:17 And not a man remained in Ai and Bethel that went not out after Israel; and they left the city open
Joshua8:18 And Jehovah said to Joshua
Joshua8:19 And the ambush arose quickly from their place
Joshua8:20 And the men of Ai turned and saw
Joshua8:21 When Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had taken the city
Joshua8:22 And the others went out of the city against them; so they were in the midst of Israel
Joshua8:23 And the king of Ai they took alive
Joshua8:24 And it came to pass when Israel had ended slaying all the inhabitants of Ai in the field
Joshua8:25 And so it was
Joshua8:26 And Joshua did not draw back his hand
Joshua8:27 Only
Joshua8:28 And Joshua burned Ai
Joshua8:29 And the king of Ai he hanged on a tree until the evening; and at the going down of the sun Joshua commanded
Joshua8:30 Then Joshua built an altar to Jehovah the God of Israel
Joshua8:31 as Moses the servant of Jehovah had commanded the children of Israel
Joshua8:32 And he wrote there on the stones a copy of the law of Moses
Joshua8:33 And all Israel
Joshua8:34 And afterwards he read all the words of the law
Joshua8:35 There was not a word of all that Moses had commanded which Joshua read not before the whole congregation of Israel

Joshua22:1 Then Joshua called the Reubenites
Joshua22:2 and said unto them
Joshua22:3 Ye have not left your brethren these many days unto this day
Joshua22:4 And now Jehovah your God hath given rest to your brethren
Joshua22:5 Only
Joshua22:6 And Joshua blessed them
Joshua22:7 And to half the tribe of Manasseh Moses had given [inheritance] in Bashan
Joshua22:8 and spoke to them
Joshua22:9 And the children of Reuben and the children of Gad and the half tribe of Manasseh returned
Joshua22:10 And they came to the districts of the Jordan that are in the land of Canaan; and the children of Reuben and the children of Gad and the half tribe of Manasseh built there an altar by the Jordan
Joshua22:11 And the children of Israel heard say
Joshua22:12 And when the children of Israel heard [of it]
Joshua22:13 And the children of Israel sent to the children of Reuben
Joshua22:14 and with him ten princes
Joshua22:15 And they came to the children of Reuben
Joshua22:16 Thus saith the whole assembly of Jehovah: What wickedness is this which ye have committed against the God of Israel
Joshua22:17 Is the iniquity of Peor too little for us? from which we are not cleansed until this day
Joshua22:18 And ye turn away this day from following Jehovah; and it will be
Joshua22:19 Notwithstanding
Joshua22:20 Did not Achan the son of Zerah commit a trespass in the accursed thing? and wrath fell on all the assembly of Israel
Joshua22:21 And the children of Reuben and the children of Gad and the half tribe of Manasseh answered and said to the heads of the thousands of Israel
Joshua22:22 The God of gods
Joshua22:23 that we have built for ourselves an altar to turn from following Jehovah
Joshua22:24 and if we have not done it from fear of this thing
Joshua22:25 Jehovah hath made the Jordan a border between us and you
Joshua22:26 And we said
Joshua22:27 but to be a witness between us and you
Joshua22:28 And we said
Joshua22:29 Far be it from us that we should rebel against Jehovah
Joshua22:30 And when Phinehas the priest and the princes of the assembly and the heads of the thousands of Israel that were with him heard the words that the children of Reuben and the children of Gad and the children of Manasseh spoke
Joshua22:31 And Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest said unto the children of Reuben
Joshua22:32 And Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest
Joshua22:33 And the thing was good in the sight of the children of Israel
Joshua22:34 And the children of Reuben and the children of Gad gave a name to the altar; for it is a witness between us that Jehovah is God.
Joshua23:1 And it came to pass a long time after that Jehovah had given rest to Israel from all their enemies round about
Joshua23:2 that Joshua called for all Israel
Joshua23:3 and ye have seen all that Jehovah your God hath done to all these nations because of you. For Jehovah your God is he that hath fought for you.
Joshua23:4 Behold
Joshua23:5 And Jehovah your God
Joshua23:6 And be ye very courageous to keep and to do all that is written in the book of the law of Moses
Joshua23:7 that ye enter not among these nations
Joshua23:8 but ye shall cleave unto Jehovah your God
Joshua23:9 For Jehovah hath dispossessed from before you great and strong nations; and as to you
Joshua23:10 One man of you chaseth a thousand; for Jehovah your God
Joshua23:11 Take great heed therefore unto your souls
Joshua23:12 For if ye in any wise go back
Joshua23:13 know for a certainty that Jehovah your God will no more dispossess these nations from before you
Joshua23:14 And behold
Joshua23:15 But it shall come to pass
Joshua23:16 when ye transgress the covenant of Jehovah your God which he commanded you

Judges1:1 And it came to pass after the death of Joshua that the children of Israel asked Jehovah
Judges1:2 And Jehovah said
Judges1:3 And Judah said to Simeon his brother
Judges1:4 And Judah went up; and Jehovah delivered the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand
Judges1:5 And they found Adoni-Bezek in Bezek
Judges1:6 And Adoni-Bezek fled
Judges1:7 And Adoni-Bezek said
Judges1:8 And the children of Judah fought against Jerusalem
Judges1:9 And afterwards the children of Judah went down to fight against the Canaanites
Judges1:10 And Judah went against the Canaanites that dwelt in Hebron the name of Hebron before was Kirjath-Arba; and they slew Sheshai and Ahiman and Talmai.
Judges1:11 And from there he went against the inhabitants of Debir; now the name of Debir before was Kirjath-sepher.
Judges1:12 And Caleb said
Judges1:13 And Othniel the son of Kenaz
Judges1:14 And it came to pass as she came
Judges1:15 And she said to him
Judges1:16 And the children of the Kenite
Judges1:17 And Judah went with Simeon his brother

Judges1:19 And Jehovah was with Judah; and he took possession of the hill-country
Judges1:20 And they gave to Caleb Hebron
Judges1:21 And the children of Benjamin did not dispossess the Jebusites
Judges1:22 And the house of Joseph
Judges1:23 And the house of Joseph sent to search out Bethel; now the name of the city before was Luz.
Judges1:24 And the guards saw a man come forth out of the city
Judges1:25 And he shewed them how to enter into the city. And they smote the city with the edge of the sword; but they let go the man and all his family.
Judges1:26 And the man went into the land of the Hittites
Judges1:27 And Manasseh did not dispossess Beth-shean and its dependent villages
Judges1:28 And it came to pass when Israel became strong
Judges1:29 And Ephraim did not dispossess the Canaanites that dwelt in Gezer; but the Canaanites dwelt among them in Gezer.
Judges1:30 Zebulun did not dispossess the inhabitants of Kitron
Judges1:31 Asher did not dispossess the inhabitants of Accho
Judges1:32 and the Asherites dwelt among the Canaanites
Judges1:33 Naphtali did not dispossess the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh
Judges1:34 And the Amorites forced the children of Dan into the hill-country
Judges1:35 And the Amorites would dwell on mount Heres
Judges1:36 And the border of the Amorites was from the ascent of Akrabbim

Judges2:4 And it came to pass

Judges3:4 And they were to prove Israel by them

Judges3:24 And when he was gone out

Judges3:27 And it came to pass when he was come

2Samuel20:18 And she spoke saying
2Samuel20:19 I am peaceable [and] faithful in Israel: thou seekest to destroy a city and a mother in Israel. Why wilt thou swallow up the inheritance of Jehovah?
2Samuel20:20 And Joab answered and said
2Samuel20:21 The matter is not so; but a man of mount Ephraim
2Samuel20:22 Then the woman went to all the people in her wisdom. And they cut off the head of Sheba the son of Bichri

2Samuel20:25 and Sheva was scribe; and Zadok and Abiathar were priests;

2Samuel22:1 And David spoke to Jehovah the words of this song in the day that Jehovah had delivered him out of the hand of all his enemies
2Samuel22:2 And he said
2Samuel22:3 God is my rock
2Samuel22:4 I will call upon Jehovah
2Samuel22:5 For the waves of death encompassed me
2Samuel22:6 The bands of Sheol surrounded me; The cords of death encountered me;
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called upon Jehovah
2Samuel22:8 Then the earth shook
2Samuel22:9 There went up a smoke out of his nostrils
2Samuel22:10 And he bowed the heavens
2Samuel22:11 And he rode upon a cherub
2Samuel22:12 And he made darkness round about him a tent
2Samuel22:13 From the brightness before him Burned forth coals of fire.
2Samuel22:14 Jehovah thundered from the heavens
2Samuel22:15 And he sent arrows
2Samuel22:16 And the beds of the sea were seen
2Samuel22:17 He reached forth from above
2Samuel22:18 He delivered me from my strong enemy
2Samuel22:19 They encountered me in the day of my calamity; But Jehovah was my stay.
2Samuel22:20 And he brought me forth into a large place; He delivered me
2Samuel22:21 Jehovah hath rewarded me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of Jehovah
2Samuel22:23 For all his ordinances were before me
2Samuel22:24 And I was upright before him
2Samuel22:25 And Jehovah hath recompensed me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 With the gracious thou dost shew thyself gracious; With the upright man thou dost shew thyself upright;
2Samuel22:27 With the pure thou dost shew thyself pure; And with the perverse thou dost shew thyself contrary.
2Samuel22:28 And the afflicted people thou dost save; And thine eyes are upon the haughty
2Samuel22:29 For thou art my lamp
2Samuel22:30 For by thee I have run through a troop; By my God have I leaped over a wall.
2Samuel22:31 As for God
2Samuel22:32 For who is God
2Samuel22:33 God is my strong fortress
2Samuel22:34 He maketh my feet like hinds' [feet]
2Samuel22:35 He teacheth my hands to war
2Samuel22:36 And thou didst give me the shield of thy salvation
2Samuel22:37 Thou enlargedst my steps under me; And mine ankles did not slip.
2Samuel22:38 I pursued mine enemies
2Samuel22:39 And I have consumed them and have crushed them
2Samuel22:40 And thou girdedst me with strength to battle: Thou didst subdue under me those that rose up against me.
2Samuel22:41 And mine enemies didst thou make to turn their backs unto me
2Samuel22:42 They looked
2Samuel22:43 And I did beat them small as the dust of the earth
2Samuel22:44 And thou hast delivered me from the strivings of my people
2Samuel22:45 Strangers come cringing unto me: At the hearing of the ear
2Samuel22:46 Strangers have faded away
2Samuel22:47 Jehovah liveth; and blessed be my rock; And exalted be the God
2Samuel22:48 The God who hath avenged me
2Samuel22:49 He brought me forth from mine enemies: Yea
2Samuel22:50 Therefore will I give thanks to thee
2Samuel22:51 [It is he] who giveth great deliverances to his king

1Kings5:3 Thou knowest that David my father could not build a house unto the name of Jehovah his God
1Kings5:4 But now Jehovah my God has given me rest on every side: there is neither adversary nor evil event.

1Kings5:6 And now command that they hew me cedar-trees out of Lebanon; and my servants shall be with thy servants; and I will give thee hire for thy servants according to all that thou shalt say; for thou knowest that there is not among us any that are experienced in cutting timber like to the Zidonians.

1Kings6:1 And it came to pass in the four hundred and eightieth year after the children of Israel were come out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 And the house that king Solomon built for Jehovah was sixty cubits in length
1Kings6:3 And the porch
1Kings6:4 And for the house he made closed windows with fixed lattices.
1Kings6:5 And against the wall of the house he built floors round about
1Kings6:6 The lowest floor was five cubits broad
1Kings6:7 And the house
1Kings6:8 The entrance to the side-chambers of the middle [floor] was in the right side of the house; and they went up by winding stairs into the middle [floor]
1Kings6:9 And he built the house
1Kings6:10 And he built the floors against all the house
1Kings6:11 And the word of Jehovah came to Solomon saying
1Kings6:12 As to this house which thou art building
1Kings6:13 and I will dwell among the children of Israel
1Kings6:14 And Solomon built the house and finished it.
1Kings6:15 And he built the walls of the house within with boards of cedar
1Kings6:16 And he built twenty cubits of the innermost part of the house
1Kings6:17 And the house
1Kings6:18 And the cedar of the house within was carved with colocynths and half-open flowers: all was cedar; there was no stone seen.
1Kings6:19 And he prepared the oracle in the midst of the house within
1Kings6:20 And the oracle within was twenty cubits in length
1Kings6:21 and Solomon overlaid the house within with pure gold
1Kings6:22 And the whole house he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:23 And he made in the oracle two cherubim of olive-wood
1Kings6:24 and one wing of the cherub was five cubits
1Kings6:25 and the other cherub was ten cubits: both the cherubim were of one measure and one form.
1Kings6:26 The height of the one cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 And he set the cherubim in the midst of the inner house; and the wings of the cherubim were stretched forth
1Kings6:28 And he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 And he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved sculptures of cherubim
1Kings6:30 And the floor of the house he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:31 And for the entrance of the oracle he made doors of olive-wood: the lintel [and] side posts were the fifth part [of the breadth of the house].
1Kings6:32 The two doors were of olive-wood; and he carved on them carvings of cherubim
1Kings6:33 And he also made for the doorway of the temple posts of olive-wood
1Kings6:34 And the two folding-doors were of cypress-wood: the two leaves of the one door were folding
1Kings6:35 And he carved on them cherubim
1Kings6:36 And he built the inner court of three rows of hewn stone
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year was the foundation of the house of Jehovah laid
1Kings6:38 and in the eleventh year
1Kings7:1 And Solomon was thirteen years building his own house; and he finished all his house.
1Kings7:2 And he built the house of the forest of Lebanon; its length was a hundred cubits
1Kings7:3 and it was covered with cedar above upon the side-chambers
1Kings7:4 And there were cross-beams in three rows
1Kings7:5 And all the doors and posts were square
1Kings7:6 And he made the porch of pillars; its length was fifty cubits
1Kings7:7 And he made the porch for the throne where he judged
1Kings7:8 And his house where he dwelt had another court within the porch
1Kings7:9 All these [buildings] were of costly stones
1Kings7:10 And the foundations were of costly stones
1Kings7:11 And above were costly stones
1Kings7:12 And the great court round about had three rows of hewn stones
1Kings7:13 And king Solomon sent and fetched Hiram out of Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was a widow's son of the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 And he formed the two pillars of brass; the height of one pillar was eighteen cubits
1Kings7:16 And he made two capitals of molten brass
1Kings7:17 [and] nets of checker-work
1Kings7:18 And he made pomegranates
1Kings7:19 And the capitals that were upon the top of the pillars were of lily-work [as] in the porch
1Kings7:20 And the capitals upon the two pillars
1Kings7:21 And he set up the pillars for the porch of the temple; and he set up the right pillar
1Kings7:22 And upon the top of the pillars was lily-work; and the work of the pillars was finished.
1Kings7:23 And he made the sea
1Kings7:24 And under the brim of it round about there were colocynths
1Kings7:25 It stood upon twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 And its thickness was a hand-breadth
1Kings7:27 And he made ten bases of brass: four cubits was the length of one base
1Kings7:28 And the work of the bases was this: they had panels
1Kings7:29 And on the panels that were between the fillets were lions
1Kings7:30 And every base had four wheels of brass
1Kings7:31 And the mouth of it within the crown and above was a cubit; and its mouth was rounded
1Kings7:32 And the four wheels were under the panels; and the supports of the wheels were in the base; and the height of a wheel was a cubit and half a cubit.
1Kings7:33 And the work of the wheels was like the work of a chariot wheel: their supports
1Kings7:34 And there were four shoulder-pieces to the four corners of one base; of the base itself were its shoulder-pieces.
1Kings7:35 And in the top of the base there was a circular elevation of half a cubit round about; and on the top of the base its stays and its panels were of the same.
1Kings7:36 And he engraved on the plates of its stays and on its panels cherubim
1Kings7:37 After this [manner] he made the ten bases: all of them had one casting
1Kings7:38 And he made ten lavers of brass: one laver contained forty baths; every laver was four cubits; upon every one of the ten bases one laver.
1Kings7:39 And he put the bases
1Kings7:40 And Hiram made the lavers
1Kings7:41 two pillars
1Kings7:42 and the four hundred pomegranates for the two networks
1Kings7:43 and the ten bases
1Kings7:44 and one sea
1Kings7:45 and the pots
1Kings7:46 In the plain of the Jordan did the king cast them
1Kings7:47 And Solomon left all the vessels [unweighed] from their exceeding number; the weight of the brass was not ascertained.
1Kings7:48 And Solomon made all the vessels that were [in] the house of Jehovah: the golden altar; and the table of gold
1Kings7:49 and the candlesticks of pure gold
1Kings7:50 and the basons
1Kings7:51 And all the work was finished that king Solomon made for the house of Jehovah. And Solomon brought in the things that David his father had dedicated; the silver and the gold and the vessels he put among the treasures of the house of Jehovah.
1Kings8:1 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel
1Kings8:2 And all the men of Israel assembled themselves to king Solomon at the feast in the month Ethanim
1Kings8:3 And all the elders of Israel came; and the priests took up the ark.
1Kings8:4 And they brought up the ark of Jehovah
1Kings8:5 And king Solomon
1Kings8:6 And the priests brought in the ark of the covenant of Jehovah to its place
1Kings8:7 for the cherubim stretched forth [their] wings over the place of the ark
1Kings8:8 And the staves were long
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark save the two tables of stone which Moses placed there at Horeb
1Kings8:10 And it came to pass when the priests were come out of the holy place
1Kings8:11 and the priests could not stand to do their service because of the cloud; for the glory of Jehovah had filled the house of Jehovah.
1Kings8:12 Then said Solomon: Jehovah said that he would dwell in the thick darkness.
1Kings8:13 I have indeed built a house of habitation for thee
1Kings8:14 And the king turned his face
1Kings8:15 And he said: Blessed be Jehovah the God of Israel
1Kings8:16 Since the day that I brought forth my people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 And it was in the heart of David my father to build a house unto the name of Jehovah the God of Israel.
1Kings8:18 But Jehovah said to David my father
1Kings8:19 nevertheless thou shalt not build the house; but thy son that shall come forth out of thy loins
1Kings8:20 And Jehovah has performed his word which he spoke; and I am risen up in the room of David my father
1Kings8:21 And I have set there a place for the ark
1Kings8:22 And Solomon stood before the altar of Jehovah in the presence of the whole congregation of Israel
1Kings8:23 And he said
1Kings8:24 who hast kept with thy servant David my father that which thou didst promise him; thou spokest with thy mouth
1Kings8:25 And now
1Kings8:26 And now
1Kings8:27 But will God indeed dwell on the earth? Behold
1Kings8:28 Yet have respect unto the prayer of thy servant
1Kings8:29 that thine eyes may be open upon this house night and day
1Kings8:30 And hearken unto the supplication of thy servant
1Kings8:31 If a man have sinned against his neighbour
1Kings8:32 then hear thou in the heavens
1Kings8:33 When thy people Israel are put to the worse before the enemy
1Kings8:34 then hear thou in the heavens
1Kings8:35 When the heavens are shut up
1Kings8:36 then hear thou in the heavens
1Kings8:37 If there be famine in the land
1Kings8:38 what prayer
1Kings8:39 then hear thou in the heavens
1Kings8:40 that they may fear thee all the days that they live upon the land which thou gavest unto our fathers.
1Kings8:41 And as to the stranger also
1Kings8:42 (for they shall hear of thy great name
1Kings8:43 hear thou in the heavens thy dwelling-place
1Kings8:44 If thy people go out to battle against their enemy
1Kings8:45 then hear thou in the heavens their prayer and their supplication
1Kings8:46 If they have sinned against thee
1Kings8:47 and if they shall take it to heart in the land whither they were carried captive
1Kings8:48 and if they return unto thee with all their heart and with all their soul
1Kings8:49 then hear thou in the heavens
1Kings8:50 and forgive thy people their sin against thee
1Kings8:51 (for they are thy people
1Kings8:52 thine eyes being open unto the supplication of thy servant
1Kings8:53 For thou hast separated them from among all peoples of the earth
1Kings8:54 And it was so
1Kings8:55 and he stood and blessed the whole congregation of Israel with a loud voice
1Kings8:56 Blessed be Jehovah
1Kings8:57 Jehovah our God be with us
1Kings8:58 that he may incline our hearts to him
1Kings8:59 And let these my words
1Kings8:60 that all peoples of the earth may know that Jehovah is God
1Kings8:61 and that your heart may be perfect with Jehovah our God
1Kings8:62 And the king
1Kings8:63 And Solomon sacrificed a sacrifice of peace-offerings
1Kings8:64 The same day the king hallowed the middle of the court that was before the house of Jehovah; for there he offered the burnt-offerings
1Kings8:65 And at that time Solomon held the feast
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away; and they blessed the king
1Kings9:1 And it came to pass when Solomon had completed the building of the house of Jehovah

1Kings9:8 and this house
1Kings9:9 And they shall say
1Kings9:10 And it came to pass at the end of twenty years
