1Kings9:13 And he said
1Kings9:14 And Hiram had sent to the king one hundred and twenty talents of gold.

1Kings9:18 and Baalath
1Kings9:19 and all the store-cities that Solomon had

1Kings10:1 And the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon in connection with the name of Jehovah
1Kings10:2 And she came to Jerusalem with a very great train
1Kings10:3 And Solomon explained to her all she spoke of: there was not a thing hidden from the king that he did not explain to her.
1Kings10:4 And when the queen of Sheba saw all Solomon's wisdom
1Kings10:5 and the food of his table
1Kings10:6 And she said to the king
1Kings10:7 but I gave no credit to the words
1Kings10:8 Happy are thy men! happy are these thy servants
1Kings10:9 Blessed be Jehovah thy God
1Kings10:10 And she gave the king a hundred and twenty talents of gold
1Kings10:11 (And the fleet also of Hiram
1Kings10:12 And the king made of the sandal-wood a balustrade for the house of Jehovah
1Kings10:13 And king Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all her desire
1Kings10:14 And the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred and sixty-six talents of gold
1Kings10:15 besides what [came] by the dealers
1Kings10:16 And king Solomon made two hundred targets of beaten gold
1Kings10:17 and three hundred shields of beaten gold
1Kings10:18 And the king made a great throne of ivory
1Kings10:19 the throne had six steps
1Kings10:20 and twelve lions stood there on the one side and on the other upon the six steps: there was not the like made in any kingdom.
1Kings10:21 And all king Solomon's drinking vessels were of gold
1Kings10:22 For the king had on the sea a Tarshish-fleet
1Kings10:23 And king Solomon was greater than all the kings of the earth in riches and in wisdom.
1Kings10:24 And all the earth sought the presence of Solomon
1Kings10:25 And they brought every man his present
1Kings10:26 And Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen; and he had a thousand four hundred chariots
1Kings10:27 And the king made silver in Jerusalem as stones
1Kings10:28 And the exportation of horses that Solomon had was from Egypt: a caravan of the king's merchants fetched a drove [of horses]
1Kings10:29 And a chariot came up and went out of Egypt for six hundred [shekels] of silver

1Kings11:2 of the nations of which Jehovah had said to the children of Israel

1Kings11:10 and had commanded him concerning this thing

1Kings11:13 only
1Kings11:14 And Jehovah stirred up an adversary to Solomon
1Kings11:15 Now it came to pass when David was in Edom
1Kings11:16 (for Joab abode there six months with all Israel

1Kings11:18 And they arose out of Midian
1Kings11:19 And Hadad found great favour in the sight of Pharaoh

1Kings11:21 And Hadad heard in Egypt that David slept with his fathers
1Kings11:22 And Pharaoh said to him

1Kings11:26 And Jeroboam the son of Nebat

1Kings11:33 because they have forsaken me

1Kings11:37 And I will take thee
1Kings11:38 And it shall be

1Kings11:41 And the rest of the acts of Solomon

2Kings23:11 And he abolished the horses that the kings of Judah had appointed to the sun at the entrance of the house of Jehovah
2Kings23:12 And the king broke down the altars that were on the roof of the upper chamber of Ahaz
2Kings23:13 And the high places that were before Jerusalem
2Kings23:14 And he broke in pieces the columns
2Kings23:15 Moreover the altar that was at Bethel
2Kings23:16 And Josiah turned himself
2Kings23:17 Then he said
2Kings23:18 And he said
2Kings23:19 And all the houses also of the high places that were in the cities of Samaria
2Kings23:20 And he sacrificed upon the altars all the priests of the high places that were there
2Kings23:21 And the king commanded all the people saying
2Kings23:22 For there was not holden such a passover from the days of the judges that judged Israel
2Kings23:23 but in the eighteenth year of king Josiah was this passover holden to Jehovah in Jerusalem.
2Kings23:24 Moreover the necromancers and the soothsayers
2Kings23:25 And before him there had been no king like him that turned to Jehovah with all his heart and with all his soul and with all his might
2Kings23:26 But Jehovah turned not from the fierceness of his great wrath
2Kings23:27 And Jehovah said
2Kings23:28 And the rest of the acts of Josiah
2Kings23:29 In his days Pharaoh-Nechoh king of Egypt went up against the king of Assyria to the river Euphrates; and king Josiah went against him; but [Nechoh] slew him at Megiddo
2Kings23:30 And his servants carried him in a chariot dead from Megiddo
2Kings23:31 Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he began to reign; and he reigned three months in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Hamutal
2Kings23:32 And he did evil in the sight of Jehovah
2Kings23:33 And Pharaoh-Nechoh had him bound at Riblah in the land of Hamath
2Kings23:34 And Pharaoh-Nechoh made Eliakim the son of Josiah king instead of Josiah his father
2Kings23:35 And Jehoiakim gave the silver and the gold to Pharaoh; but he laid a proportional tax on the land to give the money according to the command of Pharaoh: he exacted the silver and the gold of the people of the land
2Kings23:36 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign; and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Zebuddah
2Kings23:37 And he did evil in the sight of Jehovah
2Kings24:1 In his days Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up
2Kings24:2 And Jehovah sent against him the bands of the Chaldeans
2Kings24:3 Verily
2Kings24:4 and also [because of] the innocent blood that he had shed; for he had filled Jerusalem with innocent blood
2Kings24:5 And the rest of the acts of Jehoiakim
2Kings24:6 And Jehoiakim slept with his fathers
2Kings24:7 And the king of Egypt came not again any more out of his land
2Kings24:8 Jehoiachin was eighteen years old when he began to reign; and he reigned three months in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Nehushta
2Kings24:9 And he did evil in the sight of Jehovah
2Kings24:10 At that time the servants of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up against Jerusalem
2Kings24:11 And Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came against the city
2Kings24:12 And Jehoiachin king of Judah went out to the king of Babylon
2Kings24:13 And he brought out thence all the treasures of the house of Jehovah
2Kings24:14 And he carried away all Jerusalem
2Kings24:15 And he carried away Jehoiachin to Babylon
2Kings24:16 and all the men of valour
2Kings24:17 And the king of Babylon made Mattaniah his uncle king in his stead
2Kings24:18 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he began to reign; and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Hamutal
2Kings24:19 And he did evil in the sight of Jehovah
2Kings24:20 For
2Kings25:1 And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign
2Kings25:2 And the city was besieged until the eleventh year of king Zedekiah.
2Kings25:3 On the ninth of the [fourth] month the famine prevailed in the city
2Kings25:4 And the city was broken into; and all the men of war [fled] by night
2Kings25:5 And the army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king
2Kings25:6 And they took the king
2Kings25:7 and slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
2Kings25:8 And in the fifth month
2Kings25:9 and he burned the house of Jehovah
2Kings25:10 And all the army of the Chaldeans that were with the captain of the body-guard broke down the walls of Jerusalem round about.
2Kings25:11 And Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard carried away captive the rest of the people that were left in the city
2Kings25:12 But the captain of the body-guard left of the poor of the land for vinedressers and husbandmen.
2Kings25:13 And the brazen pillars that were in the house of Jehovah
2Kings25:14 The cauldrons also and the shovels and the knives and the cups
2Kings25:15 And the censers and the bowls
2Kings25:16 The two pillars
2Kings25:17 The height of one pillar was eighteen cubits
2Kings25:18 And the captain of the body-guard took Seraiah the high priest
2Kings25:19 And out of the city he took a chamberlain that was set over the men of war
2Kings25:20 And Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon to Riblah;
2Kings25:21 and the king of Babylon smote them and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. Thus Judah was carried away captive out of his land.
2Kings25:22 And [as for] the people that remained in the land of Judah
2Kings25:23 And all the captains of the forces
2Kings25:24 And Gedaliah swore unto them and to their men
2Kings25:25 And it came to pass in the seventh month
2Kings25:26 And all the people
2Kings25:27 And it came to pass in the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah
2Kings25:28 and he spoke kindly to him
2Kings25:29 And he changed his prison garments; and he ate bread before him continually all the days of his life;
2Kings25:30 and his allowance was a continual allowance given him by the king

Ezra3:1 And when the seventh month came
Ezra3:2 Then stood up Jeshua the son of Jozadak
Ezra3:3 And they set the altar on its base; for fear was upon them because of the people of the countries; and they offered up burnt-offerings on it to Jehovah
Ezra3:4 And they held the feast of tabernacles as it is written
Ezra3:5 and afterwards the continual burnt-offering
Ezra3:6 From the first day of the seventh month they began to offer up burnt-offerings to Jehovah. But the foundation of the temple of Jehovah was not [yet] laid.
Ezra3:7 And they gave money to the masons and to the carpenters; and meat and drink and oil to the Zidonians and to the Tyrians

Ezra4:1 And the adversaries of Judah and Benjamin heard that the children of the captivity were building the temple to Jehovah the God of Israel;
Ezra4:2 and they came to Zerubbabel and to the chief fathers
Ezra4:3 But Zerubbabel and Jeshua and the rest of the chief fathers of Israel said to them

Ezra4:5 and they hired counsellors against them
Ezra4:6 And in the reign of Ahasuerus
Ezra4:7 And in the days of Artaxerxes
Ezra4:8 Rehum the chancellor and Shimshai the scribe wrote a letter against Jerusalem to Artaxerxes the king after this sort:
Ezra4:9 Rehum the chancellor
Ezra4:10 and the rest of the peoples whom the great and noble Osnappar brought over and settled in the cities of Samaria
Ezra4:11 This is the copy of the letter that they sent to him: To Artaxerxes the king: Thy servants the men on this side the river
Ezra4:12 Be it known to the king that the Jews who came up from thee unto us have come to Jerusalem; they are building the rebellious and the bad city
Ezra4:13 Be it known therefore unto the king
Ezra4:14 Now
Ezra4:15 that search may be made in the book of the annals of thy fathers: so shalt thou find in the book of the annals and know that this city is a rebellious city
Ezra4:16 We inform the king that if this city be built and its walls be completed
Ezra4:17 The king sent an answer to Rehum the chancellor
Ezra4:18 The letter that ye sent to us has been read before me distinctly.
Ezra4:19 And I gave orders
Ezra4:20 And there have been mighty kings over Jerusalem
Ezra4:21 Now give order to make these men to cease
Ezra4:22 and take heed that ye fail not to do this: why should harm grow to the damage of the kings?
Ezra4:23 As soon as the copy of king Artaxerxes' letter was read before Rehum
Ezra4:24 Then ceased the work of the house of God which is at Jerusalem; and it ceased until the second year of the reign of Darius king of Persia.

Nehemiah3:4 And next to them repaired Meremoth the son of Urijah
Nehemiah3:5 And next to them the Tekoites repaired; but their nobles put not their necks to the work of their Lord.

Job1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job; and this man was perfect and upright
Job1:2 And there were born to him seven sons and three daughters.
Job1:3 And his substance was seven thousand sheep
Job1:4 And his sons went and made a feast in the house of each one on his day; and they sent and invited their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.
Job1:5 And it was so
Job1:6 And there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before Jehovah; and Satan came also among them.
Job1:7 And Jehovah said to Satan
Job1:8 And Jehovah said to Satan
Job1:9 And Satan answered Jehovah and said
Job1:10 Hast not thou made a hedge about him
Job1:11 But put forth thy hand now and touch all that he hath
Job1:12 And Jehovah said to Satan
Job1:13 And there was a day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in the house of their brother
Job1:14 And there came a messenger to Job and said
Job1:15 and [they of] Sheba fell [upon them] and took them
Job1:16 While he was yet speaking
Job1:17 While he was yet speaking
Job1:18 While he was yet speaking
Job1:19 and behold
Job1:20 And Job rose up
Job1:21 and he said
Job1:22 In all this Job sinned not
Job2:1 And there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before Jehovah
Job2:2 And Jehovah said to Satan
Job2:3 And Jehovah said to Satan
Job2:4 And Satan answered Jehovah and said
Job2:5 but put forth thy hand now
Job2:6 And Jehovah said to Satan
Job2:7 And Satan went forth from the presence of Jehovah; and he smote Job with a grievous botch from the sole of his foot unto his crown.
Job2:8 And he took a potsherd to scrape himself with; and he sat among the ashes.
Job2:9 And his wife said to him
Job2:10 But he said to her
Job2:11 And three friends of Job heard of all this evil that was come upon him. And they came each one from his place: Eliphaz the Temanite
Job2:12 And when they lifted up their eyes afar off
Job2:13 And they sat down with him on the ground seven days and seven nights; and none spoke a word to him; for they saw that [his] anguish was very great.
Job3:1 After this
Job3:2 And Job answered and said
Job3:3 Let the day perish in which I was born
Job3:4 That day let it be darkness
Job3:5 Let darkness and the shadow of death claim it; let clouds dwell upon it; let darkeners of the day terrify it.
Job3:6 That night let gloom seize upon it; let it not rejoice among the days of the year; let it not come into the number of the months.
Job3:7 Behold
Job3:8 Let them curse it that curse the day
Job3:9 Let the stars of its twilight be dark; let it wait for light
Job3:10 Because it shut not up the doors of the womb that bore me
Job3:11 Wherefore did I not die from the womb
Job3:12 Why did the knees meet me? and wherefore the breasts
Job3:13 For now should I have lain down and been quiet; I should have slept: then had I been at rest
Job3:14 With kings and counsellors of the earth
Job3:15 Or with princes who had gold
Job3:16 Or as a hidden untimely birth I had not been; as infants that have not seen the light.
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from troubling; and there the wearied are at rest.
Job3:18 The prisoners together are at ease; they hear not the voice of the taskmaster.
Job3:19 The small and great are there
Job3:20 Wherefore is light given to him that is in trouble
Job3:21 Who long for death
Job3:22 Who rejoice even exultingly and are glad when they find the grave?
Job3:23 To the man whose way is hidden
Job3:24 For my sighing cometh before my bread
Job3:25 For I feared a fear
Job3:26 I was not in safety
Job4:1 And Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said
Job4:2 If a word were essayed to thee
Job4:3 Behold
Job4:4 Thy words have upholden him that was stumbling
Job4:5 But now it is come upon thee
Job4:6 Hath not thy piety been thy confidence
Job4:7 Remember
Job4:8 Even as I have seen
Job4:9 By the breath of God they perish
Job4:10 The roar of the lion
Job4:11 The old lion perisheth for lack of prey
Job4:12 Now to me a word was secretly brought
Job4:13 In thoughts from visions of the night
Job4:14 Fear came on me
Job4:15 And a spirit passed before my face the hair of my flesh stood up
Job4:16 It stood still; I could not discern the appearance thereof: a form was before mine eyes; I heard a slight murmur and a voice:
Job4:17 Shall [mortal] man be more just than God? Shall a man be purer than his Maker?
Job4:18 Lo
Job4:19 How much more them that dwell in houses of clay
Job4:20 From morning to evening are they smitten: without any heeding it
Job4:21 Is not their tent-cord torn away in them? they die
Job5:1 Call
Job5:2 For vexation killeth the foolish man
Job5:3 I myself saw the foolish taking root
Job5:4 His children are far from safety
Job5:5 Whose harvest the hungry eateth up
Job5:6 For evil cometh not forth from the dust
Job5:7 For man is born to trouble
Job5:8 But as for me I will seek unto God
Job5:9 Who doeth great things and unsearchable
Job5:10 Who giveth rain on the face of the earth
Job5:11 Setting up on high those that are low; and mourners are exalted to prosperity.
Job5:12 He disappointeth the devices of the crafty
Job5:13 He taketh the wise in their own craftiness; and the counsel of the wily is carried headlong:
Job5:14 They meet with darkness in a the daytime
Job5:15 And he saveth the needy from the sword
Job5:16 So the poor hath what he hopeth for
Job5:17 Behold
Job5:18 For he maketh sore
Job5:19 He will deliver thee in six troubles
Job5:20 In famine he will redeem thee from death
Job5:21 Thou shalt be hidden from the scourge of the tongue; and thou shalt not be afraid of destruction when it cometh.
Job5:22 At destruction and famine thou shalt laugh
Job5:23 For thou shalt be in league with the stones of the field
Job5:24 And thou shalt know that thy tent is in peace; and thou wilt survey thy fold
Job5:25 And thou shalt know that thy seed is numerous
Job5:26 Thou shalt come to the grave in a ripe age
Job5:27 Behold this
Job6:1 And Job answered and said
Job6:2 Oh that my grief were thoroughly weighed
Job6:3 For now it would be heavier than the sand of the seas; therefore my words are vehement.
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty are within me
Job6:5 Doth the wild ass bray by the grass? loweth an ox over his fodder?
Job6:6 Shall that which is insipid be eaten without salt? Is there any taste in the white of an egg?
Job6:7 What my soul refuseth to touch
Job6:8 Oh that I might have my request
Job6:9 And that it would please God to crush me
Job6:10 Then should I yet have comfort; and in the pain which spareth not I would rejoice that I have not denied the words of the Holy One.
Job6:11 What is my strength
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones? is my flesh of brass?
Job6:13 Is it not that there is no help in me
Job6:14 For him that is fainting kindness [is meet] from his friend; or he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.
Job6:15 My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a stream
Job6:16 Which are turbid by reason of the ice
Job6:17 At the time they diminish
Job6:18 They wind about in the paths of their course
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema looked
Job6:20 They are ashamed at their hope; they come thither
Job6:21 So now ye are nothing; ye see a terrible object and are afraid.
Job6:22 Did I say
Job6:23 Or
Job6:24 Teach me
Job6:25 How forcible are right words! but what doth your upbraiding reprove?
Job6:26 Do ye imagine to reprove words? The speeches of one that is desperate are indeed for the wind.
Job6:27 Yea
Job6:28 Now therefore if ye will
Job6:29 Return
Job6:30 Is there wrong in my tongue? cannot my taste discern mischievous things?
Job7:1 Hath not man a life of labour upon earth? and are not his days like the days of a hireling?
Job7:2 As a bondman earnestly desireth the shadow
Job7:3 So am I made to possess months of vanity
Job7:4 If I lie down
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken
Job7:6 My days are swifter than a weaver's shuttle
Job7:7 Remember thou that my life is wind; mine eye shall no more see good.
Job7:8 The eye of him that hath seen me shall behold me no [more]: thine eyes are upon me
Job7:9 The cloud consumeth and vanisheth away; so he that goeth down to Sheol shall not come up.
Job7:10 He shall return no more to his house
Job7:11 Therefore I will not restrain my mouth: I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
Job7:12 Am I a sea
Job7:13 When I say
Job7:14 Then thou scarest me with dreams
Job7:15 So that my soul chooseth strangling
Job7:16 I loathe it; I shall not live always: let me alone
Job7:17 What is man
Job7:18 And that thou visitest him every morning
Job7:19 How long wilt thou not look away from me
Job7:20 Have I sinned
Job7:21 And why dost not thou forgive my transgression and take away mine iniquity? for now shall I lie down in the dust
Job8:1 And Bildad the Shuhite answered and said
Job8:2 How long wilt thou speak these things? and the words of thy mouth be a strong wind?
Job8:3 Doth God pervert judgment
Job8:4 If thy children have sinned against him
Job8:5 If thou seek earnestly unto God
Job8:6 If thou be pure and upright
Job8:7 And though thy beginning was small
Job8:8 For inquire
Job8:9 For we are [but] of yesterday
Job8:10 Shall not they teach thee
Job8:11 Doth the papyrus shoot up without mire? doth the reed-grass grow without water?
Job8:12 Whilst it is yet in its greenness [and] not cut down
Job8:13 So are the paths of all that forget God; and the profane man's hope shall perish
Job8:14 Whose confidence shall be cut off
Job8:15 He shall lean upon his house
Job8:16 He is full of sap before the sun
Job8:17 His roots are entwined about the stoneheap; he seeth the place of stones.
Job8:18 If he destroy him from his place
Job8:19 Behold
Job8:20 Behold
Job8:21 Whilst he would fill thy mouth with laughing and thy lips with shouting
Job8:22 They that hate thee shall be clothed with shame
Job9:1 And Job answered and said
Job9:2 Of a truth I know it is so; but how can man be just with God?
Job9:3 If he shall choose to strive with him
Job9:4 He is wise in heart and mighty in strength: who hath hardened himself against him
Job9:5 Who removeth mountains
Job9:6 Who shaketh the earth out of its place
Job9:7 Who commandeth the sun
Job9:8 Who alone spreadeth out the heavens
Job9:9 Who maketh the Bear
Job9:10 Who doeth great things past finding out
Job9:11 Lo
Job9:12 Behold
Job9:13 God withdraweth not his anger; the proud helpers stoop under him:
Job9:14 How much less shall I answer him
Job9:15 Whom
Job9:16 If I had called
Job9:17 He
Job9:18 He suffereth me not to take my breath
Job9:19 Be it a question of strength
Job9:20 If I justified myself
Job9:21 Were I perfect
Job9:22 It is all one; therefore I said
Job9:23 If the scourge kill suddenly
Job9:24 The earth is given over into the hand of the wicked [man]; he covereth the faces of its judges. If not
Job9:25 And my days are swifter than a runner: they flee away
Job9:26 They pass by like skiffs of reed; as an eagle that swoops upon the prey.
Job9:27 If I say
Job9:28 I am afraid of all my sorrows; I know that thou wilt not hold me innocent.
Job9:29 Be it that I am wicked
Job9:30 If I washed myself with snow-water
Job9:31 Then wouldest thou plunge me in the ditch
Job9:32 For he is not a man
Job9:33 There is not an umpire between us
Job9:34 Let him take his rod away from me
Job9:35 [Then] I will speak
Job10:1 My soul is weary of my life: I will give free course to my complaint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
Job10:2 I will say unto God
Job10:3 Doth it please thee to oppress
Job10:4 Hast thou eyes of flesh? or seest thou as man seeth?
Job10:5 Are thy days as the days of a mortal? are thy years as a man's days
Job10:6 That thou searchest after mine iniquity
Job10:7 Since thou knowest that I am not wicked
Job10:8 Thy hands have bound me together and made me as one
Job10:9 Remember
Job10:10 Hast thou not poured me out as milk
Job10:11 Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh
Job10:12 Thou hast granted me life and favour
Job10:13 And these things didst thou hide in thy heart; I know that this was with thee.
Job10:14 If I sinned
Job10:15 If I were wicked
Job10:16 And it increaseth: thou huntest me as a fierce lion; and ever again thou shewest thy marvellous power upon me.
Job10:17 Thou renewest thy witnesses before me and increasest thy displeasure against me; successions [of evil] and a time of toil are with me.
Job10:18 And wherefore didst thou bring me forth out of the womb? I had expired
Job10:19 I should be as though I had not been; I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.
Job10:20 Are not my days few? cease then and let me alone
Job10:21 Before I go
Job10:22 A land of gloom
Job11:1 And Zophar the Naamathite answered and said
Job11:2 Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man of much talk be justified?
Job11:3 Should thy fictions make men hold their peace? and shouldest thou mock
Job11:4 For thou sayest
Job11:5 But oh that God would speak
Job11:6 And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom
Job11:7 Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty to perfection?
Job11:8 [It is as] the heights of heaven; what wilt thou do? deeper than Sheol; what canst thou know?
Job11:9 The measure thereof is longer than the earth
Job11:10 If he pass by
Job11:11 For he knoweth vain men
Job11:12 Yet a senseless man will make bold
Job11:13 If thou prepare thy heart and stretch out thy hands toward him
Job11:14 If thou put far away the iniquity which is in thy hand
Job11:15 Surely then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot
Job11:16 For thou shalt forget misery; as waters that are passed away shalt thou remember it;
Job11:17 And life shall arise brighter than noonday; though thou be enshrouded in darkness
Job11:18 And thou shalt have confidence
Job11:19 Yea
Job11:20 But the eyes of the wicked shall fail
Job12:1 And Job answered and said
Job12:2 Truly ye are the people
Job12:3 I also have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you; and who knoweth not such things as these?
Job12:4 I am to be one that is a derision to his friend
Job12:5 He that is ready to stumble with the foot is a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.
Job12:6 The tents of desolators are in peace
Job12:7 But ask now the beasts
Job12:8 Or speak to the earth
Job12:9 Who knoweth not in all these
Job12:10 In whose hand is the soul of every living thing
Job12:11 Doth not the ear try words
Job12:12 With the aged is wisdom
Job12:13 With him is wisdom and might; he hath counsel and understanding.
Job12:14 Behold
Job12:15 Behold
Job12:16 With him is strength and effectual knowledge; the deceived and the deceiver are his.
Job12:17 He leadeth counsellors away spoiled
Job12:18 He weakeneth the government of kings
Job12:19 He leadeth priests away spoiled
Job12:20 He depriveth of speech the trusty
Job12:21 He poureth contempt upon nobles
Job12:22 He discovereth deep things out of darkness
Job12:23 He increaseth the nations
Job12:24 He taketh away the understanding of the chiefs of the people of the earth
Job12:25 They grope in the dark without light
Job13:1 Lo
Job13:2 What ye know
Job13:3 But I will speak to the Almighty
Job13:4 For ye indeed are forgers of lies
Job13:5 Oh that ye would be altogether silent! and it would be your wisdom.
Job13:6 Hear now my defence
Job13:7 Will ye speak unrighteously for God? and for him speak deceit?
Job13:8 Will ye accept his person? will ye contend for God?
Job13:9 Will it be well if he should search you out? or as one mocketh at a man
Job13:10 He will certainly reprove you
Job13:11 Shall not his excellency terrify you? and his dread fall upon you?
Job13:12 Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ashes
Job13:13 Hold your peace from me
Job13:14 Wherefore should I take my flesh in my teeth
Job13:15 Behold
Job13:16 This also shall be my salvation
Job13:17 Hear attentively my speech and my declaration with your ears.
Job13:18 Behold now

Job13:20 Only do not two things unto me; then will I not hide myself from thee.
Job13:21 Withdraw thy hand far from me; and let not thy terror make me afraid:
Job13:22 Then call

Job13:26 For thou writest bitter things against me
Job13:27 And thou puttest my feet in the stocks
Job13:28 One who
Job14:1 Man
Job14:2 He cometh forth like a flower
Job14:3 Yet dost thou open thine eyes upon such a one
Job14:4 Who can bring a clean [man] out of the unclean? Not one!
Job14:5 If his days are determined
Job14:6 Look away from him; and let him rest
Job14:7 For there is hope for a tree: if it be cut down
Job14:8 Though its root grow old in the earth
Job14:9 Yet through the scent of water it will bud
Job14:10 But a man dieth
Job14:11 The waters recede from the lake
Job14:12 So man lieth down

Job14:19 The waters wear the stones
Job14:20 Thou prevailest for ever against him
Job14:21 His sons come to honour
Job14:22 But his flesh hath pain for himself alone
Job15:1 And Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said
Job15:2 Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge
Job15:3 Reasoning with unprofitable talk
Job15:4 Yea
Job15:5 For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity
Job15:6 Thine own mouth condemneth thee
Job15:7 Art thou the first man that was born? and wast thou brought forth before the hills?
Job15:8 Hast thou listened in the secret council of God? And hast thou absorbed wisdom for thyself?
Job15:9 What knowest thou that we know not? [what] understandest thou which is not in us?
Job15:10 Both the greyheaded and the aged are with us
Job15:11 Are the consolations of God too small for thee? and the word gently spoken to thee?
Job15:12 Why doth thy heart carry thee away? and why do thine eyes wink?
Job15:13 That thou turnest thy spirit against God
Job15:14 What is man
Job15:15 Behold
Job15:16 How much less the abominable and corrupt
Job15:17 I will shew thee
Job15:18 Which wise men have told from their fathers
Job15:19 Unto whom alone the earth was given
Job15:20 All his days the wicked man is tormented
Job15:21 The sound of terrors is in his ears: in prosperity the destroyer cometh upon him.
Job15:22 He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness
Job15:23 He wandereth abroad for bread
Job15:24 Distress and anguish make him afraid; they prevail against him
Job15:25 For he hath stretched out his hand against God
Job15:26 He runneth against him
Job15:27 For he hath covered his face with his fatness
Job15:28 And he dwelleth in desolate cities
Job15:29 He shall not become rich
Job15:30 He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches; and by the breath of his mouth shall he go away.
Job15:31 Let him not trust in vanity: he is deceived
Job15:32 It shall be complete before his day
Job15:33 He shall shake off his unripe grapes as a vine
Job15:34 For the family of the ungodly shall be barren
Job15:35 They conceive mischief
Job16:1 And Job answered and said
Job16:2 I have heard many such things: grievous comforters are ye all.
Job16:3 Shall words of wind have an end? or what provoketh thee that thou answerest?
Job16:4 I also could speak as ye: if your soul were in my soul's stead
Job16:5 [But] I would encourage you with my mouth
Job16:6 If I speak
Job16:7 But now he hath made me weary; thou hast made desolate all my family;
Job16:8 Thou hast shrivelled me up! it is become a witness; and my leanness riseth up against me
Job16:9 His anger teareth and pursueth me; he gnasheth with his teeth against me; [as] mine adversary he sharpeneth his eyes at me.
Job16:10 They gape upon me with their mouth; they smite my cheeks reproachfully; they range themselves together against me.
Job16:11 God hath delivered me over to the iniquitous man
Job16:12 I was at rest
Job16:13 His arrows encompass me round about
Job16:14 He breaketh me with breach upon breach; he runneth upon me like a mighty man.
Job16:15 I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin
Job16:16 My face is red with weeping
Job16:17 Although there is no violence in my hands
Job16:18 O earth
Job16:19 Even now
Job16:20 My friends are my mockers; mine eye poureth out tears unto God.
Job16:21 Oh that there were arbitration for a man with God
Job16:22 For years [few] in number shall pass
Job17:1 My breath is corrupt
Job17:2 Are there not mockers around me? and doth [not] mine eye abide in their provocation?
Job17:3 Lay down now [a pledge]
Job17:4 For thou hast hidden their heart from understanding; therefore thou wilt not exalt [them].
Job17:5 He that betrayeth friends for a prey even the eyes of his children shall fail.
Job17:6 And he hath made me a proverb of the peoples; and I am become one to be spit on in the face.
Job17:7 And mine eye is dim by reason of grief
Job17:8 Upright men [shall be] astonished at this
Job17:9 But the righteous shall hold on his way
Job17:10 But as for you all
Job17:11 My days are past
Job17:12 They change the night into day; the light [they imagine] near in presence of the darkness.
Job17:13 If I wait
Job17:14 I cry to the grave
Job17:15 And where is then my hope? yea
Job17:16 It shall go down to the bars of Sheol

Job20:1 And Zophar the Naamathite answered and said
Job20:2 Therefore do my thoughts give me an answer
Job20:3 I hear a reproof putting me to shame; and [my] spirit answereth me by mine understanding.
Job20:4 Knowest thou [not] this
Job20:5 The exultation of the wicked is short
Job20:6 Though his height mount up to the heavens
Job20:7 Like his own dung doth he perish for ever; they which have seen him shall say
Job20:8 He flieth away as a dream
Job20:9 The eye which saw him shall [see him] not again; and his place beholdeth him no more.
Job20:10 His children shall seek the favour of the poor
Job20:11 His bones were full of his youthful strength; but it shall lie down with him in the dust.
Job20:12 Though wickedness be sweet in his mouth [and] he hide it under his tongue
Job20:13 [Though] he spare it
Job20:14 His food is turned in his bowels; it is the gall of asps within him.
Job20:15 He hath swallowed down riches
Job20:16 He shall suck the poison of asps; the viper's tongue shall kill him.
Job20:17 He shall not see streams
Job20:18 That which he laboured for shall he restore
Job20:19 For he hath oppressed
Job20:20 Because he knew no rest in his craving
Job20:21 Nothing escaped his greediness; therefore his prosperity shall not endure.
Job20:22 In the fulness of his sufficiency he shall be in straits; every hand of the wretched shall come upon him.
Job20:23 It shall be that
Job20:24 If he have fled from the iron weapon
Job20:25 He draweth it forth; it cometh out of his body
Job20:26 All darkness is laid up for his treasures: a fire not blown shall devour him; it shall feed upon what is left in his tent.
Job20:27 The heavens shall reveal his iniquity
Job20:28 The increase of his house shall depart
Job20:29 This is the portion of the wicked man from God
Job21:1 And Job answered and said
Job21:2 Hear attentively my speech
Job21:3 Suffer me and I will speak; and after I have spoken
Job21:4 As for me
Job21:5 Mark me
Job21:6 Even when I think [thereon]
Job21:7 Wherefore do the wicked live
Job21:8 Their seed is established with them in their sight
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from fear
Job21:10 Their bull gendereth
Job21:11 They send forth their little ones like a flock
Job21:12 They shout to the tambour and harp
Job21:13 They spend their days in prosperity
Job21:14 And they say unto God
Job21:15 What is the Almighty that we should serve him? and what are we profited if we pray unto him?
Job21:16 Behold
Job21:17 How often is the lamp of the wicked put out
Job21:18 Do they become as stubble before the wind
Job21:19 God layeth up [the punishment of] his iniquity for his children; he rewardeth him
Job21:20 His eyes shall see his destruction
Job21:21 For what pleasure should he have in his house after him
Job21:22 Can any teach God knowledge? And he it is that judgeth those that are high.
Job21:23 One dieth in his full strength
Job21:24 His sides are full of fat
Job21:25 And another dieth in bitterness of soul
Job21:26 Together they lie down in the dust
Job21:27 Lo
Job21:28 For ye say
Job21:29 Have ye not asked the wayfarers? and do ye not regard their tokens:
Job21:30 That the wicked is reserved for the day of calamity? They are led forth to the day of wrath.
Job21:31 Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?
Job21:32 Yet is he carried to the graves
Job21:33 The clods of the valley are sweet unto him; and every man followeth suit after him
Job21:34 How then comfort ye me in vain? Your answers remain perfidious.
Job22:1 And Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said
Job22:2 Can a man be profitable to God? surely it is unto himself that the wise man is profitable.
Job22:3 Is it any pleasure to the Almighty if thou art righteous? And is it gain [to him] that thou makest thy ways perfect?
Job22:4 Will he reason with thee for fear of thee? Will he enter with thee into judgment?
Job22:5 Is not thy wickedness great? and thine iniquities without end?
Job22:6 For thou hast taken a pledge of thy brother for nought
Job22:7 Thou hast not given water to the fainting to drink
Job22:8 But the powerful man
Job22:9 Widows hast thou sent empty away
Job22:10 Therefore snares are round about thee
Job22:11 Or darkness
Job22:12 Is not God in the height of the heavens? And behold the summit of the stars: how exalted are they!
Job22:13 And thou sayest
Job22:14 Thick clouds are a covering to him
Job22:15 Dost thou mark the ancient path which wicked men have trodden?
Job22:16 Who were carried off before the time
Job22:17 Who said unto God

Job22:28 And thou shalt decree a thing

Job22:30 [Even] him that is not innocent shall he deliver; yea
Job23:1 And Job answered and said
Job23:2 Even to-day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning.
Job23:3 Oh that I knew where I might find him
Job23:4 I would order the cause before him
Job23:5 I would know the words he would answer me
Job23:6 Would he plead against me with [his] great power? Nay; but he would give heed unto me.
Job23:7 There would an upright man reason with him; and I should be delivered for ever from my judge.
Job23:8 Lo
Job23:9 On the left hand
Job23:10 But he knoweth the way that I take; he trieth me
Job23:11 My foot hath held to his steps; his way have I kept
Job23:12 Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have laid up the words of his mouth more than the purpose of my own heart.
Job23:13 But he is in one [mind]
Job23:14 For he will perform [what] is appointed for me; and many such things are with him.
Job23:15 Therefore am I troubled at his presence; I consider
Job23:16 For God hath made my heart soft
Job23:17 Because I was not cut off before the darkness
Job24:1 Why are not times treasured up with the Almighty? why do not they that know him see his days?
Job24:2 They remove the landmarks; they violently take away the flocks and pasture them;
Job24:3 They drive away the ass of the fatherless
Job24:4 They turn the needy out of the way: the afflicted of the land all hide themselves.
Job24:5 Lo
Job24:6 They reap in the field the fodder thereof
Job24:7 They pass the night naked without clothing
Job24:8 They are wet with the showers of the mountains
Job24:9 They pluck the fatherless from the breast
Job24:10 These go naked without clothing
Job24:11 They press out oil within their walls
Job24:12 Men groan from out of the city
Job24:13 There are those that rebel against the light; they know not the ways thereof
Job24:14 The murderer riseth with the light
Job24:15 And the eye of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves in; they know not the light:
Job24:17 For the morning is to them all [as] the shadow of death; for they are familiar with the terrors of the shadow of death.
Job24:18 He is swift on the face of the waters; their portion is cursed on the earth: he turneth not unto the way of the vineyards.
Job24:19 Drought and heat consume snow waters; so doth Sheol those that have sinned.
Job24:20 The womb forgetteth him; the worm feedeth sweetly on him: he shall be no more remembered; and unrighteousness is broken as a tree
Job24:21 He that despoileth the barren that beareth not
Job24:22 He draweth also the mighty with his power; he riseth up
Job24:23 [God] setteth him in safety
Job24:24 They are exalted for a little
Job24:25 If it be not so now
Job25:1 And Bildad the Shuhite answered and said
Job25:2 Dominion and fear are with him; he maketh peace in his high places.
Job25:3 Is there any number of his troops? and upon whom doth not his light arise?
Job25:4 And how should man be just with God? Or how should he be clean that is born of a woman?
Job25:5 Lo
Job25:6 How much less man
Job26:1 And Job answered and said
Job26:2 How hast thou helped the powerless; how saved the arm that is without strength!
Job26:3 How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom
Job26:4 For whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?
Job26:5 The shades tremble beneath the waters and the inhabitants thereof;
Job26:6 Sheol is naked before him
Job26:7 He stretcheth out the north over empty space
Job26:8 He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds
Job26:9 He covereth the face of his throne
Job26:10 He hath traced a fixed circle over the waters
Job26:11 The pillars of the heavens tremble and are astonished at his rebuke.
Job26:12 He stirreth up the sea by his power
Job26:13 By his Spirit the heavens are adorned; his hand hath formed the fleeing serpent.
Job26:14 Lo
Job27:1 And Job continued his parable and said
Job27:2 [As] God liveth
Job27:3 All the while my breath is in me
Job27:4 My lips shall not speak unrighteousness
Job27:5 Be it far from me that I should justify you; till I die I will not remove my blamelessness from me.
Job27:6 My righteousness I hold fast
Job27:7 Let mine enemy be as the wicked
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the ungodly
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry when distress cometh upon him?
Job27:10 Doth he delight himself in the Almighty? will he at all times call upon God?
Job27:11 I will teach you concerning the hand of God; what is with the Almighty will I not conceal.
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 This is the portion of the wicked man with God
Job27:14 If his children be multiplied
Job27:15 Those that remain of him shall be buried by death
Job27:16 Though he heap up silver as the dust
Job27:17 He may prepare it
Job27:18 He buildeth his house as the moth
Job27:19 He lieth down rich
Job27:20 Terrors overtake him like waters; a whirlwind stealeth him away in the night.
Job27:21 The east wind carrieth him away and he is gone; and as a storm it hurleth him out of his place.
Job27:22 And [God] shall cast upon him and not spare: he would fain flee out of his hand.
Job27:23 [Men] shall clap their hands at him
Job28:1 Surely there is a vein for the silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken out of the dust
Job28:3 [Man] putteth an end to the darkness
Job28:4 He openeth a shaft far from the inhabitants [of the earth]: forgotten of the foot
Job28:5 As for the earth
Job28:6 The stones of it are the place of sapphires
Job28:7 It is a path no bird of prey knoweth
Job28:8 The proud beasts have not trodden it
Job28:9 [Man] putteth forth his hand upon the flinty rock
Job28:10 He cutteth out channels in the rocks
Job28:11 He bindeth the streams that they drip not
Job28:12 But wisdom
Job28:13 Man knoweth not the value thereof; and it is not found in the land of the living.
Job28:14 The deep saith
Job28:15 Choice gold cannot be given for it
Job28:16 It is not set in the balance with gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Gold and glass cannot be compared to it
Job28:18 Corals and crystal are no more remembered; yea
Job28:19 The topaz of Ethiopia shall not be compared to it
Job28:20 Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 For it is hidden from the eyes of all living
Job28:22 Destruction and death say
Job28:23 God understandeth the way thereof
Job28:24 For he looketh to the ends of the earth
Job28:25 In making a weight for the wind
Job28:26 In appointing a statute for the rain
Job28:27 Then did he see it
Job28:28 And unto man he said
Job29:1 And Job continued his parable and said
Job29:2 Oh that I were as in months past
Job29:3 When his lamp shone over my head
Job29:4 As I was in the days of my youth
Job29:5 When the Almighty was yet with me
Job29:6 When my steps were bathed in milk
Job29:7 When I went out to the gate by the city
Job29:8 The young men saw me
Job29:9 Princes refrained from talking
Job29:10 The voice of the nobles was hushed
Job29:11 When the ear heard [me]
Job29:12 For I delivered the afflicted that cried
Job29:13 The blessing of him that was perishing came upon me
Job29:14 I put on righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind
Job29:16 I was a father to the needy
Job29:17 And I broke the jaws of the unrighteous
Job29:18 And I said
Job29:19 My root shall be spread out to the waters
Job29:20 My glory shall be fresh in me
Job29:21 Unto me they listened
Job29:22 After my words they spoke not again
Job29:23 And they waited for me as for the rain
Job29:24 [If] I laughed on them
Job29:25 I chose their way
Job30:1 But now they that are younger than I have me in derision
Job30:2 Yea
Job30:3 Withered up through want and hunger
Job30:4 They gather the salt-wort among the bushes
Job30:5 They are driven forth from among [men] they cry after them as after a thief
Job30:6 To dwell in gloomy gorges
Job30:7 They bray among the bushes; under the brambles they are gathered together:
Job30:8 Sons of fools
Job30:9 And now I am their song
Job30:10 They abhor me
Job30:11 For he hath loosed my cord and afflicted me; so they cast off the bridle before me.
Job30:12 At [my] right hand rise the young brood; they push away my feet
Job30:13 They mar my path
Job30:14 They come in as through a wide breach: amid the confusion they roll themselves onward.
Job30:15 Terrors are turned against me; they pursue mine honour as the wind; and my welfare is passed away like a cloud.
Job30:16 And now my soul is poured out in me; days of affliction have taken hold upon me.
Job30:17 The night pierceth through my bones [and detacheth them] from me
Job30:18 By their great force they have become my raiment; they bind me about as the collar of my coat.
Job30:19 He hath cast me into the mire
Job30:20 I cry unto thee
Job30:21 Thou art changed to a cruel one to me; with the strength of thy hand thou pursuest me.
Job30:22 Thou liftest me up to the wind; thou causest me to be borne away
Job30:23 For I know that thou wilt bring me to death
Job30:24 Indeed
Job30:25 Did not I weep for him whose days were hard? was not my soul grieved for the needy?
Job30:26 For I expected good
Job30:27 My bowels well up
Job30:28 I go about blackened
Job30:29 I am become a brother to jackals
Job30:30 My skin is become black [and falleth] off me
Job30:31 My harp also is [turned] to mourning
Job31:1 I made a covenant with mine eyes; and how should I fix my regard upon a maid?
Job31:2 For what would have been [my] portion of God from above
Job31:3 Is not calamity for the unrighteous? and misfortune for the workers of iniquity?
Job31:4 Doth not he see my ways
Job31:5 If I have walked with falsehood
Job31:6 (Let me be weighed in an even balance
Job31:7 If my step have turned out of the way
Job31:8 Let me sow
Job31:9 If my heart have been enticed unto a woman
Job31:10 Let my wife grind for another
Job31:11 For this is an infamy; yea
Job31:12 For it is a fire that consumeth to destruction
Job31:13 If I have despised the cause of my bondman or of my bondmaid
Job31:14 What then should I do when God riseth up? and if he visited
Job31:15 Did not he that made me in the womb make him? and did not One fashion us in the womb?
Job31:16 If I have withheld the poor from [their] desire
Job31:17 Or have eaten my morsel alone
Job31:18 (For from my youth he grew up with me as with a father
Job31:19 If I have seen any perishing for want of clothing
Job31:20 If his loins have not blessed me
Job31:21 If I have lifted up my hand against an orphan
Job31:22 [Then] let my shoulder fall from the shoulder-blade
Job31:23 For calamity from God was a terror to me
Job31:24 If I have made gold my hope
Job31:25 If I rejoiced because my wealth was great
Job31:26 If I beheld the sun when it shone
Job31:27 And my heart have been secretly enticed
Job31:28 This also would be an iniquity for the judge
Job31:29 If I rejoiced at the destruction of him that hated me
Job31:30 (Neither have I suffered my mouth to sin by asking his life with a curse;)
Job31:31 If the men of my tent said not
Job31:32 The stranger did not lodge without; I opened my doors to the pathway.
Job31:33 If I covered my transgressions as Adam
Job31:34 Because I feared the great multitude
Job31:35 Oh that I had one to hear me! Behold my signature: let the Almighty answer me! And let mine opponent write an accusation!
Job31:36 Would I not take it upon my shoulder? I would bind it on to me [as] a crown;
Job31:37 I would declare unto him the number of my steps; as a prince would I come near to him.
Job31:38 If my land cry out against me
Job31:39 If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money
Job31:40 Let thistles grow instead of wheat
Job32:1 And these three men ceased to answer Job
Job32:2 Then was kindled the anger of Elihu the son of Barachel
Job32:3 and against his three friends was his anger kindled
Job32:4 But Elihu had waited till Job had finished speaking
Job32:5 And Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of the three men
Job32:6 And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said
Job32:7 I said
Job32:8 But there is a spirit which is in man; and the breath of the Almighty giveth them understanding.
Job32:9 It is not the great that are wise; neither do the aged understand judgment.
Job32:10 Therefore I say
Job32:11 Lo
Job32:12 Yea
Job32:13 That ye may not say
Job32:14 Now he hath not directed [his] words against me; and I will not answer him with your speeches.
Job32:15 They were amazed
Job32:16 And I waited
Job32:17 I will answer
Job32:18 For I am full of matter
Job32:19 Behold
Job32:20 I will speak
Job32:21 Let me not
Job32:22 For I know not how to flatter; my Maker would soon take me away.
Job33:1 Howbeit
Job33:2 Behold now
Job33:3 My words shall be of the uprightness of my heart
Job33:4 The Spirit of God hath made me
Job33:5 If thou canst
Job33:6 Behold
Job33:7 Behold
Job33:8 Surely thou hast spoken in my hearing
Job33:9 I am clean without transgression; I am pure
Job33:10 Lo
Job33:11 He putteth my feet in the stocks
Job33:12 Behold
Job33:13 Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters.
Job33:14 For God speaketh once
Job33:15 In a dream
Job33:16 Then he openeth men's ears
Job33:17 That he may withdraw man [from his] work
Job33:18 He keepeth back his soul from the pit
Job33:19 He is chastened also with pain upon his bed
Job33:20 And his life abhorreth bread
Job33:21 His flesh is consumed away from view
Job33:22 And his soul draweth near to the pit
Job33:23 If there be a messenger with him
Job33:24 Then he will be gracious unto him
Job33:25 His flesh shall be fresher than in childhood; he shall return to the days of his youth.
Job33:26 He shall pray unto God
Job33:27 He will sing before men
Job33:28 He hath delivered my soul from going into the pit
Job33:29 Lo
Job33:30 To bring back his soul from the pit
Job33:31 Mark well
Job33:32 If thou hast anything to say
Job33:33 If not
Job34:1 Moreover Elihu answered and said
Job34:2 Hear my words
Job34:3 For the ear trieth words
Job34:4 Let us choose for ourselves what is right; let us know among ourselves what is good!
Job34:5 For Job hath said
Job34:6 Should I lie against my right? My wound is incurable without transgression.
Job34:7 What man is like Job? he drinketh up scorning like water
Job34:8 And goeth in company with workers of iniquity
Job34:9 For he hath said
Job34:10 Therefore hearken unto me
Job34:11 For a man's work will he render to him
Job34:12 Yea
Job34:13 Who hath entrusted to him the earth? and who hath disposed the whole world?
Job34:14 If he only thought of himself
Job34:15 All flesh would expire together
Job34:16 If now [thou hast] understanding
Job34:17 Should he that hateth right indeed govern? and wilt thou condemn the All-just?
Job34:18 Shall one say to a king
Job34:19 [How then to him] that accepteth not the persons of princes
Job34:20 In a moment they die
Job34:21 For his eyes are upon the ways of man
Job34:22 There is no darkness
Job34:23 For he doth not long consider a man
Job34:24 He breaketh in pieces mighty men without inquiry
Job34:25 Since he knoweth their actions; and he overthroweth [them] in the night
Job34:26 He striketh them as wicked men in the open sight of others
Job34:27 Because they have turned back from him
Job34:28 So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him
Job34:29 When he giveth quietness
Job34:30 That the ungodly man reign not
Job34:31 For hath he said unto God
Job34:32 What I see not
Job34:33 Shall he recompense according to thy mind? for thou hast refused [his judgment]; for thou so choosest
Job34:34 Men of understanding will say to me
Job34:35 Job hath spoken without knowledge
Job34:36 Would that Job may be tried unto the end
Job34:37 For he addeth rebellion unto his sin
Job35:1 Moreover Elihu answered and said
Job35:2 Thinkest thou this to be right
Job35:3 For thou hast asked of what profit it is unto thee: what do I gain more than if I had sinned?
Job35:4 I will reply to thee in words
Job35:5 Look unto the heavens and see; and survey the skies: they are higher than thou.
Job35:6 If thou sinnest
Job35:7 If thou be righteous
Job35:8 Thy wickedness [may affect] a man as thou [art]
Job35:9 By reason of the multitude of oppressions they cry; they cry out by reason of the arm of the mighty:
Job35:10 But none saith
Job35:11 Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth
Job35:12 There they cry
Job35:13 Surely God will not hear vanity
Job35:14 Although thou sayest thou dost not see him
Job35:15 But now
Job35:16 For Job hath opened his mouth in vanity
Job36:1 And Elihu proceeded and said
Job36:2 Suffer me a little
Job36:3 I will fetch my knowledge from afar
Job36:4 For truly my words shall be no falsehood: one perfect in knowledge is with thee.
Job36:5 Lo
Job36:6 He saveth not the wicked alive; but he doeth justice to the afflicted.
Job36:7 He withdraweth not his eyes from the righteous
Job36:8 And if
Job36:9 Then he sheweth them their work
Job36:10 And he openeth their ear to discipline
Job36:11 If they hearken and serve [him]
Job36:12 But if they hearken not
Job36:13 But the godless in heart heap up anger; they cry not when he bindeth them:
Job36:14 Their soul dieth in youth
Job36:15 But he delivereth the afflicted in his affliction
Job36:16 Even so would he have allured thee out of the jaws of distress into a broad place
Job36:17 But thou art full of the judgments of the wicked: judgment and justice take hold [on thee].
Job36:18 Because there is wrath
Job36:19 Will he esteem thy riches? Not gold
Job36:20 Desire not the night
Job36:21 Take heed
Job36:22 Lo
Job36:23 Who hath appointed him his way? or who hath said
Job36:24 Remember that thou magnify his work
Job36:25 All men look at it; man beholdeth [it] afar off.
Job36:26 Lo
Job36:27 For he draweth up the drops of water: they distil in rain from the vapour which he formeth
Job36:28 Which the skies pour down [and] drop upon man abundantly.
Job36:29 But can any understand the spreadings of the clouds
Job36:30 Lo
Job36:31 For with them he judgeth the peoples; he giveth food in abundance.
Job36:32 [His] hands he covereth with lightning
Job36:33 His thundering declareth concerning him; the cattle even
Job37:1 Aye
Job37:2 Hear attentively the roar of his voice
Job37:3 He sendeth it forth under the whole heaven
Job37:4 After it a voice roareth: he thundereth with the voice of his excellency
Job37:5 God thundereth marvellously with his voice
Job37:6 For he saith to the snow
Job37:7 He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work.
Job37:8 And the wild beast goeth into its lair
Job37:9 From the chamber [of the south] cometh the whirlwind; and cold from the winds of the north.
Job37:10 By the breath of God ice is given; and the breadth of the waters is straitened.
Job37:11 Also with plentiful moisture he loadeth the thick clouds
Job37:12 And they are turned every way by his guidance
Job37:13 Whether he cause it to come as a rod
Job37:14 Hearken unto this
Job37:15 Dost thou know how God hath disposed them
Job37:16 Dost thou know about the balancings of the clouds
Job37:17 How thy garments become warm when he quieteth the earth by the south wind?
Job37:18 Hast thou with him spread out the sky
Job37:19 Teach us what we shall say unto him! We cannot order [our words] by reason of darkness.
Job37:20 Shall it be told him if I would speak? if a man [so] say
Job37:21 And now [men] see not the light as it gleameth
Job37:22 From the north cometh gold; with God is terrible majesty.
Job37:23 The Almighty
Job37:24 Men do therefore fear him: he respecteth not any that are wise of heart.
Job38:1 And Jehovah answered Job out of the whirlwind and said
Job38:2 Who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Gird up now thy loins like a man; and I will demand of thee
Job38:4 Where wast thou when I founded the earth? Declare
Job38:5 Who set the measures thereof if thou knowest? or who stretched a line upon it?
Job38:6 Whereupon were the foundations thereof sunken? or who laid its corner-stone
Job38:7 When the morning stars sang together
Job38:8 And who shut up the sea with doors
Job38:9 When I made the cloud its garment
Job38:10 When I cut out for it my boundary
Job38:11 And said
Job38:12 Hast thou since thy days commanded the morning? hast thou caused the dawn to know its place
Job38:13 That it might take hold of the ends of the earth
Job38:14 It is changed like the signet-clay; and [all things] stand forth as in a garment:
Job38:15 And from the wicked their light is withholden
Job38:16 Hast thou entered as far as the springs of the sea? and hast thou walked in the recesses of the deep?
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed unto thee? and hast thou seen the gates of the shadow of death?
Job38:18 Hath thine understanding compassed the breadths of the earth? Declare if thou knowest it all.
Job38:19 Where is the way to where light dwelleth? and the darkness
Job38:20 That thou shouldest take it to its bound
Job38:21 Thou knowest
Job38:22 Hast thou entered into the storehouses of the snow
Job38:23 Which I have reserved for the time of distress
Job38:24 By what way is the light parted
Job38:25 Who hath divided a channel for the rain-flood
Job38:26 To cause it to rain on the earth
Job38:27 To satisfy the desolate and waste [ground]
Job38:28 Hath the rain a father? or who begetteth the drops of dew?
Job38:29 Out of whose womb cometh the ice? and the hoary frost of heaven
Job38:30 When the waters lie hidden as in stone
Job38:31 Canst thou fasten the bands of the Pleiades
Job38:32 Dost thou bring forth the constellations each in its season? or dost thou guide the Bear with her sons?
Job38:33 Knowest thou the ordinances of the heavens? dost thou determine their rule over the earth?
Job38:34 Dost thou lift up thy voice to the clouds
Job38:35 Dost thou send forth lightnings that they may go
Job38:36 Who hath put wisdom in the inward parts? or who hath given understanding to the mind?
Job38:37 Who numbereth the clouds with wisdom? or who poureth out the bottles of the heavens
Job38:38 When the dust runneth as into a molten mass
Job38:39 Dost thou hunt the prey for the lioness
Job38:40 When they crouch in [their] dens
Job38:41 Who provideth for the raven his food
Job39:1 Knowest thou the time when the wild goats of the rock bring forth? dost thou mark the calving of the hinds?
Job39:2 Dost thou number the months that they fulfil? and knowest thou the time when they bring forth?
Job39:3 They bow themselves
Job39:4 Their young ones become strong
Job39:5 Who hath sent out the wild ass free? and who hath loosed the bands of the onager
Job39:6 Whose house I made the wilderness
Job39:7 He laugheth at the tumult of the city
Job39:8 The range of the mountains is his pasture
Job39:9 Will the buffalo be willing to serve thee
Job39:10 Canst thou bind the buffalo with his cord in the furrow? or will he harrow the valleys after thee?
Job39:11 Wilt thou put confidence in him
Job39:12 Wilt thou trust him to bring home thy seed
Job39:13 The wing of the ostrich beats joyously But is it the stork's pinion and plumage?
Job39:14 For she leaveth her eggs to the earth
Job39:15 And forgetteth that the foot may crush them
Job39:16 She is hardened against her young ones
Job39:17 For God hath deprived her of wisdom
Job39:18 What time she lasheth herself on high
Job39:19 Hast thou given strength to the horse? hast thou clothed his neck with the quivering mane?
Job39:20 Dost thou make him to leap as a locust? His majestic snorting is terrible.
Job39:21 He paweth in the valley
Job39:22 He laugheth at fear
Job39:23 The quiver rattleth upon him
Job39:24 He swalloweth the ground with fierceness and rage
Job39:25 At the noise of the trumpets he saith
Job39:26 Doth the hawk fly by thine intelligence
Job39:27 Doth the eagle mount up at thy command
Job39:28 He inhabiteth the rock and maketh his dwelling on the point of the cliff
Job39:29 From thence he spieth out the prey
Job39:30 And his young ones suck up blood; and where the slain are
Job40:1 And Jehovah answered Job and said
Job40:2 Shall he that will contend with the Almighty instruct [him]? he that reproveth God
Job40:3 And Job answered Jehovah and said
Job40:4 Behold
Job40:5 Once have I spoken
Job40:6 And Jehovah answered Job out of the whirlwind and said
Job40:7 Gird up now thy loins like a man: I will demand of thee
Job40:8 Wilt thou also annul my judgment? wilt thou condemn me that thou mayest be righteous?
Job40:9 Hast thou an arm like God? or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?
Job40:10 Deck thyself now with glory and excellency
Job40:11 Cast abroad the ragings of thine anger
Job40:12 Look on every one that is proud
Job40:13 Hide them in the dust together; bind their faces in secret.
Job40:14 Then will I also praise thee
Job40:15 See now the behemoth
Job40:16 Behold now
Job40:17 He bendeth his tail like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are woven together.
Job40:18 His bones are tubes of bronze
Job40:19 He is the chief of God's ways: he that made him gave him his sword.
Job40:20 For the mountains bring him forth food
Job40:21 He lieth under lotus-bushes
Job40:22 Lotus-bushes cover him with their shade; the willows of the brook surround him.
Job40:23 Lo
Job40:24 Shall he be taken in front? will they pierce through [his] nose in the trap?
Job41:1 Wilt thou draw out the leviathan with the hook
Job41:2 Wilt thou put a rush-rope into his nose
Job41:3 Will he make many supplications unto thee? or will he speak softly unto thee?
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with thee? wilt thou take him as a bondman for ever?
Job41:5 Wilt thou play with him as with a bird
Job41:6 Shall partners make traffic of him
Job41:7 Wilt thou fill his skin with darts
Job41:8 Lay thy hand upon him; remember the battle
Job41:9 Lo
Job41:10 None is so bold as to stir him up; and who is he that will stand before me?
Job41:11 Who hath first given to me
Job41:12 I will not be silent as to his parts
Job41:13 Who can uncover the surface of his garment? who can come within his double jaws?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of his face? Round about his teeth is terror.
Job41:15 The rows of his shields are a pride
Job41:16 One is so near to another that no air can come between them;
Job41:17 They are joined each to its fellow; they stick together
Job41:18 His sneezings flash light
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go forth flames; sparks of fire leap out:
Job41:20 Out of his nostrils goeth smoke
Job41:21 His breath kindleth coals
Job41:22 In his neck lodgeth strength
Job41:23 The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are fused upon him
Job41:24 His heart is firm as a stone
Job41:25 When he raiseth himself up
Job41:26 If any reach him with a sword
Job41:27 He esteemeth iron as straw
Job41:28 The arrow will not make him flee; slingstones are turned with him into stubble.
Job41:29 Clubs are counted as stubble; he laugheth at the shaking of a javelin.
Job41:30 His under parts are sharp potsherds: he spreadeth a threshing-sledge upon the mire.
Job41:31 He maketh the deep to boil like a pot; he maketh the sea like a pot of ointment;
Job41:32 He maketh the path to shine after him: one would think the deep to be hoary.
Job41:33 Upon earth there is not his like
Job41:34 He beholdeth all high things; he is king over all the proud beasts.
Job42:1 And Job answered Jehovah and said
Job42:2 I know that thou canst do everything
Job42:3 Who is he that obscureth counsel without knowledge? therefore have I uttered what I did not understand; things too wonderful for me
Job42:4 Hear
Job42:5 I had heard of thee by the hearing of the ear
Job42:6 Wherefore I abhor [myself]
Job42:7 And it came to pass after Jehovah had spoken these words to Job
Job42:8 And now
Job42:9 Then Eliphaz the Temanite
Job42:10 And Jehovah turned the captivity of Job
Job42:11 And all his brethren
Job42:12 And Jehovah blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning; and he had fourteen thousand sheep
Job42:13 And he had seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 And he called the name of the first
Job42:15 And in all the land were no women found [so] fair as the daughters of Job; and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.
Job42:16 And Job lived after this a hundred and forty years
Job42:17 And Job died

Psalms1:5 Therefore the wicked shall not stand in the judgment

Psalms18:1 {To the chief Musician. [A Psalm] of David
Psalms18:2 Jehovah is my rock
Psalms18:3 I will call upon Jehovah
Psalms18:4 The bands of death encompassed me
Psalms18:5 The bands of Sheol surrounded me
Psalms18:6 In my distress I called upon Jehovah
Psalms18:7 Then the earth shook and quaked
Psalms18:8 There went up a smoke out of his nostrils
Psalms18:9 And he bowed the heavens
Psalms18:10 And he rode upon a cherub and did fly; yea
Psalms18:11 He made darkness his secret place
Psalms18:12 From the brightness before him his thick clouds passed forth: hail and coals of fire.
Psalms18:13 And Jehovah thundered in the heavens
Psalms18:14 And he sent his arrows
Psalms18:15 And the beds of the waters were seen
Psalms18:16 He reached forth from above
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy
Psalms18:18 They encountered me in the day of my calamity
Psalms18:19 And he brought me forth into a large place; he delivered me
Psalms18:20 Jehovah hath rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of Jehovah
Psalms18:22 For all his ordinances were before me
Psalms18:23 And I was upright with him
Psalms18:24 And Jehovah hath recompensed me according to my righteousness
Psalms18:25 With the gracious thou dost shew thyself gracious; with the upright man thou dost shew thyself upright;
Psalms18:26 With the pure thou dost shew thyself pure; and with the perverse thou dost shew thyself contrary.
Psalms18:27 For it is thou that savest the afflicted people; but the haughty eyes wilt thou bring down.
Psalms18:28 For it is thou that makest my lamp to shine: Jehovah my God enlighteneth my darkness.
Psalms18:29 For by thee I have run through a troop; and by my God have I leaped over a wall.
Psalms18:30 As for God
Psalms18:31 For who is God save Jehovah? and who is a rock if not our God?
Psalms18:32 The God who girdeth me with strength
Psalms18:33 Who maketh my feet like hinds' [feet]
Psalms18:34 Who teacheth my hands to war
Psalms18:35 And thou didst give me the shield of thy salvation
Psalms18:36 Thou didst enlarge my steps under me
Psalms18:37 I pursued mine enemies
Psalms18:38 I crushed them
Psalms18:39 And thou girdedst me with strength to battle; thou didst subdue under me those that rose up against me.
Psalms18:40 And mine enemies didst thou make to turn their backs unto me
Psalms18:41 They cried
Psalms18:42 And I did beat them small as dust before the wind; I did cast them out as the mire of the streets.
Psalms18:43 Thou hast delivered me from the strivings of the people; thou hast made me the head of the nations: a people I knew not doth serve me.
Psalms18:44 At the hearing of the ear
Psalms18:45 Strangers have faded away
Psalms18:46 Jehovah liveth; and blessed be my rock; and exalted be the God of my salvation
Psalms18:47 The God who hath avenged me
Psalms18:48 Who hath delivered me from mine enemies: yea
Psalms18:49 Therefore will I give thanks to thee
Psalms18:50 [It is he] who giveth great deliverances to his king

Psalms22:14 I am poured out like water
Psalms22:15 My strength is dried up like a potsherd

Psalms22:18 They part my garments among them
Psalms22:19 But thou

Psalms29:10 Jehovah sitteth upon the flood; yea
Psalms29:11 Jehovah will give strength unto his people; Jehovah will bless his people with peace.

Psalms30:13 ***
Psalms31:1 {To the chief Musician. A Psalm of David.} In thee
Psalms31:2 Incline thine ear to me
Psalms31:3 For thou art my rock and my fortress; and
Psalms31:4 Draw me out of the net that they have hidden for me; for thou art my strength.
Psalms31:5 Into thy hand I commit my spirit: thou hast redeemed me
Psalms31:6 I have hated them that observe lying vanities; and as for me
Psalms31:7 I will be glad and rejoice in thy loving-kindness
Psalms31:8 And hast not shut me up into the hand of the enemy: thou hast set my feet in a large place.
Psalms31:9 Be gracious unto me
Psalms31:10 For my life is spent with sorrow
Psalms31:11 More than to all mine oppressors
Psalms31:12 I am forgotten in [their] heart as a dead man; I am become like a broken vessel.
Psalms31:13 For I have heard the slander of many terror on every side when they take counsel together against me: they plot to take away my life.
Psalms31:14 But I confided in thee
Psalms31:15 My times are in thy hand: deliver me from the hand of mine enemies
Psalms31:16 Make thy face to shine upon thy servant; save me in thy loving-kindness.
Psalms31:17 Jehovah
Psalms31:18 Let the lying lips become dumb
Psalms31:19 [Oh] how great is thy goodness
Psalms31:20 Thou keepest them concealed in the secret of thy presence from the conspiracies of man; thou hidest them in a pavilion from the strife of tongues.
Psalms31:21 Blessed be Jehovah; for he hath shewn me wondrously his loving-kindness in a strong city.
Psalms31:22 As for me
Psalms31:23 Love Jehovah
Psalms31:24 Be strong
Psalms32:1 {Of David. Instruction.} Blessed is he [whose] transgression is forgiven
Psalms32:2 Blessed is the man unto whom Jehovah reckoneth not iniquity
Psalms32:3 When I kept silence
Psalms32:4 For day and night thy hand was heavy upon me; my moisture was turned into the drought of summer. Selah.
Psalms32:5 I acknowledged my sin unto thee
Psalms32:6 For this shall every one that is godly pray unto thee at a time when thou mayest be found: surely in the floods of great waters they will not reach him.
Psalms32:7 Thou art a hiding-place for me; thou preservest me from trouble; thou dost encompass me with songs of deliverance. Selah.
Psalms32:8 I will instruct thee and teach thee the way in which thou shalt go; I will counsel [thee] with mine eye upon thee.
Psalms32:9 Be ye not as a horse
Psalms32:10 Many sorrows hath the wicked; but he that confideth in Jehovah
Psalms32:11 Rejoice in Jehovah
Psalms33:1 Exult
Psalms33:2 Give thanks unto Jehovah with the harp; sing psalms unto him with the ten-stringed lute.
Psalms33:3 Sing unto him a new song; play skilfully with a loud sound.
Psalms33:4 For the word of Jehovah is right
Psalms33:5 He loveth righteousness and judgment: the earth is full of the loving-kindness of Jehovah.
Psalms33:6 By the word of Jehovah were the heavens made
Psalms33:7 He gathereth the waters of the sea together as a heap; he layeth up the deeps in storehouses.
Psalms33:8 Let all the earth fear Jehovah; let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.
Psalms33:9 For *he* spoke
Psalms33:10 Jehovah frustrateth the counsel of the nations; he maketh the thoughts of the peoples of none effect.
Psalms33:11 The counsel of Jehovah standeth for ever
Psalms33:12 Blessed is the nation whose God is Jehovah
Psalms33:13 Jehovah looketh from the heavens; he beholdeth all the sons of men:
Psalms33:14 From the place of his habitation he looketh forth upon all the inhabitants of the earth;
Psalms33:15 He who fashioneth the hearts of them all
Psalms33:16 The king is not saved by the multitude of [his] forces; a mighty man is not delivered by much strength.
Psalms33:17 The horse is a vain thing for safety; neither doth he deliver by his great power.
Psalms33:18 Behold
Psalms33:19 To deliver their soul from death
Psalms33:20 Our soul waiteth for Jehovah: he is our help and our shield.
Psalms33:21 For in him shall our heart rejoice
Psalms33:22 Let thy loving-kindness

Psalms34:3 Magnify Jehovah with me
Psalms34:4 I sought Jehovah
Psalms34:5 They looked unto him
Psalms34:6 This afflicted one called

Psalms34:13 Keep thy tongue from evil

Psalms34:20 He keepeth all his bones; not one of them is broken.
Psalms34:21 Evil shall destroy the wicked; and they that hate the righteous shall bear their guilt.

Psalms35:1 {[A Psalm] of David.} Strive
Psalms35:2 Take hold of shield and buckler
Psalms35:3 And draw out the spear
Psalms35:4 Let them be put to shame and confounded that seek after my life; let them be turned backward and brought to confusion that devise my hurt:
Psalms35:5 Let them be as chaff before the wind
Psalms35:6 Let their way be dark and slippery
Psalms35:7 For without cause have they hidden for me their net [in] a pit; without cause they have digged [it] for my soul.
Psalms35:8 Let destruction come upon him unawares
Psalms35:9 And my soul shall be joyful in Jehovah; it shall rejoice in his salvation.
Psalms35:10 All my bones shall say
Psalms35:11 Unrighteous witnesses rise up; they lay to my charge things which I know not.
Psalms35:12 They reward me evil for good
Psalms35:13 But as for me
Psalms35:14 I behaved myself as though [he had been] a friend
Psalms35:15 But at my halting they rejoiced
Psalms35:16 With profane jesters for bread
Psalms35:17 Lord
Psalms35:18 I will give thee thanks in the great congregation; I will praise thee among much people.
Psalms35:19 Let not them that are wrongfully mine enemies rejoice over me; let them not wink with the eye that hate me without cause.
Psalms35:20 For they speak not peace; and they devise deceitful words against the quiet in the land.
Psalms35:21 And they opened their mouth wide against me; they said
Psalms35:22 Thou hast seen [it]
Psalms35:23 Stir up thyself
Psalms35:24 Judge me
Psalms35:25 Let them not say in their heart
Psalms35:26 Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine adversity; let them be clothed with shame and dishonour that magnify themselves against me.
Psalms35:27 Let them exult and rejoice that delight in my righteousness; and let them say continually
Psalms35:28 And my tongue shall talk of thy righteousness

Psalms36:1 {To the chief Musician. [A Psalm] of the servant of Jehovah; of David.} The transgression of the wicked uttereth within my heart
Psalms36:2 For he flattereth himself in his own eyes
Psalms36:3 The words of his mouth are wickedness and deceit: he hath left off to be wise
Psalms36:4 He deviseth wickedness up on his bed; he setteth himself in a way that is not good: he abhorreth not evil.
Psalms36:5 Jehovah
Psalms36:6 Thy righteousness is like the high mountains; thy judgments are a great deep: thou
Psalms36:7 How precious is thy loving-kindness
Psalms36:8 They shall be abundantly satisfied with the fatness of thy house; and thou wilt make them drink of the river of thy pleasures.
Psalms36:9 For with thee is the fountain of life: in thy light shall we see light.
Psalms36:10 Continue thy loving-kindness unto them that know thee
Psalms36:11 Let not the foot of pride come against me
Psalms36:12 There are the workers of iniquity fallen: they are cast down
Psalms37:1 {[A Psalm] of David.} Fret not thyself because of evil-doers
Psalms37:2 for they shall soon be cut down like the grass
Psalms37:3 Confide in Jehovah
Psalms37:4 and delight thyself in Jehovah

Psalms37:6 and he will bring forth thy righteousness as the light
Psalms37:7 Rest in Jehovah
Psalms37:8 Cease from anger
Psalms37:9 For evil-doers shall be cut off; but those that wait on Jehovah
Psalms37:10 For yet a little while
Psalms37:11 But the meek shall possess the land
Psalms37:12 The wicked plotteth against the righteous
Psalms37:13 The Lord laugheth at him; for he seeth that his day is coming.
Psalms37:14 The wicked have drawn out the sword
Psalms37:15 their sword shall enter into their own heart
Psalms37:16 The little that the righteous hath is better than the abundance of many wicked;
Psalms37:17 for the arms of the wicked shall be broken
Psalms37:18 Jehovah knoweth the days of the perfect; and their inheritance shall be for ever:
Psalms37:19 they shall not be ashamed in the time of evil
Psalms37:20 For the wicked shall perish
Psalms37:21 The wicked borroweth
Psalms37:22 for those blessed of him shall possess the land
Psalms37:23 The steps of a man are established by Jehovah
Psalms37:24 though he fall
Psalms37:25 I have been young
Psalms37:26 all the day he is gracious and lendeth
Psalms37:27 Depart from evil
Psalms37:28 for Jehovah loveth judgment
Psalms37:29 The righteous shall possess the land
Psalms37:30 The mouth of the righteous proffereth wisdom
Psalms37:31 the law of his God is in his heart; his goings shall not slide.
Psalms37:32 The wicked watcheth the righteous
Psalms37:33 Jehovah will not leave him in his hand
Psalms37:34 Wait for Jehovah
Psalms37:35 I have seen the wicked in great power
Psalms37:36 but he passed away
Psalms37:37 Mark the perfect
Psalms37:38 but the transgressors shall be destroyed together; the future of the wicked shall be cut off.

Psalms38:6 I am depressed; I am bowed down beyond measure; I go mourning all the day.

Psalms44:1 {To the chief Musician. Of the sons of Korah. An instruction.} O God
Psalms44:2 Thou
Psalms44:3 For not by their own sword did they take possession of the land
Psalms44:4 Thou thyself art my king
Psalms44:5 Through thee will we push down our adversaries; through thy name will we tread them under that rise up against us.
Psalms44:6 For I will not put confidence in my bow
Psalms44:7 For thou hast saved us from our adversaries
Psalms44:8 In God will we boast all the day
Psalms44:9 But thou hast cast off
Psalms44:10 Thou hast made us to turn back from the adversary
Psalms44:11 Thou hast given us over like sheep [appointed] for meat
Psalms44:12 Thou hast sold thy people for nought
Psalms44:13 Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours
Psalms44:14 Thou makest us a byword among the nations
Psalms44:15 All the day my confusion is before me
Psalms44:16 Because of the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth; by reason of the enemy and the avenger.
Psalms44:17 All this is come upon us; yet have we not forgotten thee
Psalms44:18 Our heart is not turned back
Psalms44:19 Though thou hast crushed us in the place of jackals
Psalms44:20 If we had forgotten the name of our God
Psalms44:21 Would not God search this out? for he knoweth the secrets of the heart.
Psalms44:22 But for thy sake are we killed all the day long; we are reckoned as sheep for slaughter.
Psalms44:23 Awake
Psalms44:24 Wherefore hidest thou thy face
Psalms44:25 For our soul is bowed down to the dust; our belly cleaveth unto the earth.
Psalms44:26 Rise up for our help
