Psalms45:1 {To the chief Musician. Upon Shoshannim. Of the sons of Korah. An instruction; a song of the Beloved.} My heart is welling forth [with] a good matter: I say what I have composed touching the king. My tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
Psalms45:2 Thou art fairer than the sons of men; grace is poured into thy lips: therefore God hath blessed thee for ever.
Psalms45:3 Gird thy sword upon [thy] thigh
Psalms45:4 And [in] thy splendour ride prosperously
Psalms45:5 Thine arrows are sharp peoples fall under thee in the heart of the king's enemies.
Psalms45:6 Thy throne
Psalms45:7 Thou hast loved righteousness
Psalms45:8 Myrrh and aloes
Psalms45:9 Kings' daughters are among thine honourable women; upon thy right hand doth stand the queen in gold of Ophir.
Psalms45:10 Hearken
Psalms45:11 And the king will desire thy beauty; for he is thy Lord
Psalms45:12 And the daughter of Tyre with a gift
Psalms45:13 All glorious is the king's daughter within; her clothing is of wrought gold:
Psalms45:14 She shall be brought unto the king in raiment of embroidery; the virgins behind her
Psalms45:15 With joy and gladness shall they be brought; they shall enter into the king's palace.
Psalms45:16 Instead of thy fathers shall be thy sons; princes shalt thou make them in all the earth.
Psalms45:17 I will make thy name to be remembered throughout all generations; therefore shall the peoples praise thee for ever and ever.

Psalms49:1 {To the chief Musician. Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm.} Hear this
Psalms49:2 Both men of low and men of high degree
Psalms49:3 My mouth shall speak wisdom
Psalms49:4 I will incline mine ear to a parable
Psalms49:5 Wherefore should I fear in the days of adversity
Psalms49:6 They depend upon their wealth
Psalms49:7 None can by any means redeem his brother
Psalms49:8 (For the redemption of their soul is costly
Psalms49:9 That he should still live perpetually
Psalms49:10 For he seeth that wise men die; all alike
Psalms49:11 Their inward thought is
Psalms49:12 Nevertheless
Psalms49:13 This their way is their folly
Psalms49:14 Like sheep are they laid in Sheol: Death feedeth on them; and the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning; and their comeliness shall be for Sheol to consume
Psalms49:15 But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol: for he will receive me. Selah.

Psalms49:17 For when he dieth

Psalms49:19 It shall go to the generation of his fathers: they shall never see light.

Psalms50:1 {A Psalm. Of Asaph.} God
Psalms50:2 Out of Zion
Psalms50:3 Our God will come
Psalms50:4 He will call to the heavens from above
Psalms50:5 Gather unto me my godly ones
Psalms50:6 And the heavens shall declare his righteousness; for God executeth judgment himself. Selah.
Psalms50:7 Hear
Psalms50:8 I will not reprove thee for thy sacrifices
Psalms50:9 I will take no bullock out of thy house
Psalms50:10 For every beast of the forest is mine
Psalms50:11 I know all the fowl of the mountains
Psalms50:12 If I were hungry
Psalms50:13 Should I eat the flesh of bulls
Psalms50:14 Offer unto God thanksgiving
Psalms50:15 And call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver thee
Psalms50:16 But unto the wicked God saith
Psalms50:17 Seeing thou hast hated correction and hast cast my words behind thee?
Psalms50:18 When thou sawest a thief
Psalms50:19 Thou lettest thy mouth loose to evil
Psalms50:20 Thou sittest [and] speakest against thy brother
Psalms50:21 These [things] hast thou done
Psalms50:22 Now consider this
Psalms50:23 Whoso offereth praise glorifieth me; and to him that ordereth [his] way will I shew the salvation of God.

Psalms53:4 Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge

Psalms53:6 Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! When God turneth again the captivity of his people

Psalms55:4 My heart is writhing within me

Psalms55:6 And I said
Psalms55:7 Behold
Psalms55:8 I would hasten my escape from the stormy wind
Psalms55:9 Swallow [them] up
Psalms55:10 Day and night they go about it upon the walls thereof; and iniquity and mischief are in the midst of it.
Psalms55:11 Perversities are in the midst thereof; and oppression and deceit depart not from its streets.
Psalms55:12 For it is not an enemy that hath reproached me then could I have borne it; neither is it he that hateth me that hath magnified [himself] against me then would I have hidden myself from him;
Psalms55:13 But it was thou
Psalms55:14 We who held sweet intercourse together. To the house of God we walked amid the throng.
Psalms55:15 Let death seize upon them
Psalms55:16 As for me
Psalms55:17 Evening

Psalms56:1 {To the chief Musician. On Jonathelem-rechokim. Of David. Michtam; when the Philistines took him in Gath.} Be gracious unto me
Psalms56:2 Mine enemies would swallow [me] up all the day long; for they are many that fight against me haughtily.
Psalms56:3 In the day that I am afraid
Psalms56:4 In God will I praise his word
Psalms56:5 All the day long they wrest my words; all their thoughts are against me for evil.
Psalms56:6 They gather themselves together
Psalms56:7 Shall they escape by iniquity? In anger cast down the peoples
Psalms56:8 *Thou* countest my wanderings; put my tears into thy bottle: are they not in thy book?
Psalms56:9 Then shall mine enemies return backward in the day when I call: this I know
Psalms56:10 In God will I praise [his] word; in Jehovah will I praise [his] word.
Psalms56:11 In God have I put my confidence: I will not fear; what can man do unto me?
Psalms56:12 Thy vows are upon me
Psalms56:13 For thou hast delivered my soul from death; [wilt thou] not [keep] my feet from falling

Psalms57:4 My soul is in the midst of lions; I lie down [among] them that breathe out flames
Psalms57:5 Be exalted above the heavens
Psalms57:6 They have prepared a net for my steps; my soul was bowed down: they have digged a pit before me; they are fallen into the midst thereof. Selah.

Psalms57:8 Awake
Psalms57:9 I will give thee thanks among the peoples
Psalms57:10 For thy loving-kindness is great unto the heavens

Psalms58:1 {To the chief Musician. 'Destroy not.' Of David. Michtam.} Is righteousness indeed silent? Do ye speak it? Do ye judge with equity
Psalms58:2 Yea
Psalms58:3 The wicked go astray from the womb; they err as soon as they are born
Psalms58:4 Their poison is like the poison of a serpent: [they are] like the deaf adder which stoppeth her ear;
Psalms58:5 Which doth not hearken to the voice of enchanters
Psalms58:6 O God
Psalms58:7 Let them melt away as waters that flow off; when he aimeth his arrows
Psalms58:8 Let them be as a snail that melteth as it passeth away; [like] the untimely birth of a woman
Psalms58:9 Before your pots feel the thorns
Psalms58:10 The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance; he shall wash his footsteps in the blood of the wicked:
Psalms58:11 And men shall say

Psalms63:1 {A Psalm of David; when he was in the wilderness of Judah.} O God
Psalms63:2 To see thy power and thy glory
Psalms63:3 For thy loving-kindness is better than life: my lips shall praise thee.
Psalms63:4 So will I bless thee while I live; I will lift up my hands in thy name.
Psalms63:5 My soul is satisfied as with marrow and fatness
Psalms63:6 When I remember thee upon my bed
Psalms63:7 For thou hast been my help
Psalms63:8 My soul followeth hard after thee: thy right hand upholdeth me.
Psalms63:9 But those that seek my soul
Psalms63:10 They shall be given over to the power of the sword; they shall be the portion of foxes.
Psalms63:11 But the king shall rejoice in God; every one that sweareth by him shall glory: for the mouth of them that speak lies shall be stopped.
Psalms63:12 ***
Psalms64:1 {To the chief Musician. A Psalm of David.} Hear
Psalms64:2 Hide me from the secret counsel of evil-doers
Psalms64:3 Who have sharpened their tongue like a sword
Psalms64:4 That they may shoot in secret at the perfect: suddenly do they shoot at him
Psalms64:5 They encourage themselves in an evil matter
Psalms64:6 They devise iniquities: We have it ready
Psalms64:7 But God will shoot an arrow at them: suddenly are they wounded;
Psalms64:8 By their own tongue they are made to fall over one another: all that see them shall flee away.
Psalms64:9 And all men shall fear
Psalms64:10 The righteous shall rejoice in Jehovah

Psalms65:1 {To the chief Musician. A Psalm of David: a Song.} Praise waiteth for thee in silence
Psalms65:2 O thou that hearest prayer
Psalms65:3 Iniquities have prevailed against me: our transgressions
Psalms65:4 Blessed is he whom thou choosest and causest to approach: he shall dwell in thy courts. We shall be satisfied with the goodness of thy house
Psalms65:5 By terrible things in righteousness wilt thou answer us
Psalms65:6 Who by his strength established the mountains
Psalms65:7 Who stilleth the raging of the seas
Psalms65:8 And they that dwell in the uttermost parts are afraid at thy tokens; thou makest the outgoings of the morning and evening to rejoice.
Psalms65:9 Thou hast visited the earth
Psalms65:10 Thou dost satiate its furrows
Psalms65:11 Thou crownest the year with thy goodness
Psalms65:12 They drop upon the pastures of the wilderness
Psalms65:13 The meadows are clothed with flocks

Psalms66:2 Sing forth the glory of his name
Psalms66:3 Say unto God
Psalms66:4 All the earth shall worship thee
Psalms66:5 Come and see the works of God: he is terrible in [his] doings toward the children of men.
Psalms66:6 He turned the sea into dry [land]; they went through the river on foot: there did we rejoice in him.
Psalms66:7 He ruleth by his power for ever; his eyes observe the nations: let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah.
Psalms66:8 Bless our God
Psalms66:9 Who hath set our soul in life
Psalms66:10 For thou
Psalms66:11 Thou broughtest us into a net
Psalms66:12 Thou didst cause men to ride over our head; we went through fire and through water: but thou hast brought us out into abundance.
Psalms66:13 I will go into thy house with burnt-offerings; I will perform my vows to thee
Psalms66:14 Which my lips have uttered
Psalms66:15 I will offer up unto thee burnt-offerings of fatted beasts
Psalms66:16 Come
Psalms66:17 I called unto him with my mouth
Psalms66:18 Had I regarded iniquity in my heart
Psalms66:19 But God hath heard; he hath attended to the voice of my prayer.
Psalms66:20 Blessed be God

Psalms69:1 {To the chief Musician. Upon Shoshannim. [A Psalm] of David.} Save me
Psalms69:2 I sink in deep mire
Psalms69:3 I am weary with my crying
Psalms69:4 They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head; they that would destroy me
Psalms69:5 Thou
Psalms69:6 Let not them that wait on thee
Psalms69:7 Because for thy sake I have borne reproach; confusion hath covered my face.
Psalms69:8 I am become a stranger unto my brethren
Psalms69:9 For the zeal of thy house hath devoured me
Psalms69:10 And I wept
Psalms69:11 And I made sackcloth my garment: then I became a proverb to them.
Psalms69:12 They that sit in the gate talk of me
Psalms69:13 But as for me
Psalms69:14 Deliver me out of the mire
Psalms69:15 Let not the flood of waters overflow me
Psalms69:16 Answer me
Psalms69:17 And hide not thy face from thy servant
Psalms69:18 Draw nigh unto my soul
Psalms69:19 *Thou* knowest my reproach
Psalms69:20 Reproach hath broken my heart
Psalms69:21 Yea
Psalms69:22 Let their table become a snare before them
Psalms69:23 Let their eyes be darkened
Psalms69:24 Pour out thine indignation upon them
Psalms69:25 Let their habitation be desolate; let there be no dweller in their tents.
Psalms69:26 For they persecute him whom *thou* hast smitten
Psalms69:27 Add iniquity unto their iniquity
Psalms69:28 Let them be blotted out of the book of life
Psalms69:29 But I am afflicted and sorrowful: let thy salvation
Psalms69:30 I will praise the name of God with a song
Psalms69:31 And it shall please Jehovah more than an ox
Psalms69:32 The meek shall see it
Psalms69:33 For Jehovah heareth the needy
Psalms69:34 Let heavens and earth praise him; the seas
Psalms69:35 For God will save Zion
Psalms69:36 And the seed of his servants shall inherit it

Psalms70:3 Let them turn back because of their shame that say

Psalms70:5 But I am afflicted and needy: make haste unto me

Psalms72:1 {For Solomon.} O God
Psalms72:2 He will judge thy people with righteousness
Psalms72:3 The mountains shall bring peace to the people
Psalms72:4 He will do justice to the afflicted of the people; he will save the children of the needy
Psalms72:5 They shall fear thee as long as sun and moon endure
Psalms72:6 He shall come down like rain on the mown grass
Psalms72:7 In his days shall the righteous flourish
Psalms72:8 And he shall have dominion from sea to sea
Psalms72:9 The dwellers in the desert shall bow before him
Psalms72:10 The kings of Tarshish and of the isles shall render presents; the kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer tribute:
Psalms72:11 Yea
Psalms72:12 For he will deliver the needy who crieth
Psalms72:13 He will have compassion on the poor and needy
Psalms72:14 He will redeem their souls from oppression and violence
Psalms72:15 And he shall live; and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba; and prayer shall be made for him continually: all the day shall he be blessed.
Psalms72:16 There shall be abundance of corn in the earth
Psalms72:17 His name shall endure for ever; his name shall be continued as long as the sun: and [men] shall bless themselves in him; all nations shall call him blessed.
Psalms72:18 Blessed be Jehovah Elohim
Psalms72:19 And blessed be his glorious name for ever! and let the whole earth be filled with his glory! Amen
Psalms72:20 The prayers of David the son of Jesse are ended.
Psalms73:1 {A Psalm of Asaph.} Truly God is good to Israel
Psalms73:2 But as for me
Psalms73:3 For I was envious at the arrogant
Psalms73:4 For they have no pangs in their death
Psalms73:5 They have not the hardships of mankind
Psalms73:6 Therefore pride encompasseth them as a neck-chain
Psalms73:7 Their eyes stand out from fatness
Psalms73:8 They mock and speak wickedly of oppression
Psalms73:9 They set their mouth in the heavens
Psalms73:10 Therefore his people turn hither
Psalms73:11 And they say
Psalms73:12 Behold
Psalms73:13 Truly have I purified my heart in vain
Psalms73:14 For all the day have I been plagued
Psalms73:15 If I said
Psalms73:16 When I thought to be able to know this
Psalms73:17 Until I went into the sanctuaries of God; [then] understood I their end.
Psalms73:18 Truly thou settest them in slippery places
Psalms73:19 How are they suddenly made desolate! they pass away
Psalms73:20 As a dream
Psalms73:21 When my heart was in a ferment
Psalms73:22 Then I was brutish and knew nothing; I was [as] a beast with thee.
Psalms73:23 Nevertheless I am continually with thee: thou hast holden my right hand;
Psalms73:24 Thou wilt guide me by thy counsel
Psalms73:25 Whom have I in the heavens? and there is none upon earth I desire beside thee.
Psalms73:26 My flesh and my heart faileth: God is the rock of my heart and my portion for ever.
Psalms73:27 For behold
Psalms73:28 But as for me
Psalms74:1 {An instruction: of Asaph.} Why
Psalms74:2 Remember thine assembly
Psalms74:3 Lift up thy steps unto the perpetual desolations: everything in the sanctuary hath the enemy destroyed.
Psalms74:4 Thine adversaries roar in the midst of thy place of assembly; they set up their signs [for] signs.
Psalms74:5 [A man] was known as he could lift up axes in the thicket of trees;
Psalms74:6 And now they break down its carved work altogether
Psalms74:7 They have set on fire thy sanctuary
Psalms74:8 They said in their heart
Psalms74:9 We see not our signs; there is no more any prophet
Psalms74:10 How long
Psalms74:11 Why withdrawest thou thy hand
Psalms74:12 But God is my king of old
Psalms74:13 *Thou* didst divide the sea by thy strength; thou didst break the heads of the monsters on the waters:
Psalms74:14 *Thou* didst break in pieces the heads of leviathan
Psalms74:15 *Thou* didst cleave fountain and torrent
Psalms74:16 The day is thine
Psalms74:17 *Thou* hast set all the borders of the earth; summer and winter *thou* didst form them.
Psalms74:18 Remember this
Psalms74:19 Give not up the soul of thy turtle-dove unto the wild beast; forget not the troop of thine afflicted for ever.
Psalms74:20 Have respect unto the covenant; for the dark places of the earth are full of the dwellings of violence.
Psalms74:21 Oh let not the oppressed one return ashamed; let the afflicted and needy praise thy name.
Psalms74:22 Rise up
Psalms74:23 Forget not the voice of thine adversaries: the tumult of those that rise up against thee ascendeth continually.
Psalms74:24 ***
Psalms75:1 {To the chief Musician. 'Destroy not.' A Psalm of Asaph: a Song.} Unto thee we give thanks
Psalms75:2 When I shall receive the assembly
Psalms75:3 The earth and all the inhabitants thereof are dissolved: I have established its pillars. Selah.
Psalms75:4 I said unto the boastful
Psalms75:5 Lift not up your horn on high; speak not arrogantly with a [stiff] neck.
Psalms75:6 For not from the east nor from the west
Psalms75:7 For God is the judge; he putteth down one and exalteth another.
Psalms75:8 For in the hand of Jehovah there is a cup
Psalms75:9 But as for me
Psalms75:10 And all the horns of the wicked will I cut off; [but] the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.

Psalms76:2 And in Salem is his tent
Psalms76:3 There broke he the flashings of the bow
Psalms76:4 More glorious art thou
Psalms76:5 The stout-hearted are made a spoil
Psalms76:6 At thy rebuke
Psalms76:7 Thou
Psalms76:8 Thou didst cause judgment to be heard from the heavens; the earth feared
Psalms76:9 When God rose up to judgment
Psalms76:10 For the fury of man shall praise thee; the remainder of fury wilt thou gird on thyself.
Psalms76:11 Vow and pay unto Jehovah your God: let all that are round about him bring presents unto him that is to be feared.
Psalms76:12 He cutteth off the spirit of princes; [he] is terrible to the kings of the earth.

Psalms77:1 {To the chief Musician. On Jeduthun. Of Asaph. A Psalm.} My voice is unto God
Psalms77:2 In the day of my trouble
Psalms77:3 I remembered God
Psalms77:4 Thou holdest open mine eyelids; I am full of disquiet and cannot speak.
Psalms77:5 I consider the days of old
Psalms77:6 I remember my song in the night; I muse in mine own heart
Psalms77:7 Will the Lord cast off for ever? and will he be favourable no more?
Psalms77:8 Hath his loving-kindness ceased for ever? hath [his] word come to an end from generation to generation?
Psalms77:9 Hath God forgotten to be gracious? or hath he in anger shut up his tender mercies? Selah.
Psalms77:10 Then said I
Psalms77:11 Will I remember
Psalms77:12 And I will meditate upon all thy work
Psalms77:13 O God
Psalms77:14 Thou art the God that doest wonders; thou hast declared thy strength among the peoples.
Psalms77:15 Thou hast with [thine] arm redeemed thy people
Psalms77:16 The waters saw thee
Psalms77:17 The thick clouds poured out water; the skies sent out a sound
Psalms77:18 The voice of thy thunder was in the whirlwind
Psalms77:19 Thy way is in the sea
Psalms77:20 Thou leddest thy people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
Psalms78:1 {An instruction. Of Asaph.} Give ear
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter riddles from of old
Psalms78:3 Which we have heard and known
Psalms78:4 We will not hide [them] from their sons
Psalms78:5 For he established a testimony in Jacob
Psalms78:6 That the generation to come might know [them]
Psalms78:7 And that they might set their hope in God
Psalms78:8 And might not be as their fathers
Psalms78:9 The sons of Ephraim
Psalms78:10 They kept not the covenant of God
Psalms78:11 And forgot his doings
Psalms78:12 In the sight of their fathers had he done wonders
Psalms78:13 He clave the sea
Psalms78:14 And he led them with a cloud in the daytime
Psalms78:15 He clave rocks in the wilderness
Psalms78:16 And he brought streams out of the rock
Psalms78:17 Yet they still went on sinning against him
Psalms78:18 And they tempted God in their heart
Psalms78:19 And they spoke against God: they said
Psalms78:20 Behold
Psalms78:21 Therefore Jehovah heard
Psalms78:22 Because they believed not in God
Psalms78:23 Though he had commanded the clouds from above
Psalms78:24 And had rained down manna upon them to eat
Psalms78:25 Man did eat the bread of the mighty; he sent them provision to the full.
Psalms78:26 He caused the east wind to rise in the heavens
Psalms78:27 And he rained flesh upon them as dust
Psalms78:28 And he let it fall in the midst of their camp
Psalms78:29 And they did eat
Psalms78:30 They were not alienated from their lust
Psalms78:31 When the anger of God went up against them; and he slew the fattest of them
Psalms78:32 For all this
Psalms78:33 And he consumed their days in vanity
Psalms78:34 When he slew them
Psalms78:35 And they remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 But they flattered him with their mouth
Psalms78:37 For their heart was not firm toward him
Psalms78:38 But he was merciful: he forgave the iniquity
Psalms78:39 And he remembered that they were flesh
Psalms78:40 How oft did they provoke him in the wilderness
Psalms78:41 And they turned again and tempted God
Psalms78:42 They remembered not his hand
Psalms78:43 How he set his signs in Egypt
Psalms78:44 And turned their rivers into blood
Psalms78:45 He sent dog-flies among them
Psalms78:46 And he gave their increase unto the caterpillar
Psalms78:47 He killed their vines with hail
Psalms78:48 And he delivered up their cattle to the hail
Psalms78:49 He cast upon them the fierceness of his anger
Psalms78:50 He made a way for his anger; he spared not their soul from death
Psalms78:51 And he smote all the firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 And he made his own people to go forth like sheep
Psalms78:53 And he led them safely
Psalms78:54 And he brought them to his holy border
Psalms78:55 And he drove out the nations before them
Psalms78:56 But they tempted and provoked God
Psalms78:57 And they drew back and dealt treacherously like their fathers: they turned like a deceitful bow.
Psalms78:58 And they provoked him to anger with their high places
Psalms78:59 God heard
Psalms78:60 And he forsook the tabernacle at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 And gave his strength into captivity
Psalms78:62 And delivered up his people unto the sword
Psalms78:63 The fire consumed their young men
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as one out of sleep
Psalms78:66 And he smote his adversaries in the hinder part
Psalms78:67 And he rejected the tent of Joseph
Psalms78:68 But chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 And he built his sanctuary like the heights
Psalms78:70 And he chose David his servant
Psalms78:71 From following the suckling-ewes
Psalms78:72 And he fed them according to the integrity of his heart

Psalms80:9 Thou preparedst space before it

Psalms80:11 It sent out its boughs unto the sea

Psalms88:9 Mine eye consumeth by reason of affliction. Upon thee
Psalms88:10 Wilt thou do wonders to the dead? shall the shades arise and praise thee? Selah.
Psalms88:11 Shall thy loving-kindness be declared in the grave? thy faithfulness in Destruction?

Psalms88:18 Lover and associate hast thou put far from me: my familiar friends are darkness.

Psalms89:3 I have made a covenant with mine elect
Psalms89:4 Thy seed will I establish for ever
Psalms89:5 And the heavens shall celebrate thy wonders
Psalms89:6 For who in the heaven can be compared to Jehovah? [who] among the sons of the mighty shall be likened to Jehovah?
Psalms89:7 God is greatly to be feared in the council of the saints
Psalms89:8 Jehovah
Psalms89:9 *Thou* rulest the pride of the sea: when its waves arise
Psalms89:10 Thou hast crushed Rahab as one that is slain; thou hast scattered thine enemies with the arm of thy strength.
Psalms89:11 Thine are the heavens
Psalms89:12 The north and the south
Psalms89:13 Thine is the arm of might: strong is thy hand
Psalms89:14 Righteousness and judgment are the foundation of thy throne; loving-kindness and truth go before thy face.
Psalms89:15 Blessed is the people that know the shout of joy: they walk
Psalms89:16 In thy name are they joyful all the day
Psalms89:17 For thou art the glory of their strength; and in thy favour our horn shall be exalted.
Psalms89:18 For Jehovah is our shield
Psalms89:19 Then thou spakest in vision of thy Holy One
Psalms89:20 I have found David my servant; with my holy oil have I anointed him:
Psalms89:21 With whom my hand shall be established; and mine arm shall strengthen him.
Psalms89:22 No enemy shall exact upon him
Psalms89:23 But I will beat down his adversaries before his face
Psalms89:24 And my faithfulness and my loving-kindness shall be with him
Psalms89:25 And I will set his hand in the sea
Psalms89:26 *He* shall call unto me
Psalms89:27 And as to me
Psalms89:28 My loving-kindness will I keep for him for evermore
Psalms89:29 And I will establish his seed for ever
Psalms89:30 If his sons forsake my law
Psalms89:31 If they profane my statutes
Psalms89:32 Then will I visit their transgression with the rod
Psalms89:33 Nevertheless my loving-kindness will I not utterly take from him
Psalms89:34 My covenant will I not profane
Psalms89:35 Once have I sworn by my holiness; I will not lie unto David:
Psalms89:36 His seed shall endure for ever
Psalms89:37 It shall be established for ever as the moon
Psalms89:38 But thou hast rejected and cast off; thou hast been very wroth with thine anointed:
Psalms89:39 Thou hast made void the covenant of thy servant; thou hast profaned his crown to the ground:
Psalms89:40 Thou hast broken down all his hedges; thou hast brought his strongholds to ruin.
Psalms89:41 All that pass by the way plunder him; he is become a reproach to his neighbours.
Psalms89:42 Thou hast exalted the right hand of his oppressors; thou hast made all his enemies to rejoice:
Psalms89:43 Yea
Psalms89:44 Thou hast made his brightness to cease
Psalms89:45 The days of his youth hast thou shortened; thou hast covered him with shame. Selah.

Psalms89:47 Remember
Psalms89:48 What man liveth

Psalms89:51 Wherewith thine enemies

Psalms106:2 Who can utter the mighty acts of Jehovah? [who] can shew forth all his praise?
Psalms106:3 Blessed are they that keep justice
Psalms106:4 Remember me
Psalms106:5 That I may see the prosperity of thy chosen ones
Psalms106:6 We have sinned with our fathers
Psalms106:7 Our fathers in Egypt considered not thy wondrous works; they remembered not the multitude of thy loving-kindnesses; but they rebelled at the sea
Psalms106:8 Yet he saved them for his name's sake
Psalms106:9 And he rebuked the Red Sea
Psalms106:10 And he saved them from the hand of him that hated [them]
Psalms106:11 And the waters covered their oppressors: there was not one of them left.
Psalms106:12 Then believed they his words; they sang his praise.
Psalms106:13 They soon forgot his works; they waited not for his counsel:
Psalms106:14 And they lusted exceedingly in the wilderness
Psalms106:15 Then he gave them their request
Psalms106:16 And they envied Moses in the camp
Psalms106:17 The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan
Psalms106:18 And fire was kindled in their company; a flame burned up the wicked.
Psalms106:19 They made a calf in Horeb
Psalms106:20 And they changed their glory into the similitude of an ox that eateth grass.
Psalms106:21 They forgot God their Saviour
Psalms106:22 Wondrous works in the land of Ham
Psalms106:23 And he said that he would destroy them
Psalms106:24 And they despised the pleasant land; they believed not his word
Psalms106:25 But murmured in their tents: they hearkened not unto the voice of Jehovah.
Psalms106:26 And he lifted up his hand to them
Psalms106:27 And that he would make their seed fall among the nations
Psalms106:28 And they joined themselves unto Baal-Peor
Psalms106:29 And they provoked [him] to anger with their doings; and a plague broke out among them.
Psalms106:30 Then stood up Phinehas and executed judgment
Psalms106:31 And that was reckoned unto him for righteousness
Psalms106:32 And they moved him to wrath at the waters of Meribah
Psalms106:33 For they provoked his spirit
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the peoples
Psalms106:35 But they mingled with the nations
Psalms106:36 And they served their idols; and they were a snare unto them:
Psalms106:37 And they sacrificed their sons and their daughters unto demons
Psalms106:38 And shed innocent blood
Psalms106:39 And they were defiled with their works
Psalms106:40 Then was the anger of Jehovah kindled against his people
Psalms106:41 And he gave them into the hand of the nations; and they that hated them ruled over them:
Psalms106:42 And their enemies oppressed them
Psalms106:43 Often did he deliver them; but as for them they provoked [him] by their counsel
Psalms106:44 But he regarded their distress
Psalms106:45 And he remembered for them his covenant
Psalms106:46 And he caused them to find compassion of all those that had carried them captives.

Psalms109:1 {To the chief Musician. Of David. A Psalm.} O God of my praise
Psalms109:2 For the mouth of the wicked [man] and the mouth of deceit are opened against me: they have spoken against me with a lying tongue
Psalms109:3 And with words of hatred have they encompassed me; and they fight against me without a cause.
Psalms109:4 For my love they are mine adversaries; but I [give myself unto] prayer.
Psalms109:5 And they have rewarded me evil for good
Psalms109:6 Set a wicked [man] over him
Psalms109:7 When he shall be judged
Psalms109:8 Let his days be few
Psalms109:9 Let his sons be fatherless
Psalms109:10 Let his sons be vagabonds and beg
Psalms109:11 Let the usurer cast the net over all that he hath
Psalms109:12 Let there be none to extend kindness unto him
Psalms109:13 Let his posterity be cut off; in the generation following let their name be blotted out:
Psalms109:14 Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered with Jehovah
Psalms109:15 Let them be before Jehovah continually
Psalms109:16 Because he remembered not to shew kindness
Psalms109:17 And he loved cursing; so let it come unto him. And he delighted not in blessing; and let it be far from him.
Psalms109:18 And he clothed himself with cursing like his vestment; so let it come into his bowels like water
Psalms109:19 Let it be unto him as a garment with which he covereth himself
Psalms109:20 Let this be the reward of mine adversaries from Jehovah
Psalms109:21 But do *thou* for me
Psalms109:22 For I am afflicted and needy
Psalms109:23 I am gone like a shadow when it lengtheneth; I am tossed about like the locust;
Psalms109:24 My knees are failing through fasting
Psalms109:25 And I am become a reproach unto them; [when] they look upon me they shake their heads.
Psalms109:26 Help me
Psalms109:27 That they may know that this is thy hand; that *thou*
Psalms109:28 Let *them* curse
Psalms109:29 Let mine adversaries be clothed with confusion
Psalms109:30 I will greatly celebrate Jehovah with my mouth; yea
Psalms109:31 For he standeth at the right hand of the needy

Psalms119:1 ALEPH. Blessed are the perfect in the way
Psalms119:2 Blessed are they that observe his testimonies
Psalms119:3 Who also do no unrighteousness: they walk in his ways.
Psalms119:4 Thou hast enjoined thy precepts
Psalms119:5 Oh that my ways were directed to keep thy statutes!
Psalms119:6 Then shall I not be ashamed
Psalms119:7 I will give thee thanks with uprightness of heart
Psalms119:8 I will keep thy statutes: forsake me not utterly.
Psalms119:9 BETH. Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his path? by taking heed according to thy word.
Psalms119:10 With my whole heart have I sought thee: let me not wander from thy commandments.
Psalms119:11 Thy word have I hid in my heart
Psalms119:12 Blessed art thou
Psalms119:13 With my lips have I declared all the judgments of thy mouth.
Psalms119:14 I have rejoiced in the way of thy testimonies
Psalms119:15 I will meditate upon thy precepts
Psalms119:16 I delight myself in thy statutes; I will not forget thy word.
Psalms119:17 GIMEL. Deal bountifully with thy servant [and] I shall live; and I will keep thy word.
Psalms119:18 Open mine eyes
Psalms119:19 I am a stranger in the land; hide not thy commandments from me.
Psalms119:20 My soul breaketh for longing after thy judgments at all times.
Psalms119:21 Thou hast rebuked the proud [that are] cursed
Psalms119:22 Roll off from me reproach and contempt; for I observe thy testimonies.
Psalms119:23 Princes also did sit [and] talk together against me: thy servant doth meditate in thy statutes.
Psalms119:24 Thy testimonies also are my delight [and] my counsellors.
Psalms119:25 DALETH. My soul cleaveth unto the dust: quicken me according to thy word.
Psalms119:26 I have declared my ways
Psalms119:27 Make me to understand the way of thy precepts
Psalms119:28 My soul melteth for sadness: strengthen me according to thy word.
Psalms119:29 Remove from me the way of falsehood
Psalms119:30 I have chosen the way of faithfulness; thy judgments have I set [before me].
Psalms119:31 I cleave unto thy testimonies; Jehovah
Psalms119:32 I will run the way of thy commandments when thou shalt enlarge my heart.
Psalms119:33 HE. Teach me
Psalms119:34 Give me understanding
Psalms119:35 Make me to walk in the path of thy commandments; for therein do I delight.
Psalms119:36 Incline my heart unto thy testimonies
Psalms119:37 Turn away mine eyes from beholding vanity; quicken me in thy way.
Psalms119:38 Establish thy word unto thy servant
Psalms119:39 Turn away my reproach which I fear; for thy judgments are good.
Psalms119:40 Behold
Psalms119:41 VAU. And let thy loving-kindness come unto me
Psalms119:42 So shall I have wherewith to answer him that reproacheth me; for I confide in thy word.
Psalms119:43 And take not the word of truth utterly out of my mouth; because I have hoped in thy judgments.
Psalms119:44 Then will I keep thy law continually
Psalms119:45 And I will walk at liberty
Psalms119:46 And I will speak of thy testimonies before kings
Psalms119:47 And I will delight myself in thy commandments
Psalms119:48 And I will lift up my hands unto thy commandments
Psalms119:49 ZAIN. Remember the word for thy servant
Psalms119:50 This is my comfort in mine affliction; for thy word hath quickened me.
Psalms119:51 The proud have derided me beyond measure: I have not declined from thy law.
Psalms119:52 I remembered thy judgments of old
Psalms119:53 Burning indignation hath taken hold upon me because of the wicked who forsake thy law.
Psalms119:54 Thy statutes have been my songs in the house of my pilgrimage.
Psalms119:55 I have remembered thy name
Psalms119:56 This I have had

Psalms119:58 I have sought thy favour with [my] whole heart: be gracious unto me according to thy word.
Psalms119:59 I have thought on my ways
Psalms119:60 I have made haste

Psalms119:68 Thou art good

Psalms119:70 Their heart is as fat as grease: as for me
Psalms119:71 It is good for me that I have been afflicted
Psalms119:72 The law of thy mouth is better unto me than thousands of gold and silver.

Psalms119:75 I know

Psalms119:81 CAPH. My soul fainteth for thy salvation; I hope in thy word.
Psalms119:82 Mine eyes fail for thy word
Psalms119:83 For I am become like a bottle in the smoke; I do not forget thy statutes.
Psalms119:84 How many shall be the days of thy servant? when wilt thou execute judgment on them that persecute me?
Psalms119:85 The proud have digged pits for me
Psalms119:86 All thy commandments are faithfulness. They persecute me wrongfully: help thou me.
Psalms119:87 They had almost consumed me upon the earth; but as for me
Psalms119:88 Quicken me according to thy loving-kindness
Psalms119:89 LAMED. For ever
Psalms119:90 Thy faithfulness is from generation to generation: thou hast established the earth
Psalms119:91 By thine ordinances they stand this day; for all things are thy servants.
Psalms119:92 Unless thy law had been my delight
Psalms119:93 I will never forget thy precepts; for by them thou hast quickened me.
Psalms119:94 I am thine
Psalms119:95 The wicked have awaited me to destroy me; [but] I attend unto thy testimonies.
Psalms119:96 I have seen an end of all perfection: thy commandment is exceeding broad.
Psalms119:97 MEM. Oh how I love thy law! it is my meditation all the day.
Psalms119:98 Thy commandments make me wiser than mine enemies; for they are ever with me.
Psalms119:99 I have more understanding than all my teachers; for thy testimonies are my meditation.
Psalms119:100 I understand more than the aged
Psalms119:101 I have refrained my feet from every evil path
Psalms119:102 I have not departed from thy judgments; for it is thou that hast taught me.
Psalms119:103 How sweet are thy words unto my taste! more than honey to my mouth!
Psalms119:104 From thy precepts I get understanding; therefore I hate every false path.
Psalms119:105 NUN. Thy word is a lamp unto my feet
Psalms119:106 I have sworn
Psalms119:107 I am afflicted very much; O Jehovah
Psalms119:108 Accept
Psalms119:109 My life is continually in my hand; but I do not forget thy law.
Psalms119:110 The wicked have laid a snare for me; but I have not wandered from thy precepts.
Psalms119:111 Thy testimonies have I taken as a heritage for ever; for they are the rejoicing of my heart.
Psalms119:112 I have inclined my heart to perform thy statutes for ever
Psalms119:113 SAMECH. The double-minded have I hated; but thy law do I love.
Psalms119:114 Thou art my hiding-place and my shield: I hope in thy word.
Psalms119:115 Depart from me
Psalms119:116 Uphold me according to thy word
Psalms119:117 Hold thou me up
Psalms119:118 Thou hast set at nought all them that wander from thy statutes; for their deceit is falsehood.
Psalms119:119 Thou puttest away all the wicked of the earth [like] dross; therefore I love thy testimonies.
Psalms119:120 My flesh shuddereth for fear of thee; and I am afraid of thy judgments.
Psalms119:121 AIN. I have done judgment and justice: leave me not to mine oppressors.
Psalms119:122 Be surety for thy servant for good; let not the proud oppress me.
Psalms119:123 Mine eyes fail for thy salvation
Psalms119:124 Deal with thy servant according to thy loving-kindness
Psalms119:125 I am thy servant; give me understanding that I may know thy testimonies.
Psalms119:126 It is time for Jehovah to work: they have made void thy law.
Psalms119:127 Therefore I love thy commandments above gold
Psalms119:128 Therefore I regard all [thy] precepts concerning all things to be right: I hate every false path.
Psalms119:129 PE. Thy testimonies are wonderful; therefore doth my soul observe them.
Psalms119:130 The entrance of thy words giveth light
Psalms119:131 I opened my mouth wide and panted; for I longed for thy commandments.
Psalms119:132 Turn unto me
Psalms119:133 Establish my steps in thy word; and let not any iniquity have dominion over me.
Psalms119:134 Deliver me from the oppression of man; and I will keep thy precepts.
Psalms119:135 Make thy face to shine upon thy servant
Psalms119:136 Mine eyes run down with streams of water
Psalms119:137 TZADE. Righteous art thou
Psalms119:138 Thou hast commanded thy testimonies in righteousness and exceeding faithfulness.
Psalms119:139 My zeal destroyeth me
Psalms119:140 Thy word is exceeding pure
Psalms119:141 I am little and despised: thy precepts have I not forgotten.
Psalms119:142 Thy righteousness is an everlasting righteousness
Psalms119:143 Trouble and anguish have taken hold upon me: thy commandments are my delights.
Psalms119:144 The righteousness of thy testimonies is for ever: give me understanding
Psalms119:145 KOPH. I have called with [my] whole heart; answer me
Psalms119:146 I call upon thee; save me
Psalms119:147 I anticipate the morning-dawn and I cry: I hope in thy word.
Psalms119:148 Mine eyes anticipate the night-watches
Psalms119:149 Hear my voice according to thy loving-kindness: O Jehovah
Psalms119:150 They have drawn nigh that follow after mischief: they are far from thy law.
Psalms119:151 Thou
Psalms119:152 From thy testimonies
Psalms119:153 RESH. See mine affliction
Psalms119:154 Plead my cause
Psalms119:155 Salvation is far from the wicked; for they seek not thy statutes.
Psalms119:156 Many are thy tender mercies
Psalms119:157 Many are my persecutors and mine oppressors; I have not declined from thy testimonies.
Psalms119:158 I beheld them that deal treacherously
Psalms119:159 See how I have loved thy precepts: quicken me
Psalms119:160 The sum of thy word is truth
Psalms119:161 SHIN. Princes have persecuted me without a cause; but my heart standeth in awe of thy word.
Psalms119:162 I have joy in thy word
Psalms119:163 I hate and abhor falsehood; thy law do I love.
Psalms119:164 Seven times a day do I praise thee because of thy righteous judgments.
Psalms119:165 Great peace have they that love thy law
Psalms119:166 I have hoped for thy salvation
Psalms119:167 My soul hath kept thy testimonies
Psalms119:168 I have kept thy precepts and thy testimonies; for all my ways are before thee.
Psalms119:169 TAU. Let my cry come near before thee
Psalms119:170 Let my supplication come before thee: deliver me according to thy word.
Psalms119:171 My lips shall pour forth praise when thou hast taught me thy statutes.
Psalms119:172 My tongue shall speak aloud of thy word; for all thy commandments are righteousness.
Psalms119:173 Let thy hand be for my help; for I have chosen thy precepts.
Psalms119:174 I have longed for thy salvation
Psalms119:175 Let my soul live
Psalms119:176 I have gone astray like a lost sheep: seek thy servant; for I have not forgotten thy commandments.

Psalms139:2 *Thou* knowest my down-sitting and mine uprising
Psalms139:3 Thou searchest out my path and my lying down
Psalms139:4 For there is not yet a word on my tongue
Psalms139:5 Thou hast beset me behind and before
Psalms139:6 O knowledge too wonderful for me! it is high
Psalms139:7 Whither shall I go from thy Spirit? and whither flee from thy presence?
Psalms139:8 If I ascend up into the heavens thou art there; or if I make my bed in Sheol
Psalms139:9 [If] I take the wings of the dawn [and] dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea
Psalms139:10 Even there shall thy hand lead me
Psalms139:11 And if I say

Psalms139:13 For thou hast possessed my reins; thou didst cover me in my mother's womb.
Psalms139:14 I will praise thee
Psalms139:15 My bones were not hidden from thee when I was made in secret
Psalms139:16 Thine eyes did see my unformed substance
Psalms139:17 But how precious are thy thoughts unto me
Psalms139:18 [If] I would count them
Psalms139:19 Oh that thou wouldest slay the wicked
Psalms139:20 For they speak of thee wickedly
Psalms139:21 Do not I hate them
Psalms139:22 I hate them with perfect hatred; I account them mine enemies.
Psalms139:23 Search me
Psalms139:24 And see if there be any grievous way in me; and lead me in the way everlasting.

Psalms149:7 To execute vengeance against the nations
Psalms149:8 To bind their kings with chains
Psalms149:9 To execute upon them the judgment written. This honour have all his saints. Hallelujah!
Psalms150:1 Hallelujah! Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in the firmament of his power.
Psalms150:2 Praise him in his mighty acts; praise him according to the abundance of his greatness.
Psalms150:3 Praise him with the sound of the trumpet; praise him with lute and harp;
Psalms150:4 Praise him with the tambour and dance; praise him with stringed instruments and the pipe;
Psalms150:5 Praise him with loud cymbals; praise him with high sounding cymbals.
Psalms150:6 Let everything that hath breath praise Jah. Hallelujah!
Proverbs1:1 Proverbs of Solomon
Proverbs1:2 to know wisdom and instruction; to discern the words of understanding;
Proverbs1:3 to receive the instruction of wisdom
Proverbs1:4 to give prudence to the simple
Proverbs1:5 He that is wise will hear
Proverbs1:6 to understand a proverb and an allegory
Proverbs1:7 The fear of Jehovah is the beginning of knowledge: fools despise wisdom and instruction.
Proverbs1:8 Hear
Proverbs1:9 for they shall be a garland of grace unto thy head
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 let us swallow them up alive as Sheol
Proverbs1:13 we shall find all precious substance
Proverbs1:14 cast in thy lot among us; we will all have one purse:
Proverbs1:15 my son
Proverbs1:16 for their feet run to evil
Proverbs1:17 For in vain the net is spread in the sight of anything which hath wings.
Proverbs1:18 And these lay wait for their own blood; they lurk secretly for their own lives.
Proverbs1:19 So are the paths of every one that is greedy of gain: it taketh away the life of its possessors.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom crieth without; she raiseth her voice in the broadways;
Proverbs1:21 she calleth in the chief [place] of concourse
Proverbs1:22 How long
Proverbs1:23 Turn you at my reproof: behold
Proverbs1:24 Because I have called
Proverbs1:25 and ye have rejected all my counsel
Proverbs1:26 I also will laugh in your calamity
Proverbs1:27 when your fear cometh as sudden destruction
Proverbs1:28 then will they call upon me
Proverbs1:29 Because they hated knowledge
Proverbs1:30 they would none of my counsel
Proverbs1:31 therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their way
Proverbs1:32 For the turning away of the simple shall slay them
Proverbs1:33 But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 so that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom [and] thou apply thy heart to understanding;
Proverbs2:3 yea
Proverbs2:4 if thou seekest her as silver and searchest for her as for hidden treasures:
Proverbs2:5 then shalt thou understand the fear of Jehovah
Proverbs2:6 For Jehovah giveth wisdom; out of his mouth [come] knowledge and understanding.
Proverbs2:7 He layeth up sound wisdom for the upright; [he] is a shield to them that walk in integrity;
Proverbs2:8 guarding the paths of just judgment and keeping the way of his godly ones.
Proverbs2:9 Then shalt thou understand righteousness and judgment and equity: every good path.
Proverbs2:10 When wisdom entereth into thy heart and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul
Proverbs2:11 discretion shall keep thee
Proverbs2:12 To deliver thee from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 [from those] who leave the paths of uprightness
Proverbs2:14 who rejoice to do evil
Proverbs2:15 whose paths are crooked
Proverbs2:16 To deliver thee from the strange woman
Proverbs2:17 who forsaketh the guide of her youth
Proverbs2:18 for her house inclineth unto death
Proverbs2:19 none that go unto her return again
Proverbs2:20 that thou mayest walk in the way of the good
Proverbs2:21 For the upright shall dwell in the land
Proverbs2:22 but the wicked shall be cut off from the land
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 for length of days
Proverbs3:3 Let not loving-kindness and truth forsake thee; bind them about thy neck
Proverbs3:4 and thou shalt find favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Confide in Jehovah with all thy heart
Proverbs3:6 in all thy ways acknowledge him
Proverbs3:7 Be not wise in thine own eyes; fear Jehovah
Proverbs3:8 it shall be health for thy navel
Proverbs3:9 Honour Jehovah with thy substance
Proverbs3:10 so shall thy barns be filled with plenty
Proverbs3:11 My son
Proverbs3:12 for whom Jehovah loveth he chasteneth
Proverbs3:13 Blessed is the man that findeth wisdom
Proverbs3:14 For the gain thereof is better than the gain of silver
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than rubies; and all the things thou canst desire are not equal unto her.
Proverbs3:16 Length of days is in her right hand; in her left hand riches and honour.
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are ways of pleasantness
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her; and happy is he that retaineth her.
Proverbs3:19 Jehovah by wisdom founded the earth; by understanding he established the heavens.
Proverbs3:20 By his knowledge the deeps were broken up
Proverbs3:21 My son
Proverbs3:22 so shall they be life unto thy soul
Proverbs3:23 Then shalt thou walk in thy way securely
Proverbs3:24 when thou liest down
Proverbs3:25 Be not afraid of sudden fear
Proverbs3:26 for Jehovah shall be thy confidence
Proverbs3:27 Withhold not good from them to whom it is due
Proverbs3:28 Say not unto thy neighbour
Proverbs3:29 Devise not evil against thy neighbour
Proverbs3:30 Strive not with a man without cause
Proverbs3:31 Envy not the man of violence
Proverbs3:32 For the perverse is an abomination to Jehovah; but his secret is with the upright.
Proverbs3:33 The curse of Jehovah is in the house of the wicked; but he blesseth the habitation of the righteous.
Proverbs3:34 He indeed scorneth the scorners; but he giveth grace unto the lowly.
Proverbs3:35 The wise shall inherit glory; but shame shall be the promotion of the foolish.
Proverbs4:1 Hear
Proverbs4:2 for I give you good doctrine: forsake ye not my law.
Proverbs4:3 For I was a son unto my father
Proverbs4:4 And he taught me
Proverbs4:5 Get wisdom
Proverbs4:6 Forsake her not
Proverbs4:7 The beginning of wisdom [is]
Proverbs4:8 Exalt her
Proverbs4:9 She shall give to thy head a garland of grace; a crown of glory will she bestow upon thee.
Proverbs4:10 Hear
Proverbs4:11 I will teach thee in the way of wisdom
Proverbs4:12 When thou goest
Proverbs4:13 Take fast hold of instruction
Proverbs4:14 Enter not into the path of the wicked
Proverbs4:15 avoid it
Proverbs4:16 For they sleep not except they have done mischief
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness
Proverbs4:18 But the path of the righteous is as the shining light
Proverbs4:19 The way of the wicked is as darkness: they know not at what they stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thy heart.
Proverbs4:22 For they are life unto those that find them
Proverbs4:23 Keep thy heart more than anything that is guarded; for out of it are the issues of life.
Proverbs4:24 Put away from thee perverseness of mouth
Proverbs4:25 Let thine eyes look right on
Proverbs4:26 Ponder the path of thy feet
Proverbs4:27 Turn not to the right hand nor to the left; remove thy foot from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 that thou mayest keep reflection
Proverbs5:3 For the lips of the strange woman drop honey
Proverbs5:4 but her end is bitter as wormwood
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death; her steps take hold on Sheol.
Proverbs5:6 Lest she should ponder the path of life
Proverbs5:7 And now
Proverbs5:8 Remove thy way far from her
Proverbs5:9 lest thou give thine honour unto others
Proverbs5:10 lest strangers be filled with thy wealth
Proverbs5:11 and thou mourn in thine end
Proverbs5:12 and thou say
Proverbs5:13 and I have not hearkened unto the voice of my teachers
Proverbs5:14 I was well nigh in all evil in the midst of the congregation and assembly.
Proverbs5:15 Drink waters out of thine own cistern
Proverbs5:16 Thy fountains shall be poured forth
Proverbs5:17 Let them be only thine own
Proverbs5:18 Let thy fountain be blessed; and have joy of the wife of thy youth.
Proverbs5:19 As a lovely hind and a graceful roe
Proverbs5:20 And why shouldest thou
Proverbs5:21 For the ways of man are before the eyes of Jehovah
Proverbs5:22 His own iniquities shall take the wicked
Proverbs5:23 He shall die for want of discipline; and in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.
Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 thou art snared with the words of thy mouth
Proverbs6:3 Do this now
Proverbs6:4 Give not sleep to thine eyes
Proverbs6:5 deliver thyself as a gazelle from the hand [of the hunter]
Proverbs6:6 Go to the ant
Proverbs6:7 which having no chief
Proverbs6:8 provideth her bread in the summer
Proverbs6:9 How long
Proverbs6:10 A little sleep
Proverbs6:11 So shall thy poverty come as a roving plunderer
Proverbs6:12 A man of Belial
Proverbs6:13 he winketh with his eyes
Proverbs6:14 deceits are in his heart; he deviseth mischief at all times
Proverbs6:15 Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly: in a moment shall he be broken
Proverbs6:16 These six [things] doth Jehovah hate
Proverbs6:17 haughty eyes
Proverbs6:18 a heart that deviseth wicked imaginations; feet that are swift in running to mischief;
Proverbs6:19 a false witness that uttereth lies
Proverbs6:20 My son
Proverbs6:21 bind them continually upon thy heart
Proverbs6:22 when thou walkest
Proverbs6:23 For the commandment is a lamp
Proverbs6:24 to keep thee from the evil woman
Proverbs6:25 Lust not after her beauty in thy heart
Proverbs6:26 for by means of a whorish woman [a man is brought] to a loaf of bread
Proverbs6:27 Can a man take fire in his bosom
Proverbs6:28 Can one go upon hot coals
Proverbs6:29 So he that goeth in to his neighbour's wife: whosoever toucheth her shall not be innocent.
Proverbs6:30 They do not despise a thief
Proverbs6:31 and if he be found
Proverbs6:32 Whoso committeth adultery with a woman is void of understanding: he that doeth it destroyeth his own soul.
Proverbs6:33 A wound and contempt shall he get; and his reproach shall not be wiped away.
Proverbs6:34 For jealousy is the rage of a man
Proverbs6:35 he will not regard any ransom
Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 Keep my commandments
Proverbs7:3 Bind them upon thy fingers
Proverbs7:4 Say unto wisdom
Proverbs7:5 that they may keep thee from the strange woman
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house
Proverbs7:7 and I beheld among the simple ones
Proverbs7:8 passing through the street near her corner; and he went the way to her house
Proverbs7:9 in the twilight
Proverbs7:10 And behold
Proverbs7:11 She is clamorous and unmanageable; her feet abide not in her house:
Proverbs7:12 now without
Proverbs7:13 And she caught him and kissed him
Proverbs7:14 I have peace-offerings with me; this day have I paid my vows:
Proverbs7:15 therefore came I forth to meet thee
Proverbs7:16 I have decked my bed with tapestry coverlets of variegated linen from Egypt;
Proverbs7:17 I have perfumed my couch with myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For the husband is not at home
Proverbs7:20 he hath taken the money-bag with him
Proverbs7:21 With her much enticement she beguiled him; with the smoothness of her lips she constrained him.
Proverbs7:22 He went after her straightway
Proverbs7:23 till an arrow strike through his liver: as a bird hasteth to the snare
Proverbs7:24 And now
Proverbs7:25 Let not thy heart decline to her ways
Proverbs7:26 for she hath cast down many wounded
Proverbs7:27 Her house is the way to Sheol
Proverbs8:1 Doth not wisdom cry? and understanding give forth her voice?
Proverbs8:2 On the top of high places by the way
Proverbs8:3 Beside the gates
Proverbs8:4 Unto you
Proverbs8:5 O ye simple
Proverbs8:6 Hear
Proverbs8:7 For my palate shall meditate truth
Proverbs8:8 All the words of my mouth are in righteousness; there is nothing tortuous or perverse in them.
Proverbs8:9 They are all plain to him that understandeth
Proverbs8:10 Receive my instruction
Proverbs8:11 for wisdom is better than rubies
Proverbs8:12 I wisdom dwell [with] prudence
Proverbs8:13 The fear of Jehovah is to hate evil; pride
Proverbs8:14 Counsel is mine
Proverbs8:15 By me kings reign
Proverbs8:16 by me princes rule
Proverbs8:17 I love those that love me; and they that seek me early shall find me.
Proverbs8:18 Riches and honour are with me; durable wealth and righteousness.
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than fine gold
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the path of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 that I may cause those that love me to inherit substance; and I will fill their treasuries.
Proverbs8:22 Jehovah possessed me in the beginning of his way
Proverbs8:23 I was set up from eternity
Proverbs8:24 When there were no depths
Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains were settled
Proverbs8:26 while as yet he had not made the earth
Proverbs8:27 When he prepared the heavens I was there; when he ordained the circle upon the face of the deep;
Proverbs8:28 when he established the skies above
Proverbs8:29 when he imposed on the sea his decree that the waters should not pass his commandment
Proverbs8:30 then I was by him [his] nursling
Proverbs8:31 rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth
Proverbs8:32 And now
Proverbs8:33 hear instruction and be wise
Proverbs8:34 Blessed is the man that heareth me
Proverbs8:35 For whoso findeth me findeth life
Proverbs8:36 but he that sinneth against me doeth violence to his own soul: all they that hate me love death.
Proverbs9:1 Wisdom hath built her house
Proverbs9:2 she hath slaughtered her cattle
Proverbs9:3 she hath sent forth her maidens: she crieth upon the summits of the high places of the city
Proverbs9:4 Whoso is simple
Proverbs9:5 Come
Proverbs9:6 Forsake follies and live
Proverbs9:7 He that instructeth a scorner getteth to himself shame; and he that reproveth a wicked [man] [getteth] to himself a blot.
Proverbs9:8 Reprove not a scorner
Proverbs9:9 Impart to a wise [man]
Proverbs9:10 The fear of Jehovah is the beginning of wisdom; and the knowledge of the Holy is intelligence.
Proverbs9:11 For by me thy days shall be multiplied
Proverbs9:12 If thou art wise
Proverbs9:13 The foolish woman is clamorous; she is stupid
Proverbs9:14 And she sitteth at the entry of her house
Proverbs9:15 to call passers-by who go right on their ways:
Proverbs9:16 Whoso is simple
Proverbs9:17 Stolen waters are sweet
Proverbs9:18 But he knoweth not that the dead are there; [that] her guests are in the depths of Sheol.
Proverbs10:1 The Proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father; but a foolish son is the grief of his mother.
Proverbs10:2 Treasures of wickedness profit nothing; but righteousness delivereth from death.
Proverbs10:3 Jehovah suffereth not the soul of the righteous [man] to famish; but he repelleth the craving of the wicked.
Proverbs10:4 He cometh to want that dealeth with a slack hand; but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.
Proverbs10:5 He that gathereth in summer is a wise son; he that sleepeth in harvest is a son that causeth shame.
Proverbs10:6 Blessings are upon the head of a righteous [man]; but the mouth of the wicked covereth violence.
Proverbs10:7 The memory of the righteous [man] shall be blessed; but the name of the wicked shall rot.
Proverbs10:8 The wise in heart receiveth commandments; but a prating fool shall fall.
Proverbs10:9 He that walketh in integrity walketh securely; but he that perverteth his ways shall be known.
Proverbs10:10 He that winketh with the eye causeth grief
Proverbs10:11 The mouth of a righteous [man] is a fountain of life; but the mouth of the wicked covereth violence.
Proverbs10:12 Hatred stirreth up strifes; but love covereth all transgressions.
Proverbs10:13 In the lips of an intelligent [man] wisdom is found; but a rod is for the back of him that is void of understanding.
Proverbs10:14 The wise lay up knowledge; but the mouth of the fool is near destruction.
Proverbs10:15 The rich man's wealth is his strong city; the destruction of the poor is their poverty.
Proverbs10:16 The labour of a righteous [man] [tendeth] to life; the revenue of a wicked [man]
Proverbs10:17 Keeping instruction is the path to life; but he that forsaketh reproof goeth astray.
Proverbs10:18 He that covereth hatred hath lying lips
Proverbs10:19 In the multitude of words there wanteth not transgression; but he that restraineth his lips doeth wisely.
Proverbs10:20 The tongue of the righteous [man] is [as] choice silver; the heart of the wicked is little worth.
Proverbs10:21 The lips of a righteous [man] feed many; but fools die for want of understanding.
Proverbs10:22 The blessing of Jehovah
Proverbs10:23 It is as sport to a foolish [man] to do wickedness; but a man of understanding hath wisdom.
Proverbs10:24 The fear of a wicked [man]
Proverbs10:25 As a whirlwind passeth
Proverbs10:26 As vinegar to the teeth
Proverbs10:27 The fear of Jehovah prolongeth days; but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.
Proverbs10:28 The hope of the righteous is joy; but the expectation of the wicked shall perish.
Proverbs10:29 The way of Jehovah is strength to the perfect [man]
Proverbs10:30 The righteous [man] shall never be moved; but the wicked shall not inhabit the land.
Proverbs10:31 The mouth of a righteous [man] putteth forth wisdom; but the froward tongue shall be cut out.
Proverbs10:32 The lips of a righteous [man] know what is acceptable; but the mouth of the wicked is frowardness.
Proverbs11:1 A false balance is an abomination to Jehovah; but a just weight is his delight.
Proverbs11:2 [When] pride cometh
Proverbs11:3 The integrity of the upright guideth them; but the crookedness of the unfaithful destroyeth them.
Proverbs11:4 Wealth profiteth not in the day of wrath; but righteousness delivereth from death.
Proverbs11:5 The righteousness of the perfect maketh plain his way; but the wicked falleth by his own wickedness.
Proverbs11:6 The righteousness of the upright delivereth them; but the treacherous are taken in their own craving.
Proverbs11:7 When a wicked man dieth
Proverbs11:8 The righteous is delivered out of trouble
Proverbs11:9 With his mouth a hypocrite destroyeth his neighbour; but through knowledge are the righteous delivered.
Proverbs11:10 When it goeth well with the righteous
Proverbs11:11 By the blessing of the upright the city is exalted; but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.
Proverbs11:12 He that despiseth his neighbour is void of heart; but a man of understanding holdeth his peace.
Proverbs11:13 He that goeth about talebearing revealeth secrets; but he that is of a faithful spirit concealeth the matter.
Proverbs11:14 Where no advice is
Proverbs11:15 It goeth ill with him that is surety for another; but he that hateth suretyship is secure.
Proverbs11:16 A gracious woman retaineth honour; and the violent retain riches.
Proverbs11:17 The merciful man doeth good to his own soul; but the cruel troubleth his own flesh.
Proverbs11:18 The wicked worketh a deceitful work; but he that soweth righteousness hath a sure reward.
Proverbs11:19 As righteousness [tendeth] to life
Proverbs11:20 The perverse in heart are abomination to Jehovah; but they that are perfect in [their] way are his delight.
Proverbs11:21 Hand for hand! an evil [man] shall not be held innocent; but the seed of the righteous shall be delivered.
Proverbs11:22 A fair woman who is without discretion
Proverbs11:23 The desire of the righteous is only good; the expectation of the wicked is wrath.
Proverbs11:24 There is that scattereth
Proverbs11:25 The liberal soul shall be made fat
Proverbs11:26 He that withholdeth corn
Proverbs11:27 He that is earnest after good seeketh favour; but he that searcheth for mischief
Proverbs11:28 He that trusteth in his riches shall fall; but the righteous shall flourish as a leaf.
Proverbs11:29 He that troubleth his own house shall inherit wind; and the fool shall be servant to the wise of heart.
Proverbs11:30 The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life; and the wise winneth souls.
Proverbs11:31 Behold
Proverbs12:1 Whoso loveth discipline loveth knowledge
Proverbs12:2 A good [man] obtaineth favour of Jehovah; but a man of mischievous devices will he condemn.
Proverbs12:3 A man shall not be established by wickedness; but the root of the righteous shall not be moved.
Proverbs12:4 A woman of worth is a crown to her husband; but she that maketh ashamed is as rottenness in his bones.
Proverbs12:5 The thoughts of the righteous are right; the counsels of the wicked are deceit.
Proverbs12:6 The words of the wicked are a lying-in-wait for blood; but the mouth of the upright shall deliver them.
Proverbs12:7 Overthrow the wicked
Proverbs12:8 A man is commended according to his wisdom; but he that is of a perverted heart shall be despised.
Proverbs12:9 Better is he that is lightly esteemed
Proverbs12:10 A righteous man is concerned for the life of his beast; but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.
Proverbs12:11 He that tilleth his land shall be satisfied with bread; but he that followeth the worthless is void of understanding.
Proverbs12:12 The wicked desireth the net of evil [men]; but the root of the righteous yieldeth [fruit].
Proverbs12:13 In the transgression of the lips is an evil snare; but a righteous [man] shall go forth out of trouble.
Proverbs12:14 A man is satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth; and the recompense of a man's hands shall be rendered unto him.
Proverbs12:15 The way of a fool is right in his own eyes; but he that is wise hearkeneth unto counsel.
Proverbs12:16 The vexation of the fool is presently known; but a prudent [man] covereth shame.
Proverbs12:17 He that uttereth truth sheweth forth righteousness; but a false witness deceit.
Proverbs12:18 There is that babbleth like the piercings of a sword; but the tongue of the wise is health.
Proverbs12:19 The lip of truth shall be established for ever; but a lying tongue is but for a moment.
Proverbs12:20 Deceit is in the heart of them that devise evil; but to the counsellors of peace is joy.
Proverbs12:21 There shall no evil happen to a righteous [man]; but the wicked shall be filled with mischief.
Proverbs12:22 Lying lips are an abomination to Jehovah; but they that deal truly are his delight.
Proverbs12:23 A prudent man concealeth knowledge; but the heart of the foolish proclaimeth folly.
Proverbs12:24 The hand of the diligent shall bear rule; but the slothful [hand] shall be under tribute.
Proverbs12:25 Heaviness in the heart of man maketh it stoop; but a good word maketh it glad.
Proverbs12:26 The righteous guideth his neighbour; but the way of the wicked misleadeth them.
Proverbs12:27 The slothful roasteth not what he took in hunting; but man's precious substance is to the diligent.
Proverbs12:28 In the path of righteousness is life
Proverbs13:1 A wise son [heareth] his father's instruction; but a scorner heareth not rebuke.
Proverbs13:2 A man shall eat good by the fruit of his mouth; but the soul of the treacherous
Proverbs13:3 He that guardeth his mouth keepeth his soul; destruction shall be to him that openeth wide his lips.
Proverbs13:4 A sluggard's soul desireth and hath nothing; but the soul of the diligent shall be made fat.
Proverbs13:5 A righteous [man] hateth lying; but the wicked maketh himself odious and cometh to shame.
Proverbs13:6 Righteousness preserveth him that is perfect in the way; but wickedness overthroweth the sinner.
Proverbs13:7 There is that feigneth himself rich
Proverbs13:8 The ransom of a man's life is his riches; but the indigent heareth not rebuke.
Proverbs13:9 The light of the righteous rejoiceth; but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out.
Proverbs13:10 By pride there only cometh contention; but with the well-advised is wisdom.
Proverbs13:11 Wealth [gotten] by vanity diminisheth; but he that gathereth by manual-labour shall increase [it].
Proverbs13:12 Hope deferred maketh the heart sick; but the desire [that] cometh to pass is a tree of life.
Proverbs13:13 Whoso despiseth the word shall be held by it; but he that feareth the commandment shall be rewarded.
Proverbs13:14 The teaching of the wise [man] is a fountain of life
Proverbs13:15 Good understanding procureth favour; but the way of the treacherous is hard.
Proverbs13:16 Every prudent [man] acteth with knowledge; but the foolish layeth open [his] folly.
Proverbs13:17 A wicked messenger falleth into evil; but a faithful ambassador is health.
Proverbs13:18 Poverty and shame shall be [to] him that refuseth instruction; but he that regardeth reproof shall be honoured.
Proverbs13:19 The desire accomplished is sweet to the soul; but it is abomination to the foolish to depart from evil.
Proverbs13:20 He that walketh with wise [men] becometh wise; but a companion of the foolish will be depraved.
Proverbs13:21 Evil pursueth sinners; but to the righteous good shall be repaid.
Proverbs13:22 A good man leaveth an inheritance to his children's children; but the wealth of the sinner is laid up for the righteous [man].
Proverbs13:23 Much food is in the tillage of the poor; but there is that is lost for want of judgment.
Proverbs13:24 He that spareth his rod hateth his son; but he that loveth him chasteneth him betimes.
Proverbs13:25 The righteous eateth to the satisfying of his soul; but the belly of the wicked shall want.
Proverbs14:1 The wisdom of women buildeth their house; but folly plucketh it down with her hands.
Proverbs14:2 He that walketh in his uprightness feareth Jehovah; but he that is perverted in his ways despiseth him.
Proverbs14:3 In the fool's mouth is a rod of pride; but the lips of the wise shall preserve them.
Proverbs14:4 Where no oxen are
Proverbs14:5 A faithful witness will not lie; but a false witness uttereth lies.
Proverbs14:6 A scorner seeketh wisdom
Proverbs14:7 Go from the presence of a foolish man
Proverbs14:8 The wisdom of the prudent is to discern his way; but the folly of the foolish is deceit.
Proverbs14:9 Fools make a mock at trespass; but for the upright there is favour.
Proverbs14:10 The heart knoweth its own bitterness
Proverbs14:11 The house of the wicked shall be overthrown; but the tent of the upright shall flourish.
Proverbs14:12 There is a way that seemeth right unto a man
Proverbs14:13 Even in laughter the heart is sorrowful
Proverbs14:14 The backslider in heart shall be filled with his own ways
Proverbs14:15 The simple believeth every word; but the prudent [man] heedeth his going.
Proverbs14:16 A wise [man] feareth and departeth from evil; but the foolish is overbearing and confident.

Proverbs14:19 The evil bow before the good

Proverbs14:22 Do they not err that devise evil? but loving-kindness and truth are for those that devise good.
Proverbs14:23 In all labour there is profit; but the talk of the lips [tendeth] only to want.

Proverbs14:28 In the multitude of people is the king's glory; but in the lack of people is the ruin of a prince.

Proverbs14:34 Righteousness exalteth a nation; but sin is a reproach to peoples.
Proverbs14:35 The king's favour is toward a wise servant; but his wrath is [against] him that causeth shame.
Proverbs15:1 A soft answer turneth away fury; but a grievous word stirreth up anger.

Proverbs15:20 A wise son maketh a glad father; but a foolish man despiseth his mother.

Proverbs27:15 A continual dropping on a very rainy day and a contentious woman are alike:
Proverbs27:16 whosoever will restrain her restraineth the wind
Proverbs27:17 Iron is sharpened by iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.
Proverbs27:18 Whoso keepeth the fig-tree shall eat the fruit thereof; and he that guardeth his master shall be honoured.
Proverbs27:19 As [in] water face [answereth] to face
Proverbs27:20 Sheol and destruction are insatiable; so the eyes of man are never satisfied.
Proverbs27:21 The fining-pot is for silver

Proverbs27:24 for wealth is not for ever; and doth the crown [endure] from generation to generation?

Proverbs27:26 The lambs are for thy clothing
Proverbs27:27 and there is goats' milk enough for thy food
Proverbs28:1 The wicked flee when no man pursueth; but the righteous are bold as a lion.
Proverbs28:2 By the transgression of a land many are the princes thereof; but by a man of understanding [and] of knowledge
Proverbs28:3 A poor man who oppresseth the helpless is a sweeping rain which leaveth no food.
Proverbs28:4 They that forsake the law praise the wicked; but such as keep the law contend with them.
Proverbs28:5 Evil men understand not judgment; but they that seek Jehovah understand everything.
Proverbs28:6 Better is the poor that walketh in his integrity
Proverbs28:7 Whoso observeth the law is a son that hath understanding; but he that is a companion of profligates bringeth shame to his father.
Proverbs28:8 He that by usury and unjust gain increaseth his substance gathereth it for him that is gracious to the poor.
Proverbs28:9 He that turneth away his ear from hearing the law
Proverbs28:10 Whoso causeth the upright to go astray in an evil way
Proverbs28:11 A rich man is wise in his own eyes; but the poor that hath understanding searcheth him out.
Proverbs28:12 When the righteous triumph
Proverbs28:13 He that covereth his transgressions shall not prosper; but whoso confesseth and forsaketh [them] shall obtain mercy.
Proverbs28:14 Happy is the man that feareth always; but he that hardeneth his heart shall fall into evil.
Proverbs28:15 A roaring lion
Proverbs28:16 The prince void of intelligence is also a great oppressor: he that hateth covetousness shall prolong [his] days.
Proverbs28:17 A man laden with the blood of [any] person
Proverbs28:18 Whoso walketh in integrity shall be saved; but he that is perverted in [his] double ways
Proverbs28:19 He that tilleth his land shall be satisfied with bread; but he that followeth the worthless shall have poverty enough.
Proverbs28:20 A faithful man aboundeth with blessings; but he that maketh haste to be rich shall not be innocent.
Proverbs28:21 To have respect of persons is not good; but for a piece of bread will a man transgress.
Proverbs28:22 He that hath an evil eye hasteth after wealth
Proverbs28:23 He that rebuketh a man shall afterwards find more favour than he that flattereth with the tongue.
Proverbs28:24 Whoso robbeth his father and his mother
Proverbs28:25 He that is puffed up in soul exciteth contention; but he that relieth upon Jehovah shall be made fat.
Proverbs28:26 He that confideth in his own heart is a fool; but whoso walketh wisely
Proverbs28:27 He that giveth unto the poor shall not lack; but he that withdraweth his eyes shall have many a curse.
Proverbs28:28 When the wicked rise

Proverbs29:2 When the righteous increase

Proverbs29:4 A king by just judgment establisheth the land; but he that taketh gifts overthroweth it.
Proverbs29:5 A man that flattereth his neighbour spreadeth a net for his steps.

Proverbs29:7 The righteous taketh knowledge of the cause of the poor; the wicked understandeth not knowledge.

Proverbs29:22 An angry man exciteth contention; and a furious man aboundeth in transgression.
Proverbs29:23 A man's pride bringeth him low; but the humble in spirit shall obtain honour.

Proverbs30:1 The words of Agur the son of Jakeh; the prophecy uttered by the man unto Ithiel
Proverbs30:2 Truly *I* am more stupid than any one; and I have not a man's intelligence.
Proverbs30:3 I have neither learned wisdom
Proverbs30:4 Who hath ascended up into the heavens
Proverbs30:5 Every word of God is pure: he is a shield unto them that put their trust in him.

Proverbs31:10 Who can find a woman of worth? for her price is far above rubies.
Proverbs31:11 The heart of her husband confideth in her
Proverbs31:12 She doeth him good
Proverbs31:13 She seeketh wool and flax
Proverbs31:14 She is like the merchants' ships: she bringeth her food from afar;
Proverbs31:15 And she riseth while it is yet night
Proverbs31:16 She considereth a field
Proverbs31:17 She girdeth her loins with strength
Proverbs31:18 She perceiveth that her earning is good; her lamp goeth not out by night.

Proverbs31:21 She is not afraid of the snow for her household; for all her household are clothed with scarlet.
