Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the Preacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What profit hath man of all his labour wherewith he laboureth under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 [One] generation passeth away
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun also riseth
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind goeth towards the south
Ecclesiastes1:7 All the rivers run into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are full of toil; none can express it. The eye is not satisfied with seeing
Ecclesiastes1:9 That which hath been is that which shall be; and that which hath been done is that which will be done: and there is nothing new under the sun.
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there a thing whereof it may be said
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of former things; neither shall there be remembrance of things that are to come with those who shall live afterwards.
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 And I applied my heart to seek and search out by wisdom concerning all that is done under the heavens: this grievous occupation hath God given to the children of men to weary themselves therewith.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen all the works that are done under the sun
Ecclesiastes1:15 That which is crooked cannot be made straight; and that which is wanting cannot be numbered.
Ecclesiastes1:16 I communed with mine own heart
Ecclesiastes1:17 And I applied my heart to the knowledge of wisdom
Ecclesiastes1:18 For in much wisdom is much vexation
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said of laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I searched in my heart how to cherish my flesh with wine
Ecclesiastes2:4 I made me great works; I builded me houses; I planted me vineyards;
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made me gardens and parks
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made me ponds of water
Ecclesiastes2:7 I acquired servants and maidens
Ecclesiastes2:8 I gathered me also silver and gold
Ecclesiastes2:9 And I became great
Ecclesiastes2:10 And whatsoever mine eyes desired I kept not from them: I withheld not my heart from any joy; for my heart rejoiced in all my labour
Ecclesiastes2:11 Then I looked on all the works that my hands had wrought
Ecclesiastes2:12 And I turned myself to behold wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 And I saw that wisdom excelleth folly
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise man's eyes are in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 And I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:16 For there shall be no remembrance of the wise more than of the fool for ever; because everything is already forgotten in the days which come. And how dieth the wise even as the fool?
Ecclesiastes2:17 And I hated life; for the work that is wrought under the sun was grievous unto me; for all is vanity and pursuit of the wind.
Ecclesiastes2:18 And I hated all my labour wherewith I had been toiling under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knoweth whether he will be a wise [man] or a fool? yet shall he have rule over all my labour at which I have laboured
Ecclesiastes2:20 Then I went about to cause my heart to despair of all the labour wherewith I had laboured under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 For there is a man whose labour hath been with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what will man have of all his labour and of the striving of his heart
Ecclesiastes2:23 For all his days are sorrows
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing good for man
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who can eat
Ecclesiastes2:26 For he giveth to a man that is good in his sight wisdom
Ecclesiastes3:1 To everything there is a season
Ecclesiastes3:2 A time to be born
Ecclesiastes3:3 A time to kill
Ecclesiastes3:4 A time to weep
Ecclesiastes3:5 A time to cast away stones
Ecclesiastes3:6 A time to seek
Ecclesiastes3:7 A time to rend
Ecclesiastes3:8 A time to love
Ecclesiastes3:9 What profit hath he that worketh from that wherein he laboureth?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the travail that God hath given to the sons of men to toil in.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He hath made everything beautiful in its time; also he hath set the world in their heart
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing good for them but to rejoice and to do well in their life;
Ecclesiastes3:13 yea also that every man should eat and drink
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that whatever God doeth
Ecclesiastes3:15 That which is was long ago
Ecclesiastes3:16 And moreover I saw under the sun
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:19 For what befalleth the children of men befalleth beasts; even one thing befalleth them: as the one dieth
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go unto one place: all are of the dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knoweth the spirit of the children of men? Doth it go upwards? and the spirit of the beasts
Ecclesiastes3:22 And I have seen that there is nothing better than that man should rejoice in his own works; for that is his portion; for who shall bring him to see what shall be after him?
Ecclesiastes4:1 And I returned and saw all the oppressions that are done under the sun: and behold
Ecclesiastes4:2 Then I praised the dead who are already dead more than the living who are yet alive;
Ecclesiastes4:3 and more fortunate than both is he who hath not yet been
Ecclesiastes4:4 And I saw all labour
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool foldeth his hands together
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better is a handful with quietness
Ecclesiastes4:7 And I returned and saw vanity under the sun.
Ecclesiastes4:8 There is one [alone] and without a second; also he hath neither son nor brother: yet is there no end of all his labour
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one; because they have a good reward for their labour.
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if they fall
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again
Ecclesiastes4:12 And if a [man] overpower the one
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better is a poor but wise youth than an old and foolish king
Ecclesiastes4:14 For out of the prison-house he came forth to reign
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw all the living that walk under the sun
Ecclesiastes4:16 [There is] no end of all the people
Ecclesiastes5:1 Keep thy foot when thou goest to the house of God
Ecclesiastes5:2 Be not rash with thy mouth
Ecclesiastes5:3 For a dream cometh through the multitude of business
Ecclesiastes5:4 When thou vowest a vow unto God
Ecclesiastes5:5 Better is it that thou shouldest not vow
Ecclesiastes5:6 Suffer not thy mouth to cause thy flesh to sin; neither say thou before the angel
Ecclesiastes5:7 For in the multitude of dreams are vanities; so with many words: but fear God.
Ecclesiastes5:8 If thou seest the oppression of the poor
Ecclesiastes5:9 Moreover the earth is every way profitable: the king [himself] is dependent upon the field.
Ecclesiastes5:10 He that loveth silver shall not be satisfied with silver
Ecclesiastes5:11 When goods increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 The sleep of the labourer is sweet
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a grievous evil that I have seen under the sun: riches kept for the owners thereof to their hurt;
Ecclesiastes5:14 or those riches perish by some evil circumstance
Ecclesiastes5:15 As he came forth from his mother's womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 And this also is a grievous evil
Ecclesiastes5:17 All his days also he eateth in darkness
Ecclesiastes5:18 Behold what I have seen good and comely: [it is] to eat and to drink
Ecclesiastes5:19 Every man also to whom God hath given riches and wealth
Ecclesiastes5:20 For he will not much remember the days of his life
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is an evil that I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 one to whom God giveth riches
Ecclesiastes6:3 If a man beget a hundred [sons]
Ecclesiastes6:4 For it cometh in vanity
Ecclesiastes6:5 moreover it hath not seen nor known the sun: this hath rest rather than the other.
Ecclesiastes6:6 Yea
Ecclesiastes6:7 All the labour of man is for his mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 For what advantage hath the wise above the fool? what hath the poor
Ecclesiastes6:9 Better is the seeing of the eyes than the wandering of the desire: this also is vanity and pursuit of the wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 That which is hath already been named; and what man is
Ecclesiastes6:11 For there are many things that increase vanity: what is man advantaged?
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knoweth what is good for man in life
Ecclesiastes7:1 A [good] name is better than precious ointment
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to the house of mourning than to go to the house of feasting: in that that is the end of all men
Ecclesiastes7:3 Vexation is better than laughter; for by the sadness of the countenance the heart is made better.
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better for a man to hear the rebuke of the wise
Ecclesiastes7:6 For as the crackling of thorns under a pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 Surely oppression maketh a wise man mad
Ecclesiastes7:8 Better is the end of a thing than its beginning; better is a patient spirit than a proud spirit.
Ecclesiastes7:9 Be not hasty in thy spirit to be vexed; for vexation resteth in the bosom of fools.
Ecclesiastes7:10 Say not
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom is as good as an inheritance
Ecclesiastes7:12 For wisdom is a defence [as] money is a defence; but the excellency of knowledge is
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God; for who can make straight what he hath made crooked?
Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity enjoy good
Ecclesiastes7:15 All [this] have I seen in the days of my vanity: there is a righteous [man] that perisheth by his righteousness
Ecclesiastes7:16 Be not righteous overmuch; neither make thyself overwise: why shouldest thou destroy thyself?
Ecclesiastes7:17 Be not overmuch wicked
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good that thou shouldest take hold of this; yea
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom strengtheneth the wise more than ten mighty [men] that are in a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 Surely there is not a righteous man upon earth
Ecclesiastes7:21 Also give not heed unto all words that are spoken
Ecclesiastes7:22 For also thine own heart knoweth that oftentimes thou thyself likewise hast cursed others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 All this have I tried by wisdom: I said
Ecclesiastes7:24 Whatever hath been
Ecclesiastes7:25 I turned
Ecclesiastes7:26 and I found more bitter than death the woman whose heart is nets and snares
Ecclesiastes7:27 See this which I have found
Ecclesiastes7:28 which my soul yet seeketh
Ecclesiastes7:29 Only see this which I have found: that God made man upright
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is as the wise? and who knoweth the explanation of things? A man's wisdom maketh his face to shine
Ecclesiastes8:2 I [say]
Ecclesiastes8:3 Be not hasty to go out of his sight; persist not in an evil thing: for he doeth whatever pleaseth him
Ecclesiastes8:4 because the word of a king is power; and who may say unto him
Ecclesiastes8:5 Whoso keepeth the commandment shall know no evil thing; and a wise man's heart knoweth time and manner.
Ecclesiastes8:6 For to every purpose there is time and manner. For the misery of man is great upon him;
Ecclesiastes8:7 for he knoweth not that which shall be; for who can tell him how it shall be?
Ecclesiastes8:8 There is no man who hath control over the spirit to retain the spirit; and no one hath control over the day of death; and there is no discharge in that war
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this have I seen
Ecclesiastes8:10 And I have also seen the wicked buried and going away; and such as had acted rightly went from [the] holy place
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because sentence against an evil work is not executed speedily
Ecclesiastes8:12 Though a sinner do evil a hundred times
Ecclesiastes8:13 but it shall not be well with the wicked
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is a vanity which is done upon the earth; that there are righteous [men] unto whom it happeneth according to the work of the wicked; and there are wicked [men] to whom it happeneth according to the work of the righteous. I said that this also is vanity.
Ecclesiastes8:15 And I commended mirth
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my heart to know wisdom
Ecclesiastes8:17 then I saw that all [is] the work of God
Ecclesiastes9:1 For all this I laid to my heart and [indeed] to investigate all this
Ecclesiastes9:2 All things [come] alike to all: one event to the righteous and to the wicked
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is an evil among all that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:4 For to him that is joined to all the living there is hope; for a living dog is better than a dead lion.
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they shall die; but the dead know not anything
Ecclesiastes9:6 Their love also
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let thy garments be always white
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with the wife whom thou lovest all the days of the life of thy vanity
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever thy hand findeth to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 I returned
Ecclesiastes9:12 For man also knoweth not his time: as the fishes that are taken in an evil net
Ecclesiastes9:13 This also have I seen as wisdom under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a little city
Ecclesiastes9:15 and there was found in it a poor wise man
Ecclesiastes9:16 Then said I
Ecclesiastes9:17 The words of the wise are heard in quiet more than the cry of him that ruleth among fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war; but one sinner destroyeth much good.
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies cause the ointment of the apothecary to stink [and] ferment; [so] a little folly is weightier than wisdom [and] honour.
Ecclesiastes10:2 The heart of a wise [man] is at his right hand; but a fool's heart at his left.
Ecclesiastes10:3 Yea also
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the spirit of the ruler rise up against thee
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil that I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes10:6 folly is set in great dignities
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen servants upon horses
Ecclesiastes10:8 He that diggeth a pit falleth into it; and whoso breaketh down a hedge
Ecclesiastes10:9 Whoso removeth stones is hurt therewith; he that cleaveth wood is endangered thereby.
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the iron be blunt
Ecclesiastes10:11 If the serpent bite before enchantment
Ecclesiastes10:12 The words of a wise man's mouth are gracious; but the lips of a fool swallow up himself.
Ecclesiastes10:13 The beginning of the words of his mouth is folly; and the end of his talk is mischievous madness.
Ecclesiastes10:14 And the fool multiplieth words: [yet] man knoweth not what shall be; and what shall be after him
Ecclesiastes10:15 The labour of fools wearieth them
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to thee
Ecclesiastes10:17 Happy art thou
Ecclesiastes10:18 By much sloth fulness the framework falleth in; and through idleness of the hands the house drippeth.
Ecclesiastes10:19 A feast is made for laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Curse not the king
Ecclesiastes11:1 Cast thy bread upon the waters; for thou shalt find it after many days.
Ecclesiastes11:2 Give a portion to seven
Ecclesiastes11:3 If the clouds be full of rain
Ecclesiastes11:4 He that observeth the wind will not sow; and he that regardeth the clouds will not reap.
Ecclesiastes11:5 As thou knowest not what is the way of the spirit
Ecclesiastes11:6 In the morning sow thy seed
Ecclesiastes11:7 Now the light is sweet
Ecclesiastes11:8 but if a man live many years
Ecclesiastes11:9 Rejoice
Ecclesiastes11:10 Then remove discontent from thy heart
Ecclesiastes12:1 And remember thy Creator in the days of thy youth
Ecclesiastes12:2 before the sun
Ecclesiastes12:3 in the day when the keepers of the house tremble
Ecclesiastes12:4 and the doors are shut toward the street; when the sound of the grinding is subdued
Ecclesiastes12:5 they are also afraid of what is high
Ecclesiastes12:6 before the silver cord be loosed
Ecclesiastes12:7 and the dust return to the earth as it was
Ecclesiastes12:8 Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes12:9 And moreover
Ecclesiastes12:10 The Preacher sought to find out acceptable words; and that which was written is upright
Ecclesiastes12:11 The words of the wise are as goads
Ecclesiastes12:12 And besides
Ecclesiastes12:13 Let us hear the end of the whole matter: Fear God
Ecclesiastes12:14 For God shall bring every work into judgment
SongOfSongs1:1 The song of songs

SongOfSongs1:5 I am black
SongOfSongs1:6 Look not upon me
SongOfSongs1:7 Tell me
SongOfSongs1:8 If thou know not
SongOfSongs1:9 I compare thee
SongOfSongs1:10 Thy cheeks are comely with bead-rows

Isaiah1:1 The vision of Isaiah the son of Amos
Isaiah1:2 Hear
Isaiah1:3 The ox knoweth his owner
Isaiah1:4 Ah sinful nation
Isaiah1:5 Why should ye be smitten any more? ye will revolt more and more: the whole head is sick
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even unto the head there is no soundness in him; wounds
Isaiah1:7 Your country is desolate; your cities are burned with fire; your land
Isaiah1:8 And the daughter of Zion is left
Isaiah1:9 Unless Jehovah of hosts had left us a very small residue
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of Jehovah
Isaiah1:11 To what purpose is the multitude of your sacrifices unto me? saith Jehovah. I am sated with burnt-offerings of rams
Isaiah1:12 When ye come to appear before me
Isaiah1:13 Bring no more vain oblations! Incense is an abomination unto me
Isaiah1:14 Your new moons and your set feasts my soul hateth: they are a burden to me; I am wearied of bearing [them].
Isaiah1:15 And when ye spread forth your hands
Isaiah1:16 Wash you
Isaiah1:17 learn to do well: seek judgment
Isaiah1:18 Come now
Isaiah1:19 If ye be willing and hearken
Isaiah1:20 but if ye refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 How is the faithful city become a harlot! It was full of judgment; righteousness used to lodge in it
Isaiah1:22 Thy silver is become dross
Isaiah1:23 thy princes are rebellious
Isaiah1:24 Therefore saith the Lord
Isaiah1:25 And I will turn my hand upon thee
Isaiah1:26 and I will restore thy judges as at the first
Isaiah1:27 Zion shall be redeemed with judgment
Isaiah1:28 But the ruin of the transgressors and of the sinners [shall be] together; and they that forsake Jehovah shall be consumed.
Isaiah1:29 For they shall be ashamed of the terebinths that ye have desired
Isaiah1:30 For ye shall be as a terebinth whose leaf fadeth
Isaiah1:31 And the strong shall be for tow

Isaiah17:1 The burden of Damascus. Behold
Isaiah17:2 The cities of Aroer are forsaken: they shall be for flocks; and they shall lie down and there shall be none to make them afraid.

Isaiah17:4 And in that day it shall come to pass
Isaiah17:5 And it shall be as when the reaper gathereth the corn
Isaiah17:6 And a gleaning shall be left in it

Isaiah17:8 And he will not look to the altars
Isaiah17:9 In that day shall his strong cities be as the forsaken tract in the woodland

Isaiah17:11 in the day of thy planting wilt thou make [them] to grow

Isaiah17:13 The nations rush as the rushing of many waters; but he will rebuke them

Jeremiah23:11 For both prophet and priest are profane: even in my house have I found their wickedness

Jeremiah51:13 Thou that dwellest upon many waters
Jeremiah51:14 Jehovah of hosts hath sworn by himself
Jeremiah51:15 He hath made the earth by his power
Jeremiah51:16 When he uttereth his voice
Jeremiah51:17 Every man is become brutish
Jeremiah51:18 They are vanity
Jeremiah51:19 The portion of Jacob is not like them; for it is he that hath formed all things: and [Israel] is the rod of his inheritance: Jehovah of hosts is his name.
Jeremiah51:20 Thou art my maul
Jeremiah51:21 and with thee I will break in pieces the horse and his rider; and with thee I will break in pieces the chariot and its driver;
Jeremiah51:22 and with thee will I break in pieces man and woman; and with thee will I break in pieces old and young; and with thee will I break in pieces the young man and the maid;
Jeremiah51:23 and with thee will I break in pieces the shepherd and his flock; and with thee will I break in pieces the husbandman and his yoke of oxen; and with thee will I break in pieces governors and rulers.
Jeremiah51:24 And I will render unto Babylon and to all the inhabitants of Chaldea
Jeremiah51:25 Behold
Jeremiah51:26 And they shall not take of thee a stone for a corner
Jeremiah51:27 Lift up a banner in the land
Jeremiah51:28 Prepare nations against her
Jeremiah51:29 And the land trembleth and is in pain; for the purposes of Jehovah against Babylon do stand
Jeremiah51:30 The mighty men of Babylon have ceased to fight
Jeremiah51:31 Courier runneth to meet courier
Jeremiah51:32 and the passages are seized
Jeremiah51:33 For thus saith Jehovah of hosts
Jeremiah51:34 Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon hath devoured me
Jeremiah51:35 The violence done to me and to my flesh be upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore thus saith Jehovah: Behold
Jeremiah51:37 And Babylon shall become heaps
Jeremiah51:38 They shall roar together like young lions
Jeremiah51:39 When they are heated
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How is Sheshach taken! and how is the praise of the whole earth seized! How is Babylon become an astonishment among the nations!
Jeremiah51:42 The sea is come up upon Babylon: she is covered with the multitude of its waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities are become a desolation
Jeremiah51:44 And I will punish Bel in Babylon
Jeremiah51:45 Go ye out of the midst of her
Jeremiah51:46 lest your heart faint
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore behold
Jeremiah51:48 And the heavens and the earth
Jeremiah51:49 As Babylon caused the slain of Israel to fall
Jeremiah51:50 Ye that have escaped the sword
Jeremiah51:51 We are put to shame
Jeremiah51:52 Therefore behold
Jeremiah51:53 Though Babylon should mount up to the heavens
Jeremiah51:54 The sound of a cry [cometh] from Babylon
Jeremiah51:55 for Jehovah spoileth Babylon
Jeremiah51:56 For the spoiler is come against her
Jeremiah51:57 And I will make drunk her princes
Jeremiah51:58 Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: The broad walls of Babylon shall be utterly laid bare
Jeremiah51:59 The word that Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah the son of Nerijah
Jeremiah51:60 And Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that should come upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:61 And Jeremiah said to Seraiah
Jeremiah51:62 and say
Jeremiah51:63 And it shall be
Jeremiah51:64 and shalt say
Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he began to reign; and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Hamutal
Jeremiah52:2 And he did evil in the sight of Jehovah
Jeremiah52:3 For
Jeremiah52:4 And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 And the city was besieged until the eleventh year of king Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 In the fourth month
Jeremiah52:7 And the city was broken into: and all the men of war fled
Jeremiah52:8 And the army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king
Jeremiah52:9 And they took the king
Jeremiah52:10 And the king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes; and he slaughtered also all the princes of Judah in Riblah.
Jeremiah52:11 And he put out the eyes of Zedekiah
Jeremiah52:12 And in the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 and he burned the house of Jehovah
Jeremiah52:14 And all the army of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah52:15 And Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard carried away captive of the poorest sort of the people
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard left of the poor of the land for vinedressers and for husbandmen.
Jeremiah52:17 And the brazen pillars that were in the house of Jehovah
Jeremiah52:18 The pots also
Jeremiah52:19 And the basons and the censers
Jeremiah52:20 The two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 And as to the pillars: the height of one pillar was eighteen cubits
Jeremiah52:22 And the capital upon it was brass
Jeremiah52:23 And there were ninety-six pomegranates on the [four] sides; all the pomegranates upon the network were a hundred round about.
Jeremiah52:24 And the captain of the body-guard took Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 And out of the city he took a eunuch that was set over the men of war
Jeremiah52:26 And Nebuzar-adan the captain of the body-guard took them
Jeremiah52:27 and the king of Babylon smote them
Jeremiah52:28 This is the people whom Nebuchadrezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year three thousand and twenty-three Jews;
Jeremiah52:29 in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar [he carried away captive] from Jerusalem eight hundred and thirty-two persons;
Jeremiah52:30 in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadrezzar
Jeremiah52:31 And it came to pass in the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 and he spoke kindly unto him
Jeremiah52:33 And he changed his prison garments; and he ate bread before him continually all the days of his life;
Jeremiah52:34 and his allowance was a continual allowance given him by the king of Babylon
Lamentations1:1 How doth the city sit solitary [that] was full of people! She that was great among the nations is become as a widow; the princess among the provinces is become tributary!
Lamentations1:2 She weepeth sore in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah is gone into captivity because of affliction
Lamentations1:4 The ways of Zion do mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her adversaries have become the head
Lamentations1:6 And from the daughter of Zion all her splendour is departed: her princes are become like harts that find no pasture; and they are gone without strength before the pursuer.
Lamentations1:7 In the days of her affliction and of her wanderings
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem hath grievously sinned; therefore is she removed as an impurity: all that honoured her despise her because they have seen her nakedness; and she sigheth
Lamentations1:9 Her impurity was in her skirts
Lamentations1:10 The adversary hath spread out his hand upon all her precious things; for she hath seen the nations enter into her sanctuary
Lamentations1:11 All her people sigh
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 From on high hath he sent fire into my bones
Lamentations1:14 The yoke of my transgressions is bound by his hand: they are wreathed
Lamentations1:15 The Lord hath cast down all my mighty men in the midst of me; he hath called an assembly against me to crush my young men; the Lord hath trodden as a winepress the virgin daughter of Judah.
Lamentations1:16 For these things I weep; mine eye
Lamentations1:17 Zion spreadeth forth her hands; there is none to comfort her; Jehovah hath commanded concerning Jacob
Lamentations1:18 Jehovah is righteous; for I have rebelled against his commandment. Hear
Lamentations1:19 I called for my lovers
Lamentations1:20 See
Lamentations1:21 They have heard that I sigh: I have no comforter: all mine enemies have heard of my calamity; they are glad that thou hast done it. Thou wilt bring the day that thou hast called
Lamentations1:22 Let all their wickedness come before thee; and do unto them
Lamentations2:1 How hath the Lord in his anger covered the daughter of Zion with a cloud! He hath cast down from the heavens unto the earth the beauty of Israel
Lamentations2:2 The Lord hath swallowed up all the dwellings of Jacob
Lamentations2:3 He hath cut off in fierce anger all the horn of Israel: he hath withdrawn his right hand from before the enemy; and he burned up Jacob like a flaming fire
Lamentations2:4 He hath bent his bow like an enemy; he stood with his right hand as an adversary
Lamentations2:5 The Lord is become as an enemy: he hath swallowed up Israel; he hath swallowed up all her palaces; he hath destroyed his strongholds
Lamentations2:6 And he hath violently cast down his enclosure as a garden; he hath destroyed his place of assembly: Jehovah hath caused set feast and sabbath to be forgotten in Zion
Lamentations2:7 The Lord hath cast off his altar
Lamentations2:8 Jehovah hath purposed to destroy the wall of the daughter of Zion: he hath stretched out the line
Lamentations2:9 Her gates are sunk into the ground; he hath destroyed and broken her bars. Her king and her princes are among the nations: the law is no [more]; her prophets also find no vision from Jehovah.
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the daughter of Zion sit upon the ground
Lamentations2:11 Mine eyes are consumed with tears
Lamentations2:12 They say to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 What shall I take to witness for thee? what shall I liken unto thee
Lamentations2:14 Thy prophets have seen vanity and folly for thee; and they have not revealed thine iniquity
Lamentations2:15 All that pass by clap [their] hands at thee; they hiss and shake their head at the daughter of Jerusalem: Is this the city which they called
Lamentations2:16 All thine enemies open their mouth against thee
Lamentations2:17 Jehovah hath done what he had devised; he hath fulfilled his word which he had commanded from the days of old: he hath thrown down
Lamentations2:18 Their heart cried unto the Lord. O wall of the daughter of Zion
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 See
Lamentations2:21 The child and the old man lie on the ground in the streets; my virgins and my young men are fallen by the sword: thou hast slain [them] in the day of thine anger; thou hast killed
Lamentations2:22 Thou hast called up
Lamentations3:1 I am the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.
Lamentations3:2 Me hath he led
Lamentations3:3 Surely against me hath he turned again and again his hand all the day.
Lamentations3:4 My flesh and my skin hath he wasted away
Lamentations3:5 He hath built against me
Lamentations3:6 He hath made me to dwell in dark places as those that have been long dead.
Lamentations3:7 He hath hedged me about that I cannot get out: he hath made my chain heavy.
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry and shout
Lamentations3:9 He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone
Lamentations3:10 He is unto me [as] a bear lying in wait
Lamentations3:11 He hath turned aside my ways
Lamentations3:12 He hath bent his bow
Lamentations3:13 He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.
Lamentations3:14 I am become a derision to all my people; their song all the day.
Lamentations3:15 He hath sated me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones
Lamentations3:17 And thou hast removed my soul far off from peace: I have forgotten prosperity.
Lamentations3:18 And I said
Lamentations3:19 Remember thou mine affliction and my wandering
Lamentations3:20 My soul hath [them] constantly in remembrance
Lamentations3:21 This I recall to heart
Lamentations3:22 It is of Jehovah's loving-kindness we are not consumed
Lamentations3:23 they are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness.
Lamentations3:24 Jehovah is my portion
Lamentations3:25 Jehovah is good unto them that wait for him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that one should both wait
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth:
Lamentations3:28 He sitteth solitary and keepeth silence
Lamentations3:29 he putteth his mouth in the dust
Lamentations3:30 he giveth his cheek to him that smiteth him; he is filled full with reproach.
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not cast off for ever;
Lamentations3:32 but if he have caused grief
Lamentations3:33 for he doth not willingly afflict or grieve the children of men.
Lamentations3:34 To crush under foot all the prisoners of the earth
Lamentations3:35 to turn aside the right of a man before the face of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 to wrong a man in his cause
Lamentations3:37 Who is he that saith
Lamentations3:38 Out of the mouth of the Most High doth not there proceed evil and good?
Lamentations3:39 Wherefore doth a living man complain
Lamentations3:40 Let us search and try our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our heart with [our] hands unto God in the heavens.
Lamentations3:42 We have transgressed and have rebelled: thou hast not pardoned.
Lamentations3:43 Thou hast covered thyself with anger
Lamentations3:44 Thou hast covered thyself with a cloud
Lamentations3:45 Thou hast made us the offscouring and refuse in the midst of the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have opened their mouth against us.
Lamentations3:47 Fear and the pit are come upon us
Lamentations3:48 Mine eye runneth down with streams of water for the ruin of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations3:49 Mine eye poureth down
Lamentations3:50 till Jehovah look down and behold from the heavens.
Lamentations3:51 Mine eye affecteth my soul
Lamentations3:52 They that are mine enemies without cause have chased me sore like a bird.
Lamentations3:53 They have cut off my life in a pit
Lamentations3:54 Waters streamed over my head; I said
Lamentations3:55 I called upon thy name
Lamentations3:56 Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my sighing
Lamentations3:57 Thou drewest near in the day that I called upon thee; thou saidst
Lamentations3:58 Lord
Lamentations3:59 Jehovah
Lamentations3:60 Thou hast seen all their vengeance
Lamentations3:61 Thou hast heard their reproach
Lamentations3:62 the lips of those that rise up against me and their meditation against me all the day.
Lamentations3:63 Behold thou their sitting down and their rising up: I am their song.
Lamentations3:64 Render unto them a recompence
Lamentations3:65 give them obduracy of heart
Lamentations3:66 pursue them in anger
Lamentations4:1 How is the gold become dim! the most pure gold changed! the stones of the sanctuary poured out at the top of all the streets!
Lamentations4:2 The sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even the jackals offer the breast
Lamentations4:4 The tongue of the sucking child cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst; the young children ask bread
Lamentations4:5 They that fed delicately are desolate in the streets; they that were brought up in scarlet embrace dung-hills.
Lamentations4:6 And the punishment of the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the reward of the sin of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her Nazarites were purer than snow
Lamentations4:8 Their visage is darker than blackness
Lamentations4:9 The slain with the sword are happier than the slain with hunger; for these pine away
Lamentations4:10 The hands of pitiful women have boiled their own children: they were their meat in the ruin of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations4:11 Jehovah hath accomplished his fury; he hath poured out his fierce anger
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth
Lamentations4:13 [It is] for the sins of her prophets
Lamentations4:14 They wandered about blind in the streets; they were polluted with blood
Lamentations4:15 They cried unto them
Lamentations4:16 The face of Jehovah hath divided them; he will no more regard them. They respected not the persons of the priests
Lamentations4:17 Our eyes still failed for our vain help; in our watching
Lamentations4:18 They hunted our steps
Lamentations4:19 Our pursuers were swifter than the eagles of the heavens; they chased us hotly upon the mountains
Lamentations4:20 The breath of our nostrils
Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 The punishment of thine iniquity is accomplished
Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance is turned to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We are orphans and fatherless
Lamentations5:4 Our water have we to drink for money
Lamentations5:5 Our pursuers are on our necks: we are weary
Lamentations5:6 We have given the hand to Egypt
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers have sinned
Lamentations5:8 Bondmen rule over us: there is no deliverer out of their hand.
Lamentations5:9 We have to get our bread at the risk of our lives
Lamentations5:10 Our skin gloweth like an oven
Lamentations5:11 They have ravished the women in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes were hanged up by their hand; the faces of elders were not honoured.
Lamentations5:13 The young men have borne the mill
Lamentations5:14 The elders have ceased from the gate
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our heart hath ceased; our dance is turned into mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown is fallen from our head: woe unto us
Lamentations5:17 For this our heart is faint; for these things our eyes have grown dim
Lamentations5:18 Because of the mountain of Zion
Lamentations5:19 Thou
Lamentations5:20 Wherefore dost thou forget us for ever
Lamentations5:21 Turn thou us unto thee
Lamentations5:22 Or is it that thou hast utterly rejected us? Wouldest thou be exceeding wroth against us?
Ezekiel1:1 Now it came to pass in the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 On the fifth of the month
Ezekiel1:3 the word of Jehovah came expressly unto Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 And I looked
Ezekiel1:5 Also out of the midst thereof
Ezekiel1:6 And every one had four faces
Ezekiel1:7 And their feet were straight feet; and the sole of their feet was like the sole of a calf's foot; and they sparkled as the look of burnished brass.
Ezekiel1:8 And they had the hands of a man under their wings on their four sides; and they four had their faces and their wings:
Ezekiel1:9 their wings were joined one to another; they turned not when they went; they went every one straight forward.
Ezekiel1:10 And the likeness of their faces was the face of a man; and they four had the face of a lion on the right side; and they four had the face of an ox on the left side; they four had also the face of an eagle.
Ezekiel1:11 And their faces and their wings were parted above; two [wings] of every one were joined one to another
Ezekiel1:12 And they went every one straight forward: whither the Spirit was to go
Ezekiel1:13 And as for the likeness of the living creatures
Ezekiel1:14 And the living creatures ran and returned as the appearance of a flash of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 And I looked at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 The appearance of the wheels and their work was as the look of a chrysolite; and they four had one likeness; and their appearance and their work was as it were a wheel in the middle of a wheel.
Ezekiel1:17 When they went
Ezekiel1:18 As for their rims
Ezekiel1:19 And when the living creatures went
Ezekiel1:20 Whithersoever the Spirit was to go
Ezekiel1:21 When those went
Ezekiel1:22 And there was the likeness of an expanse over the heads of the living creature
Ezekiel1:23 And under the expanse were their wings straight
Ezekiel1:24 And when they went
Ezekiel1:25 and there was a voice from above the expanse that was over their heads. When they stood
Ezekiel1:26 And above the expanse that was over their heads was the likeness of a throne
Ezekiel1:27 And I saw as the look of glowing brass
Ezekiel1:28 As the appearance of the bow that is in the cloud in the day of rain

Ezekiel25:16 therefore thus saith the Lord Jehovah: Behold

Ezekiel26:1 And it came to pass in the eleventh year

Ezekiel26:6 And her daughters that are in the field shall be slain by the sword; and they shall know that I [am] Jehovah.

Ezekiel26:8 He shall slay with the sword thy daughters in the field

Ezekiel26:12 And they shall make a spoil of thy riches
Ezekiel26:13 And I will cause the noise of thy songs to cease; and the sound of thy harps shall be no more heard.

Ezekiel27:1 And the word of Jehovah came unto me
Ezekiel27:2 And thou
Ezekiel27:3 and say unto Tyre: O thou that art situate at the entries of the sea
Ezekiel27:4 Thy borders are in the heart of the seas
Ezekiel27:5 They made all thy double boards of cypress-trees of Senir; they took cedars from Lebanon to make masts for thee.
Ezekiel27:6 Of the oaks of Bashan did they make thine oars; they made thy benches of ivory
Ezekiel27:7 Byssus with broidered work from Egypt was thy sail
Ezekiel27:8 The inhabitants of Zidon and Arvad were thy rowers; thy wise men
Ezekiel27:9 The elders of Gebal and the wise men thereof were in thee repairing thy leaks; all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in thee
Ezekiel27:10 Persia and Lud and Phut were in thine army
Ezekiel27:11 The children of Arvad with thine army were upon thy walls round about
Ezekiel27:12 Tarshish dealt with thee by reason of the abundance of all substance; with silver
Ezekiel27:13 Javan
Ezekiel27:14 They of the house of Togarmah furnished thy markets with horses
Ezekiel27:15 The children of Dedan were thy traffickers; many isles were the mart of thy hand: they rendered in payment horns of ivory
Ezekiel27:16 Syria dealt with thee for the multitude of thy handiworks: they traded in thy markets with carbuncles
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel were thy traffickers: they bartered with thee wheat of Minnith
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus dealt with thee because of the multitude of thy handiworks
Ezekiel27:19 Vedan and Javan of Uzal traded in thy markets: wrought iron
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan was thy trafficker in precious riding-cloths.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were the merchants of thy hand: in lambs
Ezekiel27:22 The merchants of Sheba and Raamah were thy traffickers: they furnished thy markets with all the choice spices
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 these traded with thee in sumptuous clothes
Ezekiel27:25 The ships of Tarshish were thy caravans for thy traffic; and thou wast replenished
Ezekiel27:26 Thy rowers have brought thee into great waters; the east wind hath broken thee in the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:27 Thy substance
Ezekiel27:28 The open places shall shake at the sound of the cry of thy pilots.
Ezekiel27:29 And all that handle the oar
Ezekiel27:30 and shall cause their voice to be heard over thee
Ezekiel27:31 And they shall make themselves utterly bald for thee
Ezekiel27:32 And in their wailing they shall take up a lamentation for thee
Ezekiel27:33 When thy wares went forth over the seas
Ezekiel27:34 In the time [when] thou art broken by the seas
Ezekiel27:35 All the inhabitants of the isles are amazed at thee
Ezekiel27:36 The merchants among the peoples hiss at thee; thou art become a terror

Ezekiel40:1 In the twenty-fifth year of our captivity
Ezekiel40:2 In the visions of God brought he me into the land of Israel
Ezekiel40:3 And he brought me thither
Ezekiel40:4 And the man said unto me
Ezekiel40:5 And behold
Ezekiel40:6 And he came to the gate which looked toward the east
Ezekiel40:7 And [each] chamber was one reed long and one reed broad; and between the chambers were five cubits; and the threshold of the gate
Ezekiel40:8 And he measured the porch of the gate within
Ezekiel40:9 And he measured the porch of the gate
Ezekiel40:10 And the chambers of the gate which was toward the east were three on this side and three on that side: they three were of one measure; and the posts on this side and on that side had one measure.
Ezekiel40:11 And he measured the breadth of the entry of the gate
Ezekiel40:12 And there was a border before the chambers of one cubit
Ezekiel40:13 And he measured the gate from the roof of [one] chamber to the roof [of the other]
Ezekiel40:14 And he made posts
Ezekiel40:15 And from the front of the gate of the entrance unto the front of the porch of the inner gate were fifty cubits.
Ezekiel40:16 And there were closed windows to the chambers
Ezekiel40:17 And he brought me into the outer court
Ezekiel40:18 And the pavement was by the side of the gates
Ezekiel40:19 And he measured the breadth from the front of the lower gate unto the front of the inner court outside
Ezekiel40:20 And the gate of the outer court
Ezekiel40:21 And its chambers were three on this side and three on that side; and its posts and its projections were according to the measure of the first gate: its length was fifty cubits
Ezekiel40:22 And its windows
Ezekiel40:23 And the gate of the inner court was opposite to the gate toward the north

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah came Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon unto Jerusalem
Daniel1:2 And the Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 And the king spoke unto Ashpenaz the chief of his eunuchs
Daniel1:4 youths in whom was no blemish
Daniel1:5 And the king appointed unto them a daily provision of the king's delicate food
Daniel1:6 Now among these were of the children of Judah
Daniel1:7 And the prince of the eunuchs gave them names: to Daniel he gave [the name] Belteshazzar
Daniel1:8 And Daniel purposed in his heart that he would not pollute himself with the king's delicate food
Daniel1:9 And God granted Daniel favour and mercy before the prince of the eunuchs.
Daniel1:10 And the prince of the eunuchs said unto Daniel
Daniel1:11 And Daniel said to the steward
Daniel1:12 Prove thy servants
Daniel1:13 then let our countenances be looked upon before thee
Daniel1:14 And he hearkened unto them in this matter
Daniel1:15 And at the end of ten days their countenances appeared fairer and were fatter in flesh than all the youths that ate of the king's delicate food.
Daniel1:16 So the steward took away their delicate food
Daniel1:17 As for these four youths
Daniel1:18 And at the end of the days that the king had said he should bring them in
Daniel1:19 And the king spoke with them; and among them all was found none like Daniel
Daniel1:20 And in all matters of judicious wisdom
Daniel1:21 And Daniel continued unto the first year of king Cyrus.
Daniel2:1 And in the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar
Daniel2:2 And the king commanded to call the scribes
Daniel2:3 And the king said unto them
Daniel2:4 And the Chaldeans spoke to the king in Aramaic
Daniel2:5 The king answered and said to the Chaldeans
Daniel2:6 But if ye shew the dream and its interpretation
Daniel2:7 They answered the second time and said
Daniel2:8 The king answered and said
Daniel2:9 but if ye do not make known unto me the dream
Daniel2:10 The Chaldeans answered before the king and said
Daniel2:11 For the thing that the king demandeth is extraordinary
Daniel2:12 For this cause the king was irritated and very wroth
Daniel2:13 And the decree went forth that the wise men were to be slain; and they sought Daniel and his companions to slay them.
Daniel2:14 Then Daniel answered with counsel and prudence to Arioch the chief of the king's bodyguard
Daniel2:15 he answered and said to Arioch the king's captain
Daniel2:16 And Daniel went in
Daniel2:17 Then Daniel went to his house
Daniel2:18 that they would desire mercies of the God of the heavens concerning this secret; that Daniel and his companions should not perish with the rest of the wise men of Babylon.
Daniel2:19 Then was the secret revealed unto Daniel in a night vision. Then Daniel blessed the God of the heavens.
Daniel2:20 Daniel answered and said
Daniel2:21 And it is he that changeth times and seasons; He deposeth kings
Daniel2:22 It is he that revealeth the deep and secret things; He knoweth what is in the darkness
Daniel2:23 I thank thee
Daniel2:24 Therefore Daniel went in unto Arioch
Daniel2:25 Then Arioch brought in Daniel before the king in haste
Daniel2:26 The king answered and said unto Daniel
Daniel2:27 Daniel answered in the presence of the king and said
Daniel2:28 but there is a God in the heavens
Daniel2:29 as for thee
Daniel2:30 And as for me
Daniel2:31 Thou
Daniel2:32 This image's head was of fine gold
Daniel2:33 its legs of iron
Daniel2:34 Thou sawest till a stone was cut out without hands; and it smote the image upon its feet of iron and clay
Daniel2:35 Then was the iron
Daniel2:36 This is the dream; and we will tell the interpretation of it before the king.
Daniel2:37 Thou
Daniel2:38 and wheresoever the children of men
Daniel2:39 And after thee shall arise another kingdom inferior to thee; then another third kingdom of brass
Daniel2:40 And the fourth kingdom shall be strong as iron: forasmuch as iron breaketh in pieces and subdueth everything
Daniel2:41 And whereas thou sawest the feet and toes
Daniel2:42 And [as] the toes of the feet were part of iron and part of clay
Daniel2:43 And whereas thou sawest the iron mixed with miry clay
Daniel2:44 And in the days of these kings shall the God of the heavens set up a kingdom which shall never be destroyed; and the sovereignty thereof shall not be left to another people: it shall break in pieces and consume all these kingdoms
Daniel2:45 Forasmuch as thou sawest that a stone was cut out of the mountain without hands
Daniel2:46 Then king Nebuchadnezzar fell on his face and worshipped Daniel
Daniel2:47 The king answered Daniel and said
Daniel2:48 Then the king made Daniel great
Daniel2:49 And Daniel requested of the king

Daniel6:13 Then they answered and said before the king
Daniel6:14 Then the king
Daniel6:15 Then these men came in a body unto the king
Daniel6:16 Then the king commanded
Daniel6:17 And a stone was brought
Daniel6:18 Then the king went to his palace
Daniel6:19 Then the king arose with the light at break of day
Daniel6:20 And when he came near unto the den
Daniel6:21 Then Daniel spoke unto the king
Daniel6:22 My God hath sent his angel
Daniel6:23 Thereupon was the king exceeding glad
Daniel6:24 And the king commanded
Daniel6:25 Then king Darius wrote unto all peoples
Daniel6:26 I make a decree
Daniel6:27 He saveth and delivereth
Daniel6:28 And this Daniel prospered in the reign of Darius
Daniel7:1 In the first year of Belshazzar king of Babylon
Daniel7:2 Daniel spoke and said
Daniel7:3 And four great beasts came up from the sea
Daniel7:4 The first was like a lion
Daniel7:5 And behold
Daniel7:6 After this I saw
Daniel7:7 After this I saw in the night visions
Daniel7:8 I considered the horns
Daniel7:9 I beheld till thrones were set
Daniel7:10 A stream of fire issued and came forth from before him; thousand thousands ministered unto him
Daniel7:11 I beheld therefore
Daniel7:12 As for the rest of the beasts
Daniel7:13 I saw in the night visions
Daniel7:14 And there was given him dominion
Daniel7:15 As for me Daniel
Daniel7:16 I came near unto one of them that stood by
Daniel7:17 These great beasts
Daniel7:18 But the saints of the most high [places] shall receive the kingdom
Daniel7:19 Then I desired to know the certainty concerning the fourth beast
Daniel7:20 and concerning the ten horns that were in its head
Daniel7:21 I beheld
Daniel7:22 until the Ancient of days came
Daniel7:23 He said thus: The fourth beast shall be a fourth kingdom upon the earth
Daniel7:24 And as to the ten horns
Daniel7:25 And he shall speak words against the Most High
Daniel7:26 And the judgment shall sit
Daniel7:27 But the kingdom and the dominion
Daniel7:28 So far is the end of the matter. As for me Daniel
Daniel8:1 In the third year of the reign of Belshazzar the king
Daniel8:2 And I saw in the vision; and it came to pass
Daniel8:3 And I lifted up mine eyes and saw
Daniel8:4 I saw the ram pushing westward
Daniel8:5 And as I was considering
Daniel8:6 And he came to the ram that had the two horns
Daniel8:7 And I saw him come close to the ram
Daniel8:8 And the he-goat became exceeding great; but when he was become strong
Daniel8:9 And out of one of them came forth a little horn
Daniel8:10 And it became great
Daniel8:11 (And he magnified [himself] even to the prince of the host
Daniel8:12 And a time of trial was appointed unto the continual [sacrifice] by reason of transgression.) And it cast down the truth to the ground; and it practised and prospered.
Daniel8:13 And I heard one saint speaking
Daniel8:14 And he said unto me
Daniel8:15 And it came to pass
Daniel8:16 And I heard a man's voice between [the banks of] the Ulai; and he called and said
Daniel8:17 And he came near where I stood; and when he came
Daniel8:18 Now
Daniel8:19 And he said
Daniel8:20 The ram that thou sawest having the two horns: they are the kings of Media and Persia.
Daniel8:21 And the rough goat is the king of Greece; and the great horn that was between his eyes is the first king.
Daniel8:22 Now that being broken
Daniel8:23 And at the latter time of their kingdom
Daniel8:24 And his power shall be mighty
Daniel8:25 And through his cunning shall he cause craft to prosper in his hand; and he will magnify [himself] in his heart
Daniel8:26 And the vision of the evening and the morning which hath been told is true; but close thou up the vision
Daniel8:27 And I Daniel fainted
Daniel9:1 In the first year of Darius the son of Ahasuerus
Daniel9:2 in the first year of his reign
Daniel9:3 And I set my face unto the Lord God
Daniel9:4 and I prayed unto Jehovah my God

Daniel9:6 And we have not hearkened unto thy servants the prophets

Daniel9:8 O Lord

Daniel9:10 and we have not hearkened unto the voice of Jehovah our God
Daniel9:11 And all Israel have transgressed thy law
Daniel9:12 And he hath performed his words
Daniel9:13 As it is written in the law of Moses
Daniel9:14 And Jehovah hath watched over the evil
Daniel9:15 And now
Daniel9:16 Lord
Daniel9:17 And now
Daniel9:18 Incline thine ear
Daniel9:19 Lord
Daniel9:20 And whilst I was speaking
Daniel9:21 whilst I was yet speaking in prayer
Daniel9:22 And he informed [me]
Daniel9:23 At the beginning of thy supplications the word went forth
Daniel9:24 Seventy weeks are apportioned out upon thy people and upon thy holy city

Daniel9:26 And after the sixty-two weeks shall Messiah be cut off
Daniel9:27 And he shall confirm a covenant with the many [for] one week; and in the midst of the week he shall cause the sacrifice and the oblation to cease
Daniel10:1 In the third year of Cyrus king of Persia a thing was revealed unto Daniel
Daniel10:2 In those days I Daniel was mourning three full weeks:
Daniel10:3 I ate no pleasant bread
Daniel10:4 And on the four-and-twentieth day of the first month
Daniel10:5 and I lifted up mine eyes and looked
Daniel10:6 and his body was like a chrysolite
Daniel10:7 And I Daniel alone saw the vision; and the men that were with me saw not the vision
Daniel10:8 And I was left alone
Daniel10:9 And I heard the voice of his words; and when I heard the voice of his words
Daniel10:10 And behold
Daniel10:11 And he said unto me
Daniel10:12 And he said unto me
Daniel10:13 But the prince of the kingdom of Persia withstood me one and twenty days; and behold
Daniel10:14 And I am come to make thee understand what shall befall thy people at the end of the days: for the vision is yet for [many] days.
Daniel10:15 And as he was speaking with me according to these words
Daniel10:16 And behold
Daniel10:17 And how can the servant of this my lord talk with this my lord? for as for me
Daniel10:18 Then there touched me again one like the appearance of a man
Daniel10:19 and he said
Daniel10:20 And he said

Daniel11:1 And I
Daniel11:2 And now will I declare unto thee the truth. Behold
Daniel11:3 And a mighty king shall stand up that shall rule with great dominion
Daniel11:4 And when he shall stand up
Daniel11:5 And the king of the south
Daniel11:6 And after the course of years they shall join affinity; and the king's daughter of the south shall come to the king of the north to make equitable conditions: but she shall not retain the strength of her arm; neither shall he stand
Daniel11:7 But out of a shoot from her roots shall one stand up in his place
Daniel11:8 He shall also carry captive into Egypt their gods
Daniel11:9 and [the same] shall come into the realm of the king of the south
Daniel11:10 And his sons shall be stirred up
Daniel11:11 And the king of the south shall be enraged
Daniel11:12 And when the multitude shall have been taken away
Daniel11:13 For the king of the north shall return
Daniel11:14 And in those times shall many stand up against the king of the south; and the violent of thy people will exalt themselves to establish the vision; but they shall fall.
Daniel11:15 And the king of the north shall come
Daniel11:16 And he that cometh against him shall do according to his own will
Daniel11:17 And he shall set his face to come with the strength of his whole kingdom
Daniel11:18 And he shall turn his face unto the isles
Daniel11:19 And he shall turn his face toward the fortresses of his own land; and he shall stumble and fall
Daniel11:20 And in his place shall one stand up who shall cause the exactor to pass through the glory of the kingdom; but in a few days he shall be broken
Daniel11:21 And in his place shall stand up a vile person
Daniel11:22 And the overflowing forces shall be overflowed from before him
Daniel11:23 And after the league made with him he shall work deceitfully
Daniel11:24 In time of peace shall he enter even into the fattest places of the province; and he shall do that which his fathers have not done
Daniel11:25 And he shall stir up his power and his courage against the king of the south with a great army; and the king of the south shall engage in battle with an exceeding great and mighty army; but he shall not stand
Daniel11:26 And they that eat of his delicate food shall break him
Daniel11:27 And both these kings' hearts [shall meditate] mischief
Daniel11:28 And he shall return into his land with great substance; and his heart shall be against the holy covenant; and he shall practise
Daniel11:29 At the set time he shall return
Daniel11:30 for ships of Chittim shall come against him; and he shall be grieved
Daniel11:31 And forces shall stand on his part
Daniel11:32 And such as do wickedly against the covenant shall he pervert by flatteries; but the people that know their God shall be strong
Daniel11:33 And they that are wise among the people shall instruct the many; and they shall fall by the sword
Daniel11:34 And when they fall
Daniel11:35 And [some] of the wise shall fall
Daniel11:36 And the king shall do according to his will; and he shall exalt himself
Daniel11:37 And he will not regard the God of his fathers
Daniel11:38 And in his place will he honour the God of fortresses; and a God whom his fathers knew not will he honour with gold and silver
Daniel11:39 And he will practise in the strongholds of fortresses with a strange God: whoso acknowledgeth him will he increase with glory; and he shall cause them to rule over the many
Daniel11:40 And at the time of the end shall the king of the south push at him; and the king of the north shall come against him like a whirlwind
Daniel11:41 And he shall enter into the land of beauty
Daniel11:42 And he shall stretch forth his hand upon the countries; and the land of Egypt shall not escape.
Daniel11:43 And he shall have power over the treasures of gold and of silver
Daniel11:44 But tidings out of the east and out of the north shall trouble him; and he shall go forth with great fury to exterminate
Daniel11:45 And he shall plant the tents of his palace between the sea and the mountain of holy beauty; and he shall come to his end
Daniel12:1 And at that time shall Michael stand up
Daniel12:2 And many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake
Daniel12:3 And they that are wise shall shine as the brightness of the expanse; and they that turn the many to righteousness as the stars
Daniel12:4 And thou
Daniel12:5 And I Daniel looked
Daniel12:6 And he said to the man clothed in linen
Daniel12:7 And I heard the man clothed in linen
Daniel12:8 And I heard
Daniel12:9 And he said
Daniel12:10 Many shall be purified
Daniel12:11 And from the time that the continual [sacrifice] is taken away
Daniel12:12 Blessed is he that waiteth
Daniel12:13 But do thou go thy way until the end; and thou shalt rest

Hosea1:4 And Jehovah said unto him
Hosea1:5 And it shall come to pass in that day
Hosea1:6 And she conceived again
Hosea1:7 But I will have mercy upon the house of Judah
Hosea1:8 And she weaned Lo-ruhamah; and she conceived and bore a son;
Hosea1:9 and he said
Hosea1:10 Yet the number of the children of Israel shall be as the sand of the sea which cannot be measured or numbered; and it shall come to pass
Hosea1:11 And the children of Judah and the children of Israel shall be gathered together
Hosea2:1 Say unto your brethren Ammi; and to your sisters
Hosea2:2 Plead with your mother
Hosea2:3 lest I strip her naked
Hosea2:4 And I will not have mercy upon her children; for they are the children of whoredoms.
Hosea2:5 For their mother hath played the harlot; she that conceived them hath done shamefully: for she said
Hosea2:6 Therefore behold
Hosea2:7 And she shall pursue after her lovers
Hosea2:8 And she did not know that I had given her the corn and the new wine and the oil
Hosea2:9 Therefore will I return
Hosea2:10 And now will I discover her impiety in the sight of her lovers
Hosea2:11 And I will cause all her mirth to cease: her feasts
Hosea2:12 And I will make desolate her vine and her fig-tree
Hosea2:13 And I will visit upon her the days of the Baals
Hosea2:14 Therefore behold
Hosea2:15 And I will give her her vineyards from thence
Hosea2:16 And it shall be in that day
Hosea2:17 for I will take away the names of the Baals out of her mouth
Hosea2:18 And I will make a covenant for them in that day with the beasts of the field
Hosea2:19 And I will betroth thee unto me for ever; and I will betroth thee unto me in righteousness
Hosea2:20 and I will betroth thee unto me in faithfulness: and thou shalt know Jehovah.
Hosea2:21 And it shall come to pass in that day
Hosea2:22 and the earth shall hear the corn
Hosea2:23 And I will sow her unto me in the land; and I will have mercy upon Lo-ruhamah; and I will say to Lo-ammi

Hosea5:1 Hear this
Hosea5:2 And they have plunged themselves in the corruption of apostasy
Hosea5:3 I know Ephraim
Hosea5:4 Their doings do not allow them to return unto their God; for the spirit of whoredoms is in the midst of them
Hosea5:5 And Israel's pride doth testify to his face; and Israel and Ephraim shall fall by their iniquity: Judah also shall fall with them.
Hosea5:6 They shall go with their flocks and with their herds to seek Jehovah; but they shall not find [him]: he hath withdrawn himself from them.
Hosea5:7 They have dealt treacherously against Jehovah; for they have begotten strange children: now shall the new moon devour them
Hosea5:8 Blow the horn in Gibeah
Hosea5:9 Ephraim shall be a desolation in the day of rebuke: among the tribes of Israel have I made known that which is sure.
Hosea5:10 The princes of Judah are become like them that remove the landmark: I will pour out my wrath upon them like water.
Hosea5:11 Ephraim is oppressed

Hosea5:14 For I will be unto Ephraim as a lion

Hosea6:3 and we shall know
Hosea6:4 What shall I do unto thee

Hosea6:7 But they like Adam have transgressed the covenant: there have they dealt treacherously against me.

Hosea6:10 In the house of Israel have I seen a horrible thing: the whoredom of Ephraim is there; Israel is defiled.

Hosea7:4 They all practise adultery
Hosea7:5 In the day of our king
Hosea7:6 For they have applied their heart like an oven to their lying in wait: their baker sleepeth all the night; in the morning it burneth like a flaming fire.

Hosea7:9 Strangers have devoured his strength

Joel1:7 He hath made my vine a desolation

Joel1:20 The beasts of the field also cry unto thee; for the water-courses are dried

Joel2:4 The appearance of them is as the appearance of horses; and as horsemen
Joel2:5 Like the noise of chariots
Joel2:6 Before them the peoples are in anguish: all faces turn pale.
Joel2:7 They run like mighty men; they climb the wall like men of war; and they march every one on his ways
Joel2:8 Neither doth one press upon another; they march every one in his path; and fall amid weapons
Joel2:9 They spread themselves over the city; they run upon the wall; they climb up into the houses; they enter in by the windows like a thief.

Joel2:11 And Jehovah uttereth his voice before his army; for his camp is very great; for strong is he that executeth his word: for the day of Jehovah is great and very terrible; and who can bear it?

Amos3:1 Hear this word that Jehovah hath spoken against you
Amos3:2 You only have I known of all the families of the earth; therefore will I visit upon you all your iniquities.

Amos3:4 Will a lion roar in the forest when he hath no prey? Will a young lion cry out of his den if he have taken nothing?
Amos3:5 Can a bird fall in a snare upon the earth when no gin [is laid] for him? Will the snare spring up from the earth when nothing at all hath been taken?
Amos3:6 Shall a trumpet be blown in the city
Amos3:7 But the Lord Jehovah will do nothing

Habakkuk1:5 See ye among the nations

Habakkuk1:9 They come all of them for violence: the crowd of their faces is forwards

Habakkuk1:11 Then will his mind change
Habakkuk1:12 Art thou not from everlasting

2Thessalonians1:1 Paul and Silvanus and Timotheus to the assembly of Thessalonians in God our Father and [the] Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians1:2 Grace to you

2Thessalonians1:4 so that we ourselves make our boast in you in the assemblies of God for your endurance and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations
2Thessalonians1:5 a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God

2Thessalonians1:8 in flaming fire taking vengeance on those who know not God
2Thessalonians1:9 who shall pay the penalty [of] everlasting destruction from [the] presence of the Lord

2Thessalonians1:11 To which end we also pray always for you
2Thessalonians1:12 so that the name of our Lord Jesus [Christ] may be glorified in you and *ye* in him

2Thessalonians2:2 that ye be not soon shaken in mind
2Thessalonians2:3 Let not any one deceive you in any manner

2Thessalonians2:9 whose coming is according to the working of Satan in all power and signs and wonders of falsehood

2Thessalonians2:11 And for this reason God sends to them a working of error
2Thessalonians2:12 that all might be judged who have not believed the truth

1Timothy1:6 which [things] some having missed

1Timothy1:18 This charge

1Timothy2:10 but

1Timothy2:13 for Adam was formed first
1Timothy2:14 and Adam was not deceived; but the woman
1Timothy2:15 But she shall be preserved in childbearing
